date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2025
author: Johnny P
title: Cuckoo 6qt Rice Cooker
body: I haven't had a need for a rice cooker, mainly cooking my rice on the stove top.After many long work nights and busy days with kids, and many youtube videos on one pot meals, I bought this rice cooker. Just a simple, no fail cook/warm button. None of those with a million cook options :).(It's important to know that these rice cooker cups that comes with the rice cooker are NOT your standard 1 cup size, it's more like 3/4 of a regular cup size).I was pleasantly surprised at how quickly it cooked my rice, especially my sweet glutonous rice where normally you would have to soak for 4-6 hrs to overnight. I was extremely impressed at how quickly and cooked thru it was in such a short amount of time. I have tried making hainanese chicken and it turned out perfectly. My new favorite go to! I've also tried various rice and chicken one pot meals, even steaming sweet potatos and it comes out perfect everytime! I love that the design is not bulky, durable, and also love the color. It's a keeper for me!

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2025
author: john a.
title: Translation issue, capacity incorrect
body: Edit: I have received the rice cooker and have been using it for about a month on a weekly basis. It's great! It's Korea's number one brand for a reason, and unlike Zojirushi, Cuckoo actually makes affordable models. You won't regret this purchase, just keep in mind that you will need to learn the ratios. I have included an explaination that I believe will have you cooking perfect rice from your first try. I is not a 6/12 imperial cup capacity cooker. There is an Asian industry standard 180ml (.76 cups) cup which comes with the cooker. By my math, the rice cooker is 4 cups dry, 8 cooked, measuring with US cups. I'm still very satisfied, I just wanted to fight false advertising, which in this case I think is just a translation issue. If you use the provided cup, you'll be fine. If you lose it just remember when the instructions say cup they mean 3/4 cup or 180ml, then fill water to the line inside which corresponds to the number of (180ml) cups you use.

author: Volcanic Truth
title: Incredible product
body: I ordered this few weeks back. Bought after reading other reviews. The first one was faulty. I think the other one was packaged wrong?? when I opened from the box from where label was placed, rice cooker was placed upside down. When I plugged it in, it was straight to on cook, instead of being on warm. I flipped the switch and it was hard but I thought maybe this is how it works. So I put some rice to cook and flipped the button on cook. Was taking forever to heat up and cook. After sometime smelled like burning?? then sometime later, I noticed the the power signal was off. Looked in it, rice was cooked. Thought that??s okay but takes the same time as my old cooker. When I ate the rice, it tasted funny?? later on the rice on the bottom part was all burned. Next day I woke up with sore throat. A day later tried to cook, no light at all. It was dead. But they sent me a replacement and it works as other reviewers said. Beautiful to look at and fast to cook. Built quality was superb. Will buy again.

author: William E Hoeft
title: Better than expected
body: My wife and I really like this rice cooker. It really does a nice job and is very easy to use. Add the right amount of water, add the right amount of rice, close the lid, and activate the button on the front. It's the only control on the system, and it only has the one position to move it to. Can't get any easier than that. It has done a very good job each time we use it. The only negative about it is that the measuring cup that was included has very faint markings that are very hard to see. Not too big of a problem for use because we always use the full amount of rice, but if you wanted to make a smaller portion, you will have to look very closely. If they could update the measuring cup, then this unit would be perfect.

author: A.B.
title: It??s okay
body: I have an original Cuckoo basic rice cooker that has lasted 13 years. It still works but the thermostat shuts it off after 5 minutes. So I decided to buy a new one. This is similar but with a few changes I??m not thrilled with. First, the cord is removable, push it in hard or it won??t start up. The cord is in a stupid location in relation to the front where the buttons are. The vent is 3 slits and too near the cord for my liking. The inner pot is much thinner and definitely cheap Teflon coating compared to my old one. My old inner pot is still non stick. Let??s see how long this one lasts. Personally, like they say, if it ain??t broke don??t fix it. Original design is better. Btw, I use one cup of rice and 2 cups of water. When it??s done. Let it sit 5-10 minutes and your rice will be fluffy. You can also add a tsp of butter or margarine prior to cooking and a bit of salt for flavor

author: Kaya Myst
title: Really nice!
body: I first bought this as a mother's day gift for my mother because every single rice cooker she ever buys either gets something broken and/or lost. I figured this type of cooker was great for her as the lid is attached and really you only need to rinse or wipe these clean instead of washing it all out. Cheap enough as well, though there were quite a few negative reviews..Unfortunately she didn't want it because of "all the cookers" she has.. yeah she is one of those.. anyways, I kept it because I do like to eat a lot of rice and did always want to try one of these.. It works pretty nice so far and I am pretty happy with it! I enjoy how I don't need to watch my rice to cook it, now and it turns perfect each time~I would totally recommend this item to anyone that wants an affordable Asian rice cooker but can't afford to invest in the more fancy models. Just please remember to unplug it when you are not using it because there really is no off button and that honestly is my only gripe, but I can't keep a lot plugged in anyways so I'm fine with it like this for now.

author: Sandy B.
title: MADE IN CHINA---not Korean quality. Very misleading. YOU WANT KOREAN QUALITY---BELIEVE ME!
body: Looks to me that Cuckoo is playing the "slight of hand" in both the packaging and 'LACK" of manufacturing info posted on Amazon. Notice in pics I've posted....THIS COOKER IS MADE IN CHINA....which is exactly opposite I was told by Amazon.The branding and packaging SHOULD be easier to determine "country of manufacture." And both Amazon staffer I ordered through....and a posted question stated this unit "MADE IN KOREA!"****NOT*****Made in China.Cheap little cooker---no "Off switch"....plugged in it is on warm. Couldn't cost but a few cents to include off switch. But typical cost cutting apparent in most Chinese products....and anyone can buy much cheaper cookers with these features on Amazon for $15-$20. I would pay $50 for Korean made cooker...but not Chinese made.Not happy....Will be sent back....due to misleading info....the fact it is virtually hidden in amazon post....and the quality of Chinese made Cuckoo's are inferior. I've owned one before. Lasted half year.Amazon SHOULD make sellers post country of manufacture.I'll try one time more to buy a KOREAN Cuckoo....then I'll simply quit wasting time and money buying on Amazon....and simply buy local---where "country of manufacture" in easily seen.C'mon Amazon---treat customers like we actually matter!Without us----you cease to exist.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 31, 2024
author: jon
title: Does the Job Very Well
body: I love to cook. I have restaurant experience from my youth, and I'm usually in the kitchen six nights a week these days. Even when I travel, I rent a house instead of booking a hotel room so I'll have a kitchen. The one thing that's always given me trouble is rice. Maybe a third of the time, it comes out okay, but the other two thirds, it's either crunchy or too wet and soggy. I've tried less heat, more heat, less water, more water, and every trick I could find online to correct the problem. The exact method that worked great last time results in crunchy rice this time.Enter this rice cooker. I followed the instructions, went about cooking the rest of our dinner, and when it was time to plate the meal, the rice was waiting inside, cooked to perfection. It could not be easier, and it doesn't take very long at all. Cleanup is easier than expected. I thought there'd be more parts inside, but it doesn't really add any more time to washing dishes. Honestly, my only gripe is that I didn't get one of these sooner. Even if you don't have issues cooking rice, just freeing up the rest of the stove is super convenient. I highly recommend this to anyone thinking about getting a rice cooker.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 22, 2024
author: Jenn M
title: Good rice cooker
body: This is a good rice cooker & steamer. Normally, I don't like to get single use appliances, meaning appliances that really have only one purpose. Rice cookers being the most common. I prefer having multi-use appliances, so that they don't just sit on a shelf in my kitchen or pantry. However, I really like the large capacity of those rice cooker. I also like the easy operation (measure out the rice, water, push the button, & let it sit). I like how easy everything is to clean as well, as all of the pieces either come out, or they come apart easily, & they can be hand washed or thrown in to the dishwasher. I also like that this can be used as a steamer for things like tamales or pot stickers (though it's not technically recommended, unless you deodorize the cooker before cooking rice in it). It is on the pricier side of most of the rice cookers that I have seen, but I still feel that it is a good value. I also like that the user manual gives you instructions on how to cook the rice so it will come out sticky (chewy) or softer. Overall, this was a good choice for me. Although, it does take up quite a bit of counter space, so this is not something I would recommend for anyone who is looking for something that is going to be a space saver.

title: Great rice cooker
body: This rice cooker is a beast! It's huge and has so many features, but it definitely takes some getting used to. I've had it for a few weeks now and I'm still figuring things out.The good news is that once you get the hang of it, this thing is amazing. The rice comes out perfect every time, and I love that I can make a giant batch and have leftovers for days. The non-stick coating is great and cleanup is easy.I've also started using it to steam vegetables and cook proteins, which is really convenient.Overall, I'm really happy with this purchase. It's a bit of an investment, but it's worth it if you cook a lot of rice and want a machine with a lot of extra features. Just be patient with yourself as you learn how to use it!

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 29, 2024
author: Amazon Customer
title: Cooks rice very quickly
body: I am amazed at how fast and well this cooks rice compared to my old rice cooker. At first, I was a bit put off because this has 10 cup capacity but takes up much more shelf space than the old one I have that does the same volume. This cuckoo brand cooker is easier to use and clean with levers instead of buttons. It comes with a steam basket, too. The water cleaner at the back is easy to remove and clear to see how much is in there so it doesn't get skipped in cleaning after use. The rice cooked in less than 20 minutes and switched itself to warm. It was perfect jasmine rice. My only complaint is that it doesn't beep loudly when done. It might be good if you're making lunch rice before breakfast and don't want to wake the house. Worth the price it is listed.

I love how portable this rice cooker is it's easy to just load it up in the truck and take it with us on the road to make rice once we reach our destination for holidays with the extended family. All the utensils that come with this rice cooker are fabulous and the directions in the booklet are super easy to follow.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2024
author: Michael
title: Missing A Key Feature
body: We??ve been using the Cuckoo CR-1095 10 Cups Basic Electric Rice Cooker and Warmer for a couple of weeks now and as much as we??re impressed with how well it cooks rice, it??s missing a key feature for which I feel compelled to deduct a star from the rating.  The directions explain that when the sensor detects the rice is cooked it will automatically switch to warming mode and to wait 15 minutes before opening the cooker.  Well, there??s no buzzer or alarm to alert the chef that the rice has finished cooking or warming.  So if you aren??t right next to the cooker when it switches to warm mode there is no way to know when the 15 minute countdown began.If the goal of the design was simplicity, they missed the mark by not providing an audible signal for when the rice is ready.  That said, if you pay close attention and notice the cooker has switched from cook to warm, wait the prescribed 15 minutes while it finishes cooking on warm mode this makes perfect rice every time.  We did find that we prefer to add a little extra water to get a more sticky consistency with the jasmine rice we enjoy.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 15, 2024
author: EmyRem
title: Massive Rice Cooker
body: This rice cooker is HUGE. I didn't realize how much space on my counter it would take, and if I had looked at that in advance, I would not have gotten this. I only make a few servings at a time, so if you are like me, please read the sizing on the listing before purchasing. I kinda regret getting it myself with my very limited kitchen space.Past that personal oof, I found that making rice in this is so easy and clear. The instructions help a bit and the measurements of rice to water are easy to keep to.The bowl is anti-stick, so cleaning is a cinch and washing the rice in a separate bowl prior to cooking will likely keep that anti-stick for much longer.There are only two modes, cook and warm. Basic but really all you need to get started. It automatically switches from cook to warm.The included basket, paddle, and scoop are lovely.All in all, if this were half or even a third the size, I'd swear it up and down. This is just so big, it would only make sense for a minority of households.

The instructions are easy to read for use and care and the cooker itself is easy to use since it has only one button for cooking and warming. Since both the white basket and inner pot comes out, it makes cleaning after use very easy. Last, not only is it a rice cooker, but it says you can also use it as a slow cooker (I have one of those so I probably won't use it for that but it is a nice to have just in case).

author: WDWRKR51
title: A VERY NICE BASIC RICE COOKER AND WARMER - TEN CUPS SIZE!
body: I am a member of the Amazon Vine program. I order new items, and then write honest, unbiased reviews of that product, along with my impressions and experiences. I am not paid for these reviews.I really do like this Cuckoo CR 1095 basic rice cooker. It is well-made, nicely finished, and it does a fine job of cooking my favorite white rice easily.This machine has a simple one-touch operation, a nice stick free inner pot, a rice measuring cup, and a rice scooper. It comes with a good operations manual. It looks good, and it is easy to clean. What more could you ask for your rice-loving family? This thing can save you time and effort while you tend to other things!I just made ONE mistake. We are a household of two, and my wife tells me we cannot eat all the rice this thing is capable of making.  Ten Cups might be just a bit too big for our purpose. This is not the fault of the Cockoo Rice Maker company, this one is on me.  I'm sorry.  Still, I do recommend this fine basic electric rice cooker and warmer. Happy eating!

title: good cooker but NOT capable of 6 cups dry rice - more like 4-5 max
body: On the whole I like this cooker. A few things to note:(1) It does NOT allow 6 cups dry rice. I'm not sure what rice type or method allows 6 cups dry rice; I make brown and black rices which I parboil 1st for 5 minutes, then rinse and add to Cuckoo cooker, using 1:1 water ratio (do NOT use more otherwise rice will be mushy) using multi-grain setting. there is no way this will allow 6 cups dry rice, more like 3-4 cups dry rice if you don't water flying out the top vent.(2) removing the rubber gasket from the inner plate is a bit of a chose and it's easy to damage the rubber gasket, so be careful - must be done though because it traps rice water steam which will grow bacteria(3) the user interface is not great and instructions are written in microscopic font. wish they'd spend some time making the instructions clearer, simpler and larger font.on the whole though I really like this as we cook enormous quantities of rice for 2 active people and a hungry dog every week!

body: There is no clock display which makes it a bit hard to learn how to deal with the timer. How many minutes to go at a time? Anti intuitive. Rice is cooked well though. author: Tobi
title: Great Affordable Starter Rice Cooker
body: I bought CUCKOO Rice Cooker when it was on sale and it was worth every penny. I do also own a more expensive model (with more bells and whistles), but when I moved states for a short term stay (6 months) I bought the cheaper version and was impressed by the value.For the price, this rice cooker delivers great results. The quick cook function is a huge plus, cooking rice in under 10 minutes. I have played around with the other settings and I get rice of the perfect texture nearly every time. The built-in measuring cup system, makes it super easy to gage the correct amount of water-to-rice as well.I liked how lightweight it is compared to the other models, that were extremely heavy.  On the downside, I have to hold the lid when opening since it will swing open and topple over (learned that the hard way).I also appreciate how easy it is to clean, between the non-stick pot and the fact its dishwasher safe.The only negative, is that I felt the fuzzy logic system on this model isn??t as advanced as the higher-end CUCKOO models, so it may not offer as much precision or extra features.For the price, it??s a great option for anyone looking for a reliable rice cooker without breaking the bank.

body: I will not purchase this brand again due to a number of quality issues. The top of the rice cooker is made from low quality material, therefore when water/steam comes through the top vent it stains the top of the cooker. The buttons are also hard to push over time. And lastly it stopped working within 15 months ?? which is enough to never purchase this brand again. Thankfully, Amazon was able to assist with a solution so I can order a different brand.

author: NC
title: This Product Could Be Great
body: This product could be great, if it didn't have one flaw that ruins it. On the inner pot, the markings for the water levels are imprinted into the metal, so after each time you cook, food is stuck in the tiny imprints. It is very difficult to try to get the food out of the tiny imprints in the metal, and it will take you a while to try to do so when washing it. Sometimes you won't be able to get all of the food out, no matter how hard you wash and scrub it. Unless you like to have food stuck inside the pot that you may not be able to wash out, I wouldn't recommend this model.

date: Reviewed in Canada on March 21, 2025
author: Dormouse
title: Absolutely unacceptable quality and customer service.
body: I purchased this rice cooker expecting it to last more than a few months, but was seriously disappointed. The bottom of the inner pot started bubbling and peeling off, which is not only gross but also potentially hazardous. I followed all care instructions to a T??no metal utensils, no dishwasher, and only used it for cooking rice. There is absolutely no excuse for this kind of wear on a product that??s supposed to handle heat.When I contacted the company, they didn't offer any kind of repair, replacement, or warranty support. Instead, they told me to buy a new inner pot??for over $70. That's nearly the cost of the entire unit! So essentially, their solution is ??sorry, just give us more money.??A rice cooker that self-destructs and a company that refuses to stand by its product? Hard pass. Do yourself a favor and look elsewhere??there are plenty of brands out there that actually care about quality and customer experience.

body: Wow! Just bought this rice cooker to replace my previous analog one. I used it for the first time and WOW! Being Asian, I??ve always bought fancy short grain rice. I??ve tried different rice brand and varying amounts of water but it seems to burn the bottom 100% of the time. Last week, I decided to upgrade to something more modern both aesthetically and technology wise. Am I glad I did! The first batch of rice came out perfect!!! The rice was chewy, in a good way, and not more burnt bottoms! The price of this rice cooker was about the same as my last analog cooker and that??s why I??m so surprised! title: Needs more safety controls
body: Very sleek design and cooks rice to perfection. Overall the construction feels solid. My reasoning for the 2 stars is because there seems to be some design flaws that really make this piece a bit of a fire hazard. The thing will stay on even if theres nothing in the pot or no pot at all in the cooker. This can be a bit alarming, for theres already been about 3 instances where i wake up and realize my kitchen is warmer than usual bc i left the rice cooker on all night. Also, the options are a bit confusing and the button/menu layout isnt very intuitive. The buttons are touch based and you have to fiddle with them to get the settings right every time. Overall this rice cooker is good at cooking rice but bad at many other things amd  wish it had some features in it to make it safer and not waste electricity.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2025
author: Fordhouse
title: Beautiful, but with flawed controls and markings.
body: I looks absolutely great compared to my old and trusty zojirushi, and the pot feels a lot sturdier, though there is major design flaw. My old Zojirushi was a 3 cup model, compared to the 6 cup capacity of this one, so I am not sure if that is a fair comparison. Maybe Zojirushi's higher capacity models also have sturdier pots. The downside of this model is the controls. They are not pleasant to use at all. Often, in fact almost always, I must press buttons several times before anything happens at all. Trying to cancel something, or to turnoff the keep warm setting can be frustrating when the buttons wont respond. Another downside is the markings inside the pot. Unless you are in an extremely well lit area, the measuring indications inside the pot are very difficult to read. Unlike the zojirushe, they are just raised stampings, and do not have a contrasting color. Having to use the flashlight on my phone to see how much water to pour in is frustrating. If you are using the rice cooker in a sufficiently lit area, this might be a non-issue, or at least a smaller issue. I also miss the little tune my that Zojirushi rice cookers plays when the rice is done. The rice comes out fine.

You can hear the sound it makes when it opens.Using the touch buttons is very frustrating and not user-friendly??you must press a very small area precisely for them to work. Come on, it??s just a rice cooker, not a spaceship! Why is it so hard to use? That said, the overall quality of the material is really good, but it??s not functional at all.

title: Easy to use! ???? I don??t dread cooking as much
body: I love this thing and use it almost every day. I??m neurospicy and I get overwhelmed with all of the cooking and sanitation steps with cooktop cooking, but I LOVE sauces and pastas.This cooker has reduced my cooking anxiety SO MUCH! And it has also been easy to shift to cooking sauces with this- I can still cook the meat first, add sauce and simmer for a while, and either add pasta and rice right in the cooker to finish the meal, or cook them aside and combine at plating.It??s really easy to clean as well, and there??s even a small water reservoir that catches the steam drips off of the lid. So you just dump that water, clean the lid and top, and wash the rice pot. Done. Easy!Oh! And the rice quality is great in my white-lady opinion. If you choose ??sticky rice?? it will come out sticky, etc.Note! The steam vent lets out a fair bit of water vapor, so use this on an open countertop and not beneath an overhead cabinet.

I reached out to customer service who said it would cost $30-100 for repairs, $20 for shipping (one way), and shipping there (most likely would be $50 for the size alone). That would be the cost of a new rice cooker. It worked great for the two years but now we have to use tape in order for the latch to stay closed. If the tape comes loose, the lid pops open and the rice becomes hard and inedible.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 10, 2024
author: Phyllis Boone
title: Easy to learn to use for great results!
body: After a few years of using a low- budget rice cooker with consistently frustrating results, I decided to splurge on a higher quality cooker with the ability to cook most other kinds of grains as well.This attractive cooker is meeting and exceeding my expectations. So far, both white and brown rices have been cooked to perfection. The cooker's menu is easy to follow. I found it to be intuitive. The instruction manual is translated from Korean, but is not hard to follow given the illustrations and associated text.Buyers should know that the recommended portion sizes are quite large, but with the extra left I put together a nice fried rice recipe for me and my husband.I would recommend this rice cooker for others!

title: Not Worth the Premium ?? Very Disappointing
body: I was really excited to upgrade to a Cuckoo rice cooker, especially since I??ve heard such great things and it costs quite a bit more than other brands. But after less than three months of use, the inner pot started showing wear and tear. I thought this had to be a defect, so I contacted customer service. To my surprise, they told me that this kind of wear is ??expected?? ?? which, honestly, is ridiculous.I??ve had an Aroma rice cooker for years, used it heavily, and never had any issues like this. Why am I paying a premium for Cuckoo, only to get worse quality and terrible customer service? The fact that they brush off this issue as normal is unacceptable. I was expecting something durable, but instead, I??m left wondering why I didn??t just stick with my old, reliable Aroma cooker.If you??re considering Cuckoo because you think paying more means better quality ?? don??t. I??ve been seriously let down. Save yourself the frustration and look elsewhere.

When I tried over and over to get this to make basmati or Jasmine rice and even with the settings the company suggested it came out "better/less mushy" but produced a crusty layer of clumpy rice at the bottom.  I switched to another regular rice cooker without this technology and all my rice turned out perfect like my old model that broke after 20 years.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 15, 2024
author: TW
title: This is NOT a pressure rice cooker like other Cuckoos.  Very disappointing.
body: This is NOT a pressure rice cooker. Be sure you understand that before buying.  It is just an ordinary rice cooker that cooks by steaming.  That makes it different from other Cuckoo rice cookers.  But that is not made clear in the product description.  Misleading description.  One of the great advantages of other Cuckoo rice cookers is that they cook by pressure cooking.  Not only does it speed the time, but it makes the rice come out exceptionally well.  Unfortunately, this is not like a normal Cuckoo rice cooker.  It just cooks by steaming.  And it is very poorly made compared to the high quality of other Cuckoo rice cookers.  It is way overpriced for what it is.  If you want a rice steamer of this quality, you can get one for a lot, lot less money.  Extremely disappointing.  Additionally, the rice sticks to the inner pot in a way that was not true of my previous Cuckoo cooker, making it harder to clean.  If you are looking for a good quality, traditional  Cuckoo rice cooker, look at one of the other models.  Do not buy this one.  I make rice every day and have used a Cuckoo cooker for years, with great happiness.  But this model is a piece of junk.  And it does not even do a good job of cooking rice.

author: Merbert
title: Slow but Accurate
body: Once you adjust the water quantities to correspond to the particular rice you are using to achieve the desired texture, the Cuckoo creates excellent rice every time. Porridge can be a bit irregular, but overall pretty good. Cleanup is a breeze. Making conventional rice on the stove is much quicker but requires some attention. The Cuckoo lets you measure, pour, and go. Unfortunately, I still cannot trust it to make perfect sushi rice, so it's the stovetop and a heavy pot for that. I'm too much of a purist for that!

title: Good, could be better
body: So.. it cooks rice perfectly. Only issue is the buttons. The buttons for the cooking options are not very sensitive, plus the actual area to press is slightly to the left of the button titles. It feels inefficient and slow because you have to get your finger in the right spot and press pretty hard and hope it registers your choice. Also, the large button on the front to open the lid is annoying. I prefer ones that have the button on top, but that's my fault for not realizing before purchase. The lid opens fast and it's so heavy that it rocks the cooker when it opens. You have to place your hand on the top of the lid and guide it open to prevent this, but that makes it easy to be burned by steam if you're not careful. Overall, it does what it needs to do, just wish it was designed a little more conveniently.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 23, 2024
author: Amazon Customer
title: Cuckoo Micon Rice Cooker
body: The CUCKOO Micom Rice Cooker CR-0675F Gray is a versatile kitchen appliance with 13 menu options, including White, GABA, Porridge, Baby, and more, catering to various cooking needs. Its Fuzzy Logic technology ensures precise cooking by adjusting temperature and cooking time based on the rice type, delivering consistently delicious results. With a capacity of 6 cups or 1.5 quarts (uncooked), it's suitable for small to medium-sized families. The sleek gray design adds a modern touch to any kitchen countertop. Overall, this rice cooker offers convenience, functionality, and reliability, making it a worthy addition to any kitchen arsenal.

author: Jae
title: Modern, Efficient, and Perfectly Priced Rice Cooker
body: After my old rice cooker gave up on me, I reluctantly switched back to stove-top rice before deciding it was time for a new cooker. I chose the CUCKOO Micom Rice Cooker for its attractive price point and modern look, not to mention the rave reviews about its performance. This cooker has not disappointed??every batch of rice comes out perfectly, and the operation is straightforward and hassle-free. I noticed some users were puzzled about how to turn off the heat, but a simple long press on the cancel button does the trick, shutting everything down smoothly. It??s an upgrade in both functionality and style from my previous cooker, and despite being one of the more budget-friendly offerings from CUCKOO, the quality is exactly what you'd expect from the brand. For anyone in the market for a reliable, easy-to-use rice cooker that also adds a touch of modernity to your kitchen, this model is a fantastic choice.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2024
author: Luna95
title: Nice  Micom Rice Cooker
body: Bought this Micom during Dec sale. I haven't tried korean cooker models before. My findings are its  consistently delivers well-cooked rice, regardless of the grain type. One of the key features of this rice cooker is its ability to cook rice to perfection every time. This ensures precise temperature control and cooking times, resulting in fluffy rice with each grain cooked evenly. It comes with user-friendly interface and clear LCD display make it easy to select cooking options and make adjustments.It comes with a capacity of 6 cups (uncooked), It looks very big It??s also equipped with a programmable delay timer, a feature that allows you to have freshly cooked rice ready at your desired time.The non-stick inner pot is another highlight looks solid and good quality , preventing rice from sticking and making cleaning effortless. The quality of the inner pot and accessories, such as the rice spatula and measuring cup, is commendable and adds to the overall durability of the product.While the cooker excels in cooking rice, it??s also versatile enough for other cooking tasks comes with various options  making baby food, steaming vegetables, and even preparing soups and porridges. The multicooking feature is particularly handy, offering convenience and freeing up time for other tasks.However, one potential downside is its relatively large size, which may take up more counter space than expected. . It??s not overly expensive, yet it performs well and has been free of issues for users over several months of use Worthful investment for those seeking consistency, convenience, and versatility in a rice cooker at economic price.

author: Katherine Dahlgren
title: Only lasted a little over a year when??
body: This rice cooker served us well for just a little over a year till it over flowed during a very normal sized 3 cups of rice and 4.5 cups of water batch. During the cooking phase water started bubbling over from the vent and almost instantly the top control panel went out. It was still cooking but I could tell what was happening or if it went to ??warming?? cycle. I would have expected a little hot water to make the unit completely useless. Now it doesn??t do anything when I plug in or try to select any options.

body: So if you don??t have anything holding the rice cooker at the back, you will have to hold the lid every time you open it. IT WILL FLIP THE WHOLE RICE COOKER IF YOU DONT. Bad design for sure but other than that the cooker is good.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2024
author: EL
title: Perfect for Baby Food and Beyond!
body: I used to own an expensive Cuckoo rice cooker, but after a couple of fixing attempts, I decided to donate it. When I saw this cooker on sale at a reasonable price, I thought, why not give it a try? Primarily, I use it for making baby food and steaming veggies. I've also explored multicooking, especially when preparing oxtail soup for my baby. Although it takes a little over an hour and a half, it's more convenient than constantly checking manually, and the meat turns out soft enough for my little one.I've even utilized the soup without meat to make porridge for my baby. Over the past year, it has worked perfectly for my needs. If you're only using it for cooking rice, it's just okay. Expensive rice cookers tend to make the rice sweeter and shinier, whereas this one is more basic in that regard. However, I'm delighted to continue using this rice maker for preparing a variety of foods. It's also easy to clean, which adds to its appeal.

date: Reviewed in Canada on January 1, 2024
author: Dobbs
title: Frustrating
body: This is a very poorly-designed piece of tech and I do not recommend it.The inner bowl, which is the only place that specifies the amount of water necessary, is basically illegible. I've attached a photo of mine. It simply cannot be read. I've literally carried the bowl into each room of my house to see if the light can make it more legible. Nope. You need this information every time you use the machine, so essentially you're frustrated every time you use it.That info (amount of water needed) is not in the manual. It's not on a sticker. It's literally only in one space and cannot be read.Next, the lid doesn't stay shut. Each time you press a button on the lid to change the program or start or end the process, the lid rattles and makes a click sound.In order to press the button to open the unit, you either need to use two hands or have the back of the unit up against a wall so stop it from moving. Only have one hand or only have one hand free? Forget it, you ain't getting your rice unless you slide the unit to the back of your counter.Then, when the lid does open, the whole pot leaps off the counter. Doesn't matter if the bowl is full or empty.Also, you know how modern manuals are in English, and then there's a French section and then German, etc. Nope. This one has French and English seemingly randomly spread. Two pages of English, one of French, three English, two French...I will be sending this back and will instead purchase a model that, at the very least, has legible markings in the bowl. I hated using this device.

author: Praz
title: Good
body: It's easy to use and clean. Rice remains warm and soft even after hours and hours of cooking. Only the downside is that the options/touch buttons on the rice cooker can't be seen clearly.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2023
author: K Truman.
title: Fluffy Rice All Day, But with a Tricky Lid
body: I recently bought the CUCKOO CR-0675F Micom Rice Cooker and it has been a fantastic addition to my kitchen. As a fan of the CUCKOO brand, I had high expectations, and this rice cooker certainly lives up to them. It consistently produces exceptionally fluffy and delicious rice, which is a big plus for any rice lover.One of the standout features for me is its ability to keep the rice perfect throughout the day. It's wonderful to be able to cook a batch in the morning and still have perfectly warm and fresh rice for dinner.However, there is a small drawback. The button to open the lid requires a bit of effort, often needing two hands to operate. This can be a bit inconvenient, especially when you're multitasking in the kitchen.Despite this minor issue, I'm very pleased with my purchase. The CUCKOO CR-0675F is reliable, efficient, and maintains the quality of the rice for hours. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a high-quality rice cooker, just be prepared for a little extra effort when opening it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2023
author: Marc
title: Amazing rice cooker
body: Replaced a 5 year old cuisinart 4 cup rice cooker and this is a massive upgrade! My rice dispenser measures in kilograms so I have to estimate the water level and its surprisingly leaves alot of room for error. I've added more or less water and rice always cooks well. The best part is that I can leave my rice in the cooker for two days and it still taste great by just using the reheat function!

date: Reviewed in the United Kingdom on December 5, 2023
author: Amazon Customer
title: DO NOT BUY!!!
body: The service was terrible to start off with. We received an incorrect adapter which first of all, took more than a month to be delivered, and on its arrival it didnt even work.Secondly the rice cooker has now stopped working within 6 months of purchase. Halfway through cooking, the rice cooker just ceased to turn on!

date: Reviewed in Canada on November 28, 2023
author: J
title: This brand probably have other good product but this isn't one.
body: Can't turn off without unplugging, the "buttons" are hard to feel and use.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2023
author: MOHAMMAD MOHTASHAM
title: Good all around rice cooker
body: I am Iranian, and my wife is Chinese. We have distinct preferences when it comes to eating rice. Chinese cuisine leans towards sticky rice, while Iranians prefer their rice to be well-grained and scorched at the bottom. That's why we opted for this rice cooker, allowing us to prepare rice in both styles. Since it is designed more for Asian cooking, it excels at making sticky rice using the quick and regular modes. However, crafting scorched rice takes approximately 1 hour. While it does a decent job, the rice tends to be a bit too sticky for the Iranian style, though this doesn't bother me personally.In terms of cleaning, the pot is easy to clean, but reassembling the lid can be a bit challenging.The touchscreen control buttons have a sleek appearance, but they are not as user-friendly as physical buttons. The captions are not easily readable from different angles, which might be a slight inconvenience for some, although it doesn't bother me much.I do wish it had a dedicated power button. Currently, the only way to turn it off completely is to unplug it, which can be a bit cumbersome as it requires the use of both hands.Despite these minor drawbacks, I am considering buying this rice cooker again mainly because of its scorched rice feature.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 27, 2023
author: xoraclez
title: Not the best rice cooker for my needs
body: As a long time Zojirushi owner, this Korean brand let me down.I was hoping for equivalent performance as the trusty Zojirushi NS-LGC05XB that I've been using for over a decade.Sadly, the Cuckoo doesn't measure up.Pros :its a very good quality rice cooker with all the features that I wantedCons :it doesn't have a quick cook modethe cup/water level marks are impossible to read (black on black!!!)the water level marks are not correct!  I need to add more water that indicated or else I get "crunchy" rice!the Oatmeal setting is worthless (i have to cook at least 3-4 times at that setting to get an acceptable bowl of oatmeal!!!)No cake mode!  (I need this to make fluffy japanese pancakes).Summary, I wish I had just bought another Zojirushi instead of this.Sure, it makes great rice if you know how to overcome its idiosyncrasies, but everything else is a huge let down.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2023
author: youreajaywalker
title: It??s okay for day of rice
body: This cooker makes tasty rice. It??s a small minimalistic design that doesn??t take up too much space. However, if you want to make a batch of rice and leave it in the warmer, the quality of the rice goes bad after 30 hours or so.I wish I could make a batch of rice that would last longer than 2 days. This isn??t the cooker for that. Spend a little extra if you??re looking for something more.

date: Reviewed in Canada on November 25, 2023

body: This thing is so aesthetic visually, but acts so goofy.I bought the 3 cup version. Whenever I open the lid, it is so heavy it makes the rice cooker slides back 4 inches. The inner pot is too small and spins in place, when I want to scoop rice I have to sacrifice my finger to hold the hot inner pot so I can scoop rice and it doesn't run away from me.Start up instructions don't say anything about cleaning before using, smelled like factory for the first 2 times I cooked it.Regular cook and turbo cook of white rice produces very different texture and taste so use that to your advantage. Tastes good both ways3 stars cause I'm still in denial

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 17, 2023
author: Kenny Lor
title: Delivery, set-up, and quality
body: The delivery was great and came on the time! Setting the rice cooker up was pretty easy as it already came pre-set. All I had to do was read instructions, wash the rice bowl, and wipe it down with a damp towel. The rice quality was great, I usually cook white rice. I have not tried it for sticky rice though. I would say the rice cooker was pretty perfect. Except since all the wiring and buttons are on top, the balance on the rice cooker is a bit funny when you open it.The button to open is at the front of the rice cooker on the sides. When clicked open the top part becomes heavy and makes the rice cooker unbalanced, leaning toward the side with the most weight. It did cause a concern since I would like to open the rice cooker without holding it down. I usually hold the back end of the side before clicking open. This cuckoo should be opened carefully. Even though I held it down with both hands it was still able to tip a bit.Overall I still really like it and I feel it was fitting for the price.I hope my review helps you in deciding if this Cuckoo is right for you!

author: norminsf
title: Love the modern look but missing some details
body: i purchased this cuckoo because i wanted to try a different brand than my previous rice cooker, which was a top japanese brand.  i was super happy with my previous rice cooker which i've been using for about 20 years.  i assumed the cuckoo would be just as good in design and cooking.i received the unit in good condition, it was packed very well and came with a rice paddle and rice measuring cup.  out of the box, this cuckoo is beautiful.  i stared and admired it for a few minutes then pressed on the button to open the top lid....when the heavy lid swung open and tilted the unit back a bit, this was my first disappointment.  then i realized the cord was not designed to be retractable, nor does it have a place to keep the cord tidy.  the unit also does not have a handle.i was still excited to use the unit and the cooking buttons are not as intuitive.  also delayed cooking timer has to be set by how many hours later you want it to cook...not the time you would like the rice to be finished.  also, if you brush by the 'cancel' button with your hand, it will stop the cooking.the rice does finish perfect.  we've tried oatmeal and that cooks fine as well.overall, i can't say it's the best rice cooker i have ever had.  i've have had quite a few in my lifetime.this cuckoo does cook rice very well but there are some missing features that will probably shift me back to the top japanese brand next time.

The rice cooker is low quality, very plastic, cheap and lightweight. Buttons are hard to press, not the common knob ease selection. The inner cooking bowl is thin metal.No way steel cut oatmeal will cook in 10 mins the lowest setting they have to cook in it??s setting function! It was just sitting in lukewarm water for that 10 mins .Very disappointed. Even if Cuckoo approved their name to be manufactured elsewhere. date: Reviewed in the United States on June 30, 2023
author: Secret Angel
title: Reliable and Efficient Rice Cooker
body: I recently purchased the CUCKOO CR-0675F 6-Cup (Uncooked) Micom Rice Cooker, and overall, it has been a reliable addition to my kitchen. This rice cooker offers efficiency and ease of use, delivering well-cooked rice consistently.One of the standout features of this rice cooker is its compact size. With a capacity of 6 cups (uncooked), it is perfect for smaller households or those with limited counter space. The compact design doesn't compromise on performance, as it cooks rice evenly and thoroughly.The micom (microcomputer) technology employed by the CUCKOO CR-0675F ensures precise temperature control and cooking times. This results in well-cooked rice with a desirable texture and consistency. The rice is fluffy, and each grain is properly cooked without any undercooked or overcooked portions.The user-friendly interface and simple operation make this rice cooker accessible to both beginners and experienced users. The clear LCD display and intuitive buttons allow for easy selection of cooking options and adjustments. Additionally, the programmable delay timer is a convenient feature, enabling you to have freshly cooked rice ready at your desired time.The non-stick inner pot is another positive aspect of this rice cooker. It prevents rice from sticking to the bottom, making cleaning a breeze. The inner pot and accessories, such as the rice spatula and measuring cup, are of good quality and durable.

author: Su-Tsen Wu
title: Cook Rice Fast
body: This is an all digital rice cooker. When turn on, all the cooking options show up on the screen. You have to use the arrow button to get to the option (white rice, brown rice, etc.)The exterior is simple and clean-cut with a flat top.  I got the 3-cup cooker so it does not take a lot of space on the counter.  The interior is designed for easy cleaning.  The top inner lid can be removed and put back easily with a snap.One little negative point: I paid a slightly higher price for a model CR-0375FW that is supposed to be made in Korea (per the product description). I got one (model CR-0375F) that is made in China.  I am sure both perform the same.  However, I felt I was misled and paid extra for the same model that was sold for a few dollars less.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 12, 2023
author: Elise Maloney
title: Update - NO Sushi rice setting!!!
body: There??s still no sushi rice setting?? BUT, that being said?? it??s still a great machine. I soaked my rice for 30 minutes and then just cooked on the standard white rice setting and I had PERFECT sushi rice. I mean PERFECT. This machine will be getting a lot of use in our houseDespite what other people have answered, there is literally no sushi rice setting on this machine. Very disappointed. I'm going to give it a try with just soaking my rice for 30 minutes before cooking it on the white rice setting (essentially doing what I would do with my stovetop sushi rice) - then adding my salt/sugar/rice vinegar mix. Hope this works out since this was pretty much what I wanted it for to begin with.

author: C
title: Really good budget rice cooker
body: We??ve been using this rice cooker for a little over 2 months and love it so far! It cooks rice pretty fast and is super easy to clean. The only complaints we have are about the design of the rice cooker. The inside pot (that you put the rice in) doesn??t have handles so you??ll have to wait until it cools to clean it and it moves when you are fluffing/scooping the rice. There isn??t a little holder for the paddle it comes with. The button to open the rice cooker is hard to press without two hands. It also closes/opens kinda aggressively. These cons are not that big a deal to us but are definitely notable. If you??re looking for a cheaper rice cooker that will (probably) last you longer than a black+decker one then this one is perfect!!! It??s also really cute :)

We have gotten up to 3 cups rice to 4 cups water and set it on sticky rice and it's the worst batch yet.  Had to throw the rice out. I'm just sorry I waited to long to return this.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2022
author: Hard2Please
title: Not perfect, but makes good rice
body: Preface: I'm on a quest for the perfect, reasonably priced rice cooker. Sadly this is not it the one for me. My family eats white, sticky rice every day, so I'm looking for a rice cooker that looks good on the counter and not only makes tasty rice, but also keeps it warm and soft for days. My old Panasonic was retired after 25 years. It was replaced with a Zojirushi NS-TSC18 which made tastier rice, but after a few months, it started drying out rice on the keep warm setting. I tried going back to a basic Zojirushi with no bells and whistles (Zojirushi NS-RPC18FJ), but I realized that a micom (microcomputer) rice cooker does actually make rice with better taste and texture.As for the Cuckoo CR-0675F | 6-Cup (Uncooked) Micom Rice CookerPROS:- cooks rice well- keeps rice warm without drying it out- high quality non-stick finish on cooking pan- versatile menu options- compact design- attractive appearance (important since mine lives on the counter)- affordably pricedCONS:- push button on front (the whole unit slides back when you push the button)- top-heavy lid (in order to keep footprint small, electronics are in the lid. When you open the lid, it has so  much momentum that the entire unit rocks back and forth)- quantity markings inside the pan are hard to read (black on black)- I prefer a longer soak time in the programming (of course one could just soak the rice before starting the  cycle, like I used to do with my old rice cooker; it really does improve the taste and texture)- not a retractable cord- no spatula holderFor the money, it is a decent rice cooker, but for me, there are too many annoying things about it to put up with every day.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2022
author: Spade and Scalpel
title: Functional & Stylish
body: Unlike all other traditional rice cookers. This rice cooker is compact, sleek and functional. It has a few more "buttons" than the usual flip switch - so you can select what type of rice you will be using. Cooked 2 cups of uncooked Jasmine rice and the video shows you the amount of space left inside.Couple design flaws that may or may not be deal-breaker for others: 1) cooker may topple back when you press button to open lid - solution: hold top lid as you press button as I did on the video 2) no handles to make it easy to grasp or hold if you want to move it - solution: still able to hold it like a planter or carry it with arm/s around it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 22, 2022
author: DeeGee
title: There are better choices
body: I use my rice cooker to make rice a few times each month, and to make oatmeal practically every day.  I generally make the oatmeal using traditional "old fashioned" rolled oats and I only make enough for one serving which is about 1/3 cup of uncooked oats.  My old rice cooker, a brand that started with a Z, worked great for both of these tasks for 5 years until it broke (the handle latch).  Instead of having it repaired I decided that the cookers with fuzzy logic must all be similar, so I purchased this one in the 3 cup version.  I usually make jasmine rice and so far it has tended to be on the dry and hard side, despite following the instructions exactly.  I'm sure in time I'll get it adjusted to be right.  The oatmeal however has been a great disappointment.  My old machine made delicious creamy oatmeal, though once in a while it would spill over and make a mess.  I never could figure out why that happened when it did.  This Cuckoo machine has belched out a gloppy mess through the vent every single time.  I've tried adjusting all settings including cooking time, warming temp, ratios of oatmeal/water, rinsing longer, etc.  all to no avail.  I switched to steel cut oats and it no longer spills over so I'm hopeful that I'll get a good end result eventually, but I prefer the traditional oats.  I also prefer to put them in the night before on timer so they are ready in the morning.  This process was much easier on the old machine.  I'd set it and it stayed set.  This one requires re-setting of the timer duration each night.  In general, this machine probably offers more user adjustable functions, but the old one was simpler and just plain worked better.  I'm quite disappointed.  The manual is also very poorly written.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 7, 2022
author: N. Morris
title: Barely lasted a month
body: I really wanted to like this pot. My friends joke that I'm one of the few (only?) Asians they know who doesn't use a rice cooker. Even my mom used a rice cooker when I was growing up. But I married a non-asian who barely cooks but knows how to boil rice. When he showed me how, it was so easy, so I just made rice on the stove for the past 18 years.After one  burnt rice incident too many, I was finally sold on the thought that I can program the rice cooking and avoid burning my house downCuckoo seemed like a good option. Good reviews, not too big, cute enought to sit on my counter; not too expensive but not suspiciously cheap. She arrived carefully and beautifully packaged.I did not understand her buttons, even after studying the manual. So I decided to keep things nice and simple between Cuckoo and I as we got to know each other. Wash rice in a different bowl, drain,  pour rice into the rice cooker pot; measure water; wipe everything dry and pop everything into the cooker. Set Sticky White Rice -> Start. Seemed easy enough.First time I used it, the jasmine rice cooked nicely. Second time I tried sushi rice and it didn't cook through.  Could have been my mistake with measuring the water but I figured I'll start understanding Cuckoo better soon and things  will improve. Third time, I got an error mid-way. There's no off button and the error beep would not stop. I had to unplug then reset the cooking options.Well, this should have been the point that I decided it was time for goodbye; but no... Cuckoo and I continued like this with me trying to coax her and she sometimes obliging. I thought, she comes with a warranty maybe they can fix her. I called the customer service line and got a kind-sounding lady who didn't speak much english.  I finally understood everyone was out at lunch and I should call back.A few days later I tried again and got someone who spoke a little bit more english. My options were to ship them Cuckoo (I would pay for shipping) and they would then figure it out and ship her back. At this point I wasn't keen at at all in pouring more money into this.Unfortunately I was 2 days beyond the Amazon return window. I hate the thought of tosing a $100 pot that barely lasted a month; so I may just bite the bullet and send it back for repair. In the meantime, it's just me and my trusty old quart pot and I'll try not to burn the house down. But really, it is very easy to cook rice on the stove.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 25, 2022
author: Jason Williamson
title: Better rice cooker = better rice
body: I think like most people, I assumed any rice cooker is just as good as the next. Dear God was I wrong. We had the old throw down Oster rice cooker that we've had for years. I think we paid $15 for it at Wally world back when my wife and I first got together, and we made plenty of rice in it over the years.Finally much to our chagrin, it died. It was a peaceful death and we had a beautiful celebration of life ceremony for it, right before we threw it in the trash can. That's when I went on the hunt for a new one. I knew I wanted a slightly larger cooker, since we had felt like we couldn't make enough rice for dinner parties. That was basically my only requirement when I started looking.I had no idea that there were so many rice cookers out in the world, nor did I know you could pay in excess of $500 for one. Who knew?I found out rather quickly that cuckoo was a very widely used and respected brand and slapped down about $100 for this one. Doing a little better than I was 15 years ago, I figured why not.And that's when I realized that I had been eating terrible rice made by a cheap rice cooker all these years. The first time I made rice in this cooker I couldn't believe how much better it was. This thing consistently churns out the best tasting rice I've ever had. I legitimately eat rice at least three times a week now. And I can't believe I went this long eating the gruel that my old cheap cooker was producing.It's got more options than you're likely to ever use. But it has 4 different settings for white rice. Savory is my favorite, even though I takes the longest. There is also a quick rice setting which cooks in a comparable amount of time as my old one. But still makes better rice.I would highly recommend this machine if you're in the market for one. As I said, buy a better rice cooker and you'll get better rice.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2022
author: NG
title: Several things to consider before purchase
body: First, it is pretty. I have a white and grayish kitchen and it looks so pretty but there are several things I found it uncomfortable using it as a daily rice cooker. So far Ive had it for about good 3 months or so. It cooks rice ok. It keeps it warm ok. It is missing a holder on the side for a rice scooper so I have to leave it on the top of the lid. ( my previous rice cooker had a scooper holder on the side which was so convenient to use ) the power cord kinda doesn't stay plugged in well to the port. When I pull the rice cooker towards me, the power cord pops out easily from the port losing the power instantly. If you don't notice right away, you will end up with a cold rice when you are about to eat. The water level on the rice pot is hard to read compare to other rice cookers. The buttons on the lid????.. soooo hard to press??. I dont have a crooked finger??. It just wont press right?? lastly, to set a timer??.. wow??.. to set up to cook rice after 12 hours from now, I have to press button so times to go to the right timing since 1 click gives you 10 minutes?? They should had to make it easier by putting a clock on the rice cooker so I can just easily set up to like for example 6:30 am or so instead of having to set up to be cooked after 12 hours from now making me click 72 times??

body: Well I don't know if these reviews are real or fake since I've seen them exact same ones in another website and also I don't see this exact model (Cuckoo CR-0675F) in their Cuckoo website, to be honest it only appears in Amazon and Bestbuy when you google it.Anyways, so far I've only used it once and it made flawless rice like advertised. No complains so far looks really sleek for a rice cooker but will update soon the more I use it. Also it says 6 cups uncooked, 12 cups cooked, well I don't know how is this possible and I think is very misleading since that is what I was actually looking for anyways I will still keep it because of the looks and how well rice makes. But to explain what that it means is this product comes with its own measuring cup and I guess that's how they measured the 6 cups which are actually half a cup of a regular "American cup" capacity. So far I cooked only once and I used jasmine rice 2 cups of rice and 1.5 cup of water per cup of rice and it got to the top of the measuring ruler the inner pot has which I think that is the max raise of rice.Also I've only washed it by hand and it looks very durable not cheap made in anyway.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2025
author: Susan I
title: Cooks good and cute but it spits starchy rice water all over while it??s cooking
body: I really like this rice pot for its price point, it cooks well and I like the keep warm feature, it keeps the rice for a few days although I won??t push it past 3 days personally. I??ve played with the customized cook feature and it??s awesome. I like our rice a bit stickier. My only issue with this pot is from day one, as it cooks it releases sticky starchy stream water as it cooks. It covers the lid and runs down the back all over the counter. And if you don??t catch it right away, it dries up to a paste that you have to soak to get off. The water on the cooker will just flake right off the plastic when it dries. But this nasty sticky counter water issue is pretty depressing. I??ve resorted to ??diapering?? it by setting the back end of it on a light kitchen towel and draping the other half of it over the top vent. It works okay, better than doing nothing. My steam vent cap and inner lid are washed every time, so it??s not plugged. I can??t figure it out. But other than that, rice tastes great, easy to use, nice to look at, the rice pot itself is strong and cleans up like a dream, it all comes right out if u let it dry. Truly nonstick!  And it keeps well for a while. I??d probably upgrade to a higher model next time but this size is great for the two of us.

body: I was given this Korean brand rice cooker as a gift from  relative of mine who vacationed in Korea. They know I like fancy looking kitchen appliances. I also have many different rice cookers some costing over $450 which are made in Japan. I even have a Korean rice cooker which is also a pressure cooker from this company that was also over $350.  This rice is cooker is very inexpensive costing less than $75. It is attractive and very easy to use. This brand is very popular in Korea and is found in many homes unless they can afford the better Japanese made brands. This rice cooker wasn't used very often but unfortunetly it also didn't last very long. It was used maybe twice a month but only lasted just over year. The buttons no longer function and the rice cooker no longer turns on.  Nice looking but quality wise not built to last. And although I have a German name I am part Islander and Asian.

author: Julie
title: I LOVE MY CUCKOO.
body: If you??re like me, you love Asian food but struggle with executive function in the kitchen and in feeding yourself properly.I??d been making steamed/sticky rice on the stovetop for years because I??d never had a rice cooker- which was an extra effort to get the heat and timing right, and even still I tended to struggle to get the rice quality where I wanted it.THIS THING IS AMAZING.All you literally have to do is throw your rice in, fill the water level to the premeasured lines depending on the type of rice, and tell it what kind of rice it??s cooking and.. that??s it. It literally does all the rest.And beyond that, it will keep the rice warm enough to be in the safe range to eat for LITERAL HOURS. I can make rice for dinner and have some leftover for breakfast and to make my lunch with.It??s so easy to clean, too- the nonstick surface is exceptional, the LID LINER even comes out and washes clean with ease.The cooking time is quick, even when not on turbo, and the amount of rice this bad boy will handle is insane. Up to six cups . SIX. The taste and texture of the rice is on POINT, too.God I can??t say enough good things about this rice cooker. Please get yourself one. It??s exceptional.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2024
author: Chichi
title: Not what I expected
body: I bought this because my old Tiger rice cooker was slowly breaking. I wanted to try something new and upon seeing all of the positive reviews and feedback from friends and family, I decided to go with this one.Instructions were clear and easy to set up. My husband and I went according to the chart that was suggested in the manual. We tried many different kinds to see how we can make our rice perfect. I was so excited to try this new rice cooker as it had so many different features. This Cuckoo model failed me immediately. As the rice was cooking (in turbo mode), the rice cooker was emitting this strange burnt plastic smell. That was already alarming. Just after 12 hours the rice has been cooked, all of the rice turned yellow and smelled old, as if it has been sitting for days. I ignored this and decided to give it a few more chances. Even after changing the modes and features, the results were the same. After a week of use, I decided to return the rice cooker. Thankfully, I kept my old Tiger rice cooker, so I??ll be using it until I find another. I was really hoping for long-lasting, white, and fluffy rice. My old tiger rice cooker can keep my rice warm and white even after 48 hours. I??ll try to give Cuckoo another chance again with a different model but until then, I??ll stick to my Tiger.

But I am a student, so I did not need a lot of bells and whistles, and this one just does the basics of cooking rice well. The flavor of the rice is good enough and the cooking time is not too long. Strongly recommend for students!

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 20, 2023
author: Alvin
title: This rice cooker from south korea is a TANK. Cuckoo is amazing.
body: My family have always brought Tiger brand rice cooker from Japan which lasted 10-15 years but after these new style of space pod like cookers came out, the Tiger brand from Costco, it would always go bad due to a lack of ventilation and condensation after the second day. I have no idea why the new models does not have a water collecting cup like the previous tiger brand but alas, it was returned. I have heard of cuckoo brand from watching my favorite streamer on Twitch when she did a cooking stream and thus, the person who owns it is a chef. I decided to jump the gun and buy one. Out of of the box, it does look like the newer style pods one however, it has a water collecting cup! This means that the rice will stay good for a week! Sure enough, after cooking it, the rice came out perfect and stayed the same throughout the week! After reading the manual, I activated the "turn off" during night time, but "reheat" again starting at 4am and that further preserves the rice! I have no idea how South Korea manufactures rice cookers but this is the Cadillac of all rice cookers! I will never go back to another brand!Con: the buttons are extremely difficult to press, especially with no rubber feet for resistance. You will have to press in with the other hand holding the back of the rice cooker for resistance. Also, the top flings open when you open it so you should be prepared with the second to gently catch it.Easy start mode: To start cooking, press "cancel" (to cancel the reheat option) and press "cook." Press it again if you want turbo mode (supposedly cook rice faster but I rarely use it). Nonetheless, it takes two buttons (cancel and start) to start the rice instead of a lever like in the older style rice cooker. Takes 40 mins to cook rice. Well worth it.Big enough to feed a family of four.

author: Michael Kiese
title: Changed our lives
body: This Cuckoo rice cooker is amazing.I have been using Zojirushi ever since I was a kid.This Cuckoo is better.  It cooks rice much faster, and keeps the rice edible for much longer.It's just great.I grew up in an Asian family where rice is a staple.  Rice never went bad due to drying out or burning because it was all gone and eaten up between all the kids in the house.Now that I'm an adult and I have a 2 person household, the rice tends to not get eaten up very fast.  With the Zoji, by day 2, the rice was already brown and yellowing.  By day 2 the Cuckoo's rice still looks like it is only a couple of hours old.This Cuckoo rice cooker really is amazing.  It's reduced food wastage, and it's also reduced me over eating rice to not waste food.  It's very convenient because you can come home from a long day, and you know that at least the rice is still good so you can cook something else.A real time saver.  Can't recommend it enough!

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 6, 2022
author: Brigandere
title: Great Rice Cooker but DOES NOT LAST
body: This is an excellent rice cooker, especially the "quick cook" option which makes the pot of rice in a much shorter time, even as quickly as 30 minutes - most other rice cookers routinely require 60 minutes to cook a pot of rice regardless of the number of cups of rice.  I gave this a 4 star review only because of one problem - the rice cooker slides to easily on counter surfaces.  This is our second purchase because our first slid off of the counter when we bumped it while wiping down the counter with a cloth.  It hit the ground and the silver colored latch that holds the lid in place when closed popped off and bent.  We were able to continue to use it for another few months but over time, the lid sat more and more open and last week it popped open while cooking so we finally gave up and purchased a new one.  I tried looking for replacement latches online but none are available.  For the new one, I added these adhesive rubber pads to the bottom and the rice cooker does not budge when bumped.  Enjoy!UPDATE: so we purchased a second CUCKOO because we thought that we broke the locking clip when the pot fell off of the counter.  Well, turns out that the locking clip for the lid is a faulty design.  Our second purchase is having the same problem after a few months.  I included pics.  I cannot find any replacement parts.  I WOULD NOT PURCHASE THIS.  It makes great rice and quickly but after a while, you will not be able to use it because when the locking clip fails, the lid will no longer stay locked down and the rice will no longer cook.

We retired to a rural area that does not have any good teriyaki (my go-to comfort food), so we are learning to make it ourselves, and this Cuckoo rice cooker makes it that much easier. Overall I'm not going to deduct a star though I do wish I can just buy a replacement part and keep trucking along. 6.5 years is a good run. title: Do not buy this.
body: It's been about 2 months since I purchased this.  Initially, it worked fine.  Last week, I put in 4 cups of rice and poured water accordingly to the line.  After the beep that indicated the rice was done, I tried it right away.  The rice wasn't done.  I thought perhaps it's the capacity issue and only cooked 2 cups next time. date: Reviewed in the United States on September 23, 2021
author: Clara
title: Strong chemical smell that won't go away
body: The inner pot as well as the rice cooker itself has a lingering chemical smell. Other than that it is a good product. I've purchased this exact rice cooker a few years ago with no problem, but the inner pot has a bit of scratches after years of usage so I ended up ordering a new one. It came a bit wobbly with a little squeak when I'd open the lid (it came new definitely); which is no longer an issue. But this chemical smell is very noticeable. I am taking my chance and ordering a replacement. Keeping my fingers crossed as many have said that they did not have this issue.10/15/21I've received my replacement and tested it out for two weeks now. I'd like to mention that I don't believe this particular one was new as the inside pot had smudge marks (oily?), and the time on the cooker was running (5:45 pm lol) unplugged like they do once the cooker has been used to make rice. But it looked almost new; maybe new.This cooker also has a chemical smell, but a very slight one and I think there is a good reason behind that. The inner pot is smooth on the outside; polished? Whereas the other inner pot had a rough feel to it, as though it wasn't polished? sealed smoothly? ... not sure if that makes sense but I did notice a significant difference; chemical smell.So, while I don't have to worry about the chemical smell so much, the new replacement I was sent has its own issue: the plastic attachment to collect the water from the rice cooker when it's cooking my rice doesn't catch any water at all. Why? Because steamy water droplets decides to stay inside on the top of the cooker, and all over around above the rice that's just been freshly cooked. And I didn't even unplug the cooker. Not a single water drop in the plastic thingy in two weeks because they all like to stay in the rice cooker. This was not the case with the other two I've had previously. Maybe this one is introverted ... hmm ... oh well

author: Amazon Customer
title: Good when it works!
body: This was delivered on July 13th, 2021. I barely use it since I don't eat rick, but my husband and kids do. We make rice 3-4 times a week at most. And the most cup of rice we make is about 3 cups each time. Today, I woke up to make breakfast for my family and it just completely stopped working. I tried everything! I wiped the inside down using a dry towel, including the insert and under the lid. Plugged it in. Hit the quick cook button. Nothing. It only shows the outline of the rice on the top left. The time in the middle. But nothing else. No error. Nothing! How is this even ok? Ugh! JUNK! Now I have to go find a box to return this. How inconvenient and annoying!

author: Naughty Chicken
title: Great Rice, easy to clean
body: I think it takes a bit longer than our previous rice cooker (OYAMA) but it's a cute little machine that's easy to clean and pretty easy to use. You really have to press the button to make it start, like, one hand on the back of the machine and one finger poking the button, because the weight of the machine doesn't counteract the amount of pressure you have to use to press the buttons. But that is the only complaint I have. The rice was delicious, a wonderful texture, and the pot is so easy to clean. I was impressed that I set the time on it, then unplugged it to move it across the kitchen, and the time was still set when I plugged it back in.I don't think I care enough about rice to buy one of the $200 rice cookers, though we do eat rice 1-2 times a week in our house. So this seemed like a good middle ground. I am glad I got it.

The sticky rice comes out so beautifully perfect. Even the short grained brown rice came out perfect and slightly sticky (a serious perk if you??re using chopsticks).The warming feature is honestly magical. I forgot to turn the cooker off after cooking some brown rice, only to discover it the next day (between 12-15 hours later) still moist and perfect. Cannot stress enough how much we love this cooker!

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2020
author: Sandy P
title: Such an improvement
body: I bought this to replace a 15 year old Zojirushi micom 4 cup that was starting to show degradation (rice wasn??t cooking well and lots of cleanup). Decided to go with the Cuckoo because several of my mom??s friends had them, and the price was more affordable than another Zojirushi. Wow, what an improvement. This is in no way saying it is better than a new Zojirushi...but it??s a definitely an improvement on my old one. Flavor is good, cook time seems to be a bit faster than my previous unit, and the menu is easy to understand. The best part is the fact that the steam this unit generates is minimal while cooking. One of the biggest issues with my previous cooker is that I, like so many other, use it on the counter, which is directly below the kitchen cabinets/cupboard. As it cooked, it would send out a steady stream of steam through most of the cooking time that would condensate and threaten to warp my cabinet doors and base. This Cuckoo is a great improvement over that one and while steam does escape, the volume is much less than what I'm use to. Aesthetics-wise: despite cooking twice the amount of rice, it??s almost the same size as my Zoj and the pink color is quite cute. I also appreciate the ladle holder on the side.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2020
author: RT
title: Gives me yummy, fresh-tasting rice every day!
body: My family is Korean and my mom uses this at her place. When I moved out I got a cheap Zojirushi which did the job but didn't have any other functions (warming being the most notable one) and I missed this cooker so badly that even two years later, it's the one I decided to get. And it's just as great as I remembered! It has a turbo mode which takes roughly thirty minutes, and a regular mode which doesn't take a whole lot longer (maybe an hour?). I haven't noticed a difference in taste between turbo or regular, besides the turbo-cooked rice not seeming to keep as long in the cooker. It's super easy to clean, just pop out the inner pot and wash it with a sponge, dry and put it back. Do take care to clean off the metal part on the 'roof' of the cooker, as that can affect taste when it gets dirtied.This cooker is a tad pricy but you pay for quality!

They are amazing! I don't think I will ever buy another brand again.As far as performance I usually cook white rice and it comes out fantastic.
