date: Reviewed in the United States on May 6, 2019
author: Amanda n Brandon
title: Time is everything
body: I bought this on March 6, 2019 and today is May 5,2019. I waited until now to do this review for one reason, the pot. In every rice cooker I've ever purchased,  the pot always ends up scratched in one way or another, even though there's never any metal in them.  I thought it was because I was buying cheap ones.  Well In less than 2 months this much more expensive rice cooker did something else, the protective layer on the pot is actually bubbling up and off. Let me tell you why that's a problem. I use it once every 3 days to make rice for my meal prep, as soon as it's done it's then scooped into my meal prep containers. It's turned off, left to cool, then immediately hand washed, paper towel dried and put back in its place, and i'm talking about everything that needs cleaned. So for this to happen is just wrong and messed up. If I wanted a cheap rice maker, then I would have bought American. It's a real shame to say this because it makes the best rice I've ever had, but quality is everything when it comes an item like this. But if you are looking for long lasting, maybe buy a Japanese rice cooker like I was going to buy didn't because I love Korea.

title: best rice cooker
body: We were tempted to get the cheaper Cuckoo rice cooker, but we found out that the cheaper one was made in China but this one is from Korea.  So far, we love it, and it is the best rice cooker we have ever owned.  It takes one push of a button. Also, if we accidentally unplug the power and replug it back in, it resumes its warming cycle.  It makes plenty of rice for one person  or for an entire family. Only negative is that the color is pink.  I wish it was in another color.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2019

title: good for about 2 years
body: works great and super easy to clean. taking off a star for the weird smell it has when washing the rice bowl-----UPDATED: I bought this rice cooker almost two years ago... within the last few months, the rice cooker has really started to disappoint. The water spews out from the sides and top, creating a mess of sticky rice water around it. The rice doesn't come out consistently even when the same measurements are used. It dries up and turns yellow within a few hours and the rice has started to taste and smell bad after a few hours. Can't let it sit more than a day, otherwise the rice is no good. Though cost-efficient and very pretty, the quality is not up to par with other Cuckoo rice cookers I've had in the past. Disappointing because each of the rice cookers my family had previously lasted 10+ years. Had I known this one would only has me a 2 years, I would have rather paid much more for one that would last another 10+years.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2018
author: Amazon Customer
title: It's SO FLUFFY!!!!
body: I'm rating the cuckoo on three things: cook of rice, style/color and complexity/features.Cook of Rice: 5/5 I have had cheap one button rice cookers, besides the expensive zojirushi my parent have. I could never justify buying an expensive rice cooker but I have been eyeing this one for a while. It only took one Christmas gift to make me a believer (and a warranty, in case it breaks).Style: 4/5 I like the shape, but the pink color is painful to me at times. It literally is the only pink thing in my kitchen.Complexity/Features: 4/5 I do need to say that I also have an instantpot, so the slow cooker feature compare to it, it not as good. But for people that do not want both, or have both, it is beneficial. The manual was a little confusing, that I had to read twice just to be sure I was understanding it correctly.Also since I just got it, I have not tried all the features, like the delay cook, but I will say that my rice is SUPER fluffy.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 14, 2018
author: Allaya Fleischer
title: Excellent rice cooker!
body: I really appreciate the thoughtful engineering in this rice cooker, particularly the stream collection cup in the back. The top plate on the lid comes off and goes on pretty easily, and is no trouble to clean.  I'm also pleased with the heavy weight of the cooking bowl, which no doubt assists in even coming of the rice. The reheat function is quick and effective, and I particularly appreciate the time cook function, which guarantees my rice will be done exactly when I need it. The insulation is very good, compared to other rice cookers I've used, which keeps the outside surfaces only slightly warm to the touch when it's in use. Even in warm mode, which I use frequently over Shabbat, it doesn't dry the rice out as much as my previous rice cookers.Although I have this rice cooker five stars because it  cook fantastic rice, there are a couple of minor things that you might consider before buying this rice cooker. First, if you aren't familiar with rice cookers, or are not a terribly technical person, it's worth mentioning that the instructions aren't necessarily intuitive, and the English instructions for using the rice cooker are translated (sometimes badly) from Korean. There are many excruciatingly complicated aspects to the rice cooking that you can control, but for most people, simply pressing the "cook" button will work just fine. It in no way interferes with the functionality of the rice cooker, nor the event results, though.Also, in a perfect world, I'd have loved for this rice cooker to have a steamer basket. Unfortunately, you'll have to but one of these on your own, if you're interested in doing any streaming, and to my knowledge, the company does not offer one for sale. Since these things aren't really "standard" sizes, a silicone one might be the best option.Overall though, speaking as an Asian person who takes rice very seriously, I think this is a great rice cooker for the money, especially if you keep a busy schedule and will utilize all the timing functions.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 9, 2018
author: A
title: Reliable for multigrain rice, high quality with a few quibbles
body: My 12 year old Zojirushi neurofuzzy died and I bought this Cuckoo rice cooker to replace it in January 2018.  It's still working great being used almost daily.  Compared to the Zoji, this one doesn't have the steamed ricewater overflow from the top vent the way my old Zoji did, and also does a better job of cooking japgokbap (mixed grain mix to regular rice ratio of 1:3) --- I do a 30 minute soak of the mixed grains prior to popping into the cooker, and it turns out to be perfectly done each time on the mixed brown rice setting.  If you have had Korean food at a restaurant and are wondering what that slightly purple rice with other things is, that's japgokbap, and you can buy bags of the mixed grains to make your white rice a healthier/more fibrous option.The parts and fit are high quality - nothing creaks or is out of alignment and it doesn't feel cheap.  It's made in Korea which may be important to some because a lot of Zoji are now made in China with questionable quality.The couple of important drawbacks that I find annoying:The black pan does not have any handles or any structure to prevent movement, so mixing the rice causes the pan to rotate/move around the base which can scratch the finish of the pan.  The Zoji I had had handles which prevented this from happening.Because it's a smart rice cooker, cleaning it requires cleaning multiple small parts (the vent tube + cover, the water collector at the back, the removable top lid, etc) that take 10 minutes longer than a cheap no-setting one-tab rice cooker (with just a lid and a pan).  When you're busy it can be annoying especially if you cook rice every day.Otherwise I am very pleased with my purchase.  I would consider buying one of the more expensive pressure-cooker Cuckoos in the future, but for now this meets my family's needs.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 3, 2018
author: anonymous
title: Love it. Perfect Rice Every Time.
body: I had a really cheaply made crappy rice cooker a long time ago that was disappointing. We cook a lot of rice, though, and it's just not the same on the stove, so I read a ton of reviews and decided to give this little Cuckoo a try. We LOVE it. So easy, keeps it warm, makes perfect rice every single time. I've made 3 different styles of rice in it so far & all came out perfect. The removable plate & steamer thing make it SO easy to clean too.  I'm so happy with this purchase.**4 years later** Still one of the best purchases ive ever made. Use it all the time. still in great condition

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 27, 2018
author: Dr. Fishopolis
title: Fantastic rice cooker
body: This is an excellent rice cooker that will cook your rice to perfection every time. It has multiple different settings for white/brown/mixed rice, steaming, slow cooking, etc. Also very helpful for the American model that it has dual English/Korean buttons. For new users worried about negative reviews of starchy liquid 'gunk' being left behind on your kitchen counter: I had the same problem the first couple times I used this rice cooker, but it seems to resolve itself after a few uses and may just need a breaking in.I actually had this exact same rice cooker model when I lived in South Korea for three years, and I loved it so much I instantly bought it upon moving back to the States. Gave my old one to the in-laws, and it is still going strong in their kitchen 5 years after the purchase!

title: It makes so much more than just rice.
body: First off, I have made more than just rice in this thing.  I will say the English directions...suck. Knowing a little Korean has saved me lots of frustrations with this product.  Once you get the hang of it, you will be making sticky rice (which is does beautifully), Juk doesn't take nearly as long to make as it does on the stove nor does it require constant attention.  I have even made one pot meals in this thing.  Add some calrose rice, a can of stewed tomatoes (or succotash if you can find it), some nice sausages, and some frozen okra, put it on steam and viola, you got a cheap easy and quick meal in just 30 minutes.  I have fell in love with this rice cooker and have used it to do so much, this was very well worth the price tag for sure.

The rice cooker is small and I think their seal is not tight enough to make rice soft and easy chew. I would not recommend this product to others.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2018
author: Amazon Customer
title: Just okay
body: I was looking for a replacement for my Zojirushi NS-ZCC10 and purchased this Cuckoo. I ran this side-by-side to compare its performance.The Cuckoo wins on the following points:* Cooking time is shorter by like 8 mins* Rice is ever so slightly better both in the turbo and normal modes, although it's so slight I can barely tell if it's better or the same.The Cuckoo loses on the following points:* The base was not even so it was slightly wobbly. Not a good impression of quality. I couldn't live with that wobble so I decided to return it for this reason.* The lid was overly forceful. If I press the button, the lid swings open so forcefully that the entire unit lifts itself up. It's quite annoying. The Zoji's lid opens up quite gracefully.* The buttons are hard to press. At first I thought it was defective because the buttons weren't responding when trying to set the clock. I had to really dig my fingers in to activate the buttons holding the unit with both hands (otherwise the unit would just move as I'm pressing the button)* The line markings on the inner pan are indentations only (hard to read) and only on one side (hard to level the pot to line up the water level to the markings). The Zoji has white markings and on opposite sides of the pot.* No handles on the inner pan. The Zoji has it and it's easier to hold.* The spatula holder is not as good as the one on the Zoji* There is a condensation cup in the back where you have to empty every now and then. The Zoji does not need one.So overall, it does cook rice slightly quicker and a hair better than the Zoji. However, I think the Zoji is the better rice cooker.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2018
author: Amish Princess
title: We love that the vent and "cover plate" can be detached ...
body: Had this for several months.  We love that the vent and "cover plate" can be detached and cleaned.  Our last rice cooker's "cover plate" did not remove and would get gross between the cover plate and lid.It has many cooking options for steaming a variety of other foods (standard with most cookers), and the instructions say you can control the sticky and fluffiness of the rice (unusual for cheap cookers).  However, we have not tried these cooking options (other than cooking standard white rice) to give a proper review.Only wish for this rice cooker is for a retractable cord.UPDATE: the little white plastic "drip catch" in the back broke when we tried to remove it to clean it out (after every use per instructions but, never looked dirty).  I would be docking .5 stars for that since it was silly for it to break so quickly but, we do not really think that was a very useful feature anyway.  I give 4.5 stars instead of the 5.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2018
author: SC
title: Good for single person who also has large groups of at their house
body: I got got this for about $110 here on Amazon. I saw it at a Korean grocery store for $200 so I'm glad I bought it here. I can keep my rice in it for a week and its still good though it starts to yellow by about the 3rd day.It makes just one cup of (uncooked) rice very nicely as well, but you need to estimate the water level since the first water line starts at 2 cups. This size allows me to make small portions for just myself and also cook more for larger parties.Make sure that when you buy a rice cooker, you check whether the number of cups is cooked or uncooked. Asian rice cookers usually lists the capacity as uncooked cups of rice and other rice cookers are generally cooked cups of rice. So an 8 cup capacity rice cookers can be very different sizes depending on whether it is cooked or uncooked.

title: The coating is great on the pot because it makes washing so easy
body: This cooker is so worth buying. It cooks much faster than my previous cooker and the rice texture comes out just right. I use it primarily to cook rice. I tried cooking congee once but it took a lot longer so I just use pressure cooker for congee. I don't mind using only the rice function though as it is small and elegant on my counter. The coating is great on the pot because it makes washing so easy. I can leave the rice in for 24 hours and the pot is still a breeze to wash. I really recommend this cooker to everyone who wants a reliable rice cooker. Don't leave the rice in longer than 24 hours as I left it in for 36 hours and it got slightly smelly. Good enough tho!

body: This is a very nice and easy to use rice cooker. When making basmati rice I use an equal portion or ratio of water and rice.  For example, I cooked 2 cups of dry basmati rice with 2 cups of water and selected the glutaneous rice feature and also selectec turbo. The rice turned out like it should, seperated and fluffy.  There was no biuld up on the rice cooker lid plate. I also did not was the rice. When I follow the basmati rice packaging water to rice ratio it does not turn out right. date: Reviewed in the United States on October 23, 2016
author: Joe
title: Wow! Not can match this appliance!
body: A few years ago a bought a Haan floor steamer. When I got that, it was the first South Korean appliance that I had ever had experience with. Everything about it was built like a tank and had the best components. The quality of the steel and plastics and even the sheathing for the wires was top notch. It's never let me down. When I started looking into a rice cooker I noticed this brand and had seen it in the Korean market where I buy my groceries. But until I started looking for this type of appliance, I did not know that this brand was the top selling brand in Korea. I am an American and proud of my country, and until I had this appliance in my hand and saw how it was put together and the fit and finish of it, I did not understand why it seemed like its price was high in comparison to other fairly high reviewed rice cookers by other manufacturers like Panasonic or Roval or GE. But now that I see it and tested it out and saw how it was put together, I can say that nobody holds a candle to So Korean engineers and manufacturing quality control. This thing is stunning! If America could make something of this quality and at this price, we would have every right to crow about it. But sadly, we don't. Literally, this thing is a Mercedes or an Audi vs a late 70's Chevy. I am sad that we no longer have the skills and standards to produce objects of such quality as this. I really wish that we did, because we would have every right to boast if we did. If you order this, just look at it. Look at it under scrutiny. Look at the inside lining of it, even the bottom of it, areas. It ordinarily looked at. It's a frickin' work of art! I would easily pay 75% more for it after seeing it in my hands. I am even thinking of checking out a Kia now that I've seen this. No joke, I know that you will be impressed if you but this. I think that this should be held up as a standard for the rest of the appliance world to look at what could be achieved and presented to customers. Unless there is some bizarre fluke, I am fairly certain that you will look this thing over in silence and feel a curiosity about what else could be coming from South Korea. I know I am going to start looking into it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2016
author: GMarie
title: I love my rice, and this rice cooker!
body: Love it! Keeps rice warm and fresh at least 3-4 days if you can keep rice around that long. Good size for small family.  Multiple settings for different types of rice. Easy to use. I make brown and white rice regularly, and they come out perfect every time. I use measurements of: white rice, 1:1 cup of water, rice:water, brown rice, 1:1.5 cups water. Very cute design and doesn't take up too much counter space. It tells you how long since you last opened it and how long its been done cooking. It's easy to clean, especially the top and vent that gets so much build up from the steam. When its unplugged, it displays the time.  The little slot on the side holds the rice spoon for easy access. I got a smaller cuckoo at my local korean market for $30 more, so this was a great deal! This was a must have for my little family.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 13, 2016
author: iDealShopper
title: Beats my Zojirushi $426 Induction Rice Cooker. Cuckoo is a brand I have switch to and will never look at back Zojirushi again.
body: Beats my Zojirushi Induction Rice Cooker that I paid $426, that only lasted me 2 yrs. before the non-stick coating on the pot started to come off. It was such a big disappointment, not only because I spent $426 but because is no longer made in Japan with Japanese parts, instead they switch manufacturing to China. When I bought my Zojirushi Induction Rice Cooker, I didn't think they would of switch from Japan to China, since I own a 1982 Zojirushi Rice Cooker that works great still. Zojirushi is now a brand that I no longer consider High-End anymore.There are lots of different brand on the market today for a rice cooker. They range from $15 - $800. Since I no longer consider Zojirushi as a brand I want to buy anymore, I was debating between the Cuckoo & the Cuchen rice cookers. Those are the top brands of rice cooker in Korea. I went with the Cuckoo CR-0631F because since I have never used a Korean Rice cooker before I wasn't sure what the outcome would be.To my surprise, this Cuckoo CR-0631F give the one of the best rice cooking results since my Zojirushi Induction Rice cooker that I replace it with. The non-stick coating on this Cuckoo CR-0631F is different that I have seen. It has a metallic bronze color, yet it repels water very well. It was very easy to clean this pot, just using soapy warm water and a soft sponge, no scrubbing needed at all.The only thing that I need to get use to with this Cuckoo CR-0631F rice cooker is the terms they use for white rice, it's called GLUTINOUS. One of the best features that I really like about this Cuckoo CR-0631F is the KEEP WARM, my rice stays warm for 72 hrs before it starts to look a little yellowish. Now that was just a test to see how long it can keep warm. I usually only make 2 DRY CUP (4 total cups of cooked rice) and finish it within 1 to 1 1/2 hrs.This is one of the BEST rice cook money can buy and it's a brand that I have finally replace the old trusted Zojirushi (use to be made in Japan, now it's all made in China with low quality materials). I have had this Cuckoo CR-0631F for about 6 whole months before writing this review. Enjoy my video of this Cuckoo CR-0631F rice cooker.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2014
author: Little Dorrit
title: Makes some Terrific Rice!
body: This is our first rice cooker and it has been a terrific introduction!  I was concerned about the lack of good instructions and almost returned it, but I am so glad I didn't.  If what you want is rice cooked to perfection then don't worry about all the settings for other things and just wash and rinse your rice well, put it in the cooker with the recommended amount of water on the package and let it sit for 30 minutes, plug it in, hit turbo cook on the top right and after it beeps about 20 minutes later unplug it and let it sit for 10 minutes then fluff it with the paddle (wetting it slightly helps keep it from sticking to the rice) and you will have perfect rice.  Especially if you used some Tamaki Gold or Tamaki Haiga.

title: Little cooker, best rice.
body: Omg! Best find ever got this I think during prime days so I got it a bit on sale, but it's worth the money! Perfect rice every time! Don't have to worry about timing or boiling over. Does the job for you. Don't have to think about portion math the cooker comes with its own cup and the pot has the water measurements! Often forget about cookers like this? No problem will keep the rice hot and fresh for many hours! Has a timer to help you keep track of its freshness. I have never heard it make noise, except while boiling. Beware when you cook turbo or the water level is higher than the portion usually wants some water with spit out of the steam hole. Just cover with paper towels and keep cooking. It has never once stuck to the pot easy serving directly from the pot and very aromatic rice! Parts separate easily and can be cleaned before next use. Love this machine! It lives on our counter now it deserves to be used often.

body: A bit of a preamble -  lately I've gotten into the habit of not necessarily "meal prepping" but making a decent amount of food on Sundays. title: Perfect but may be too much
body: Let me preface this with I eat a lot of rice. I also have worked in a Japanese restaurants. I've been around these cookers a lot.Pending on how much rice you make or how many people you're cooking for. You may want a smaller rice cooker. I generally cook at least four cups or more at a time saving the extra to make something else later or meal prep for the week.For value I'd say it's great, this performs as well as some of the one's I've used for restaurants and it's pretty simple until you get to some of the advanced settings.It's easy to clean and the color matches the pictures.It does still take a bit to make your rice so I generally start it first, granted I rinse and presoak my rice.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2025
author: JinSh
body: Took a while to get it. ??Glutinous?? function here isnt meant for ??glutinous rice??. Because there is no ??regular rice?? button so the button for ??glutinous?? is really for regular rice.So far, I??ve cooked couscous (glutinous function) and it came out perfect.Today, I made Hainanese chicken rice. 6 uncooked cups of rice yield a full pot (cooked rice to the brim - see photo).  I used hot broth instead of water when I cook the chicken rice. No burnt base. No spill over . Rice turned out good!The spout does emit a lot of steam so I used a cereal box (cut open to shape like a cone shape hat) to cover it as it not only blocks the steam from going everywhere under my countertop, it also absorb the steam.I wish it comes w a steam basket so you can use the steam function.For those comments who complained and had videos showing the liquid spewing everywhere is BECAUSE THEY ONLY COOK ONE CUP OF RICE. It will happen to ANY rice cooker if you only cook 1 cup or very small amount of rice. Suggestion if you are alone or don??t need to cook for so many diners, is to cook more and then save the leftovers in the fridge. Leftovers overnight rice makes great fried rice.The rice cooker takes up little space (compared to many others I??ve shopped) for a 6 cup rice cooker! Very good if you have limited counter top space.The machine rocks back when you press the open button- whether with or without food in the pot. See video.My biggest complain is the markings inside the pot are very hard to read. I need to put on glasses to use this rice cooker! I hope they will change the markings to something more prominent like other competitive brands.The instructions suggests not to rinse the rice in this pot. I guess it saves the teflon from overuse.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 2, 2024
author: Jamazon
title: Gorgeous and Cute - Math is Bad
body: I bought this, opened it up to inspect, and carefully re-packaged to return.  Hear me out.  The rice cooker is very cute, the colors pop, looks like a hot little Alfa Romeo sports car.  The red is a beautiful McLaren sports car speckled sparkly red.  It's very nice.  It's a well respected brand name.  Many of my friends have $200+ Cuckoo rice cookers, for years.But the math doesn't work.  Math never lies.  The 6 cup hash mark, as others have said is a bit hard to see, is 3/4's way up from the bottom. Rice doubles in size when cooked.  There is no way this will make 12 cups of cooked rice as stated.  I think this can make maybe 7 cups of cooked rice.  Should take care of a family of 4-5 for a dinner.  If this size works for you, then I would recommend it.  For the price, the looks, brand quality, I just wish it was a bit bigger.  Our family of 3, we use the InstaPot.  We wanted a convenient rice cooker for when we have 10+ guests.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 2, 2024
author: Renee Aubuchon
title: An acceptable rick cooker
body: I was curious about rice cookers, and this one was on sale, so I bought it.  While some families have used rice cookers for generations, they were new to me.  I had always made rice in a pan in on the stove and thought- well, that's as good as it gets.  Well- it's not.  I have found that it is easier to cook rice with a rice cooker, and the rice tastes better.  Somehow it is tender and yet has texture.  From now on I will be using a rice cooker to cook rice.One of the things I don't like about this rice cooker, which was also pointed out in a review of rice cookers, is that the measurement indications on the inside of the pot cannot be read.  The pan is dark and the measurement indicators are dark, and it would darned near take a flashlight to be able to read them.  I bought a rice cooker cookbook that gives the appropriate measurements for all kinds of rice, and will be using that book to guide me.The other thing I didn't like is that there is no steamer basket for this rice cooker.  A rice cooker that comes highly recommended and has a steamer basket also costs more than twice what this one does.If you are looking for a rice cooker that is not very expensive and does a good job of cooking various kinds of rice, this rice cooker is an acceptable choice.

date: Reviewed in Canada on September 13, 2024
author: brooke18
title: Not non-stick!
body: The whole point of a rice cooker is to make it effortless to cook rice. Not if you have to soak the pot for several hours, scrape the thick layer of rice stuck to it, and spend a good half hour scrubbing it (with a scrubber meant for non-stick cookware, of course).There is no time countdown and it doesn't beep when it's done so if you're not watching it carefully, it will end but keep the rice warm. This is a terrible feature because all it does is make the rice overcooked and stick even worse to the bottom of the very not non-stick pot. When you cook white rice, it spews a sticky mess everywhere. I put a folded piece of paper towel over the steam vent, which allowed steam but captured the mess. But instead of going all over the place, it now dripped down the sides of the pot so I still had a mess to clean up.When you press the lid to open the cover, it pops open with great force, almost tipping the rice cooker over. The feet are not non-skid so you have to hold it with one hand while pressing the buttons as it moves around. The curved front where the buttons are makes it more difficult to press the buttons.Also, the manual is almost useless as it's written in broken English and a lot of it just doesn't make sense. Why not label it white rice instead of glutenous? I had to research what all these terms like GABA meant. Ridiculous. This rice cooker is not marketed for western culture, obviously.The buttons don't always respond. Have to insert pot before plugging it in. If you plug it in first, it knows there is no pot and a symbol appears on the screen and you can't get rid of it. Even after you put the pot in, it won't show the menu settings (even if you press Cancel) so you have to unplug it and plug it back in. The menu defaults to whatever you last cooked so if you're now cooking a different type of rice, you have to press Cancel. But it doesn't work so you press it again. Then unplug it, try again. Then it seems to work if you press and HOLD the Cancel button.The screen is terrible font and dim so very hard to read. Helps to use a flashlight. Took forever to change the time and once I did, I couldn't adjust it (when I realized it was off by a few minutes). You can't shut it off completely, so the battery is just going to run down as it's on 24/7.If the pot was actually non-stick and took a few minutes to clean, I would put up with the many other issues. I just threw it out because it's not even fit to give to charity! Wouldn't want to inflict this piece of garbage on anyone. What a waste of $130

body: If you're considering the Tiger units sold at the warehouse clubs, and they seem to be the main competition for this Cuckoo, the winner is clear.  The Cuckoo out performs the Tiger in every  category, the algorithmic cooking phases, the useability, the top quality rice that this machine makes, the durability and usefulness of the components, the design that favors easy clean up especially puts the Tiger to shame.  Now, if you want a Zojirushi level experience, don't pretend that you can save 90% by buying a Cuckoo and get the same kind of product.  But the Cuckoo is a whole lot closer to the Zojirushi than the Tiger could ever hope to be.  I know I will be satisfied for life with this Cuckoo, unless I outlive it.

author: StaceyR
title: Makes very good rice if you already know what you??re doing
body: My 20-year-old $20 rice cooker from Target was very simple: add rice, water, push button - made fairly good rice but didn??t use often.  The last few batches stuck to the bottom and I decided to replace.  I chose this one based on price, reviews, size and because it??s kinda cute.  Since I??m cooking for two and don??t eat rice that often I didn??t want to spend $200-$300.This Cuckoo offers options for several different types of rice.  Unfortunately I mostly eat basmati and there was no setting nor instructions for that.  I found the instructions somewhat confusing in general and had to do lots of research on how to adapt for basmati.  Fortunately I found a YouTube (FamiLee Living) that made me realize I was over-complicating the setup and a website (Tea for Turmeric) explaining how to cook basmati in a rice cooker.Thanks to FamiLee Living and the Tea for Turmeric recipe the Cuckoo makes really delicious basmati rice easily.  It??s so good I??ll be eating a lot more rice in the future.So I rate it 5 for price and cooking quality but 1 for instructions - at least for beginners.

I??ve had this rice cooker for about a year now and have noticed the quality of the cooked rice is not as good as it was previously or compared to better units I??ve owned.I finally gave up and decided to spend the money and get a better rice cooker.3 stars.

It can be useful when we are busy. When we have guests over it makes a fair amount of rice so it's not much of a hassle. I would recommend to small families or those who are just starting out to live independently as a small family.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2024
author: Zane Austin
title: Great bang for buck.
body: This was the first actual rice cooker I've purchased (other than a $20 Walmart brand cooker), had mine for over a year now and it's great.I genuinely think this is one of the best bang for buck rice cookers out there.  My friend has a Zujiroshi, and while it's better, it's not $150 more better.When I bought this it was highly rated and I can see why.  Makes great rice, easy to setup and use, rice doesn't stick to inner pot which also makes it easy to clean, and it doesn't burn the rice at the bottom of the pot if you leave it on warm like other budget cookers or instapot alternatives.Highly recommend.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2024

This alien helmet piece of machinery is absolutely perfect. This will absolutely be my go to house warming gift for any of my friends and family.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2024
author: Avdi Grimm
title: Practically perfect
body: It's hard to think of what could improve this cooker. It makes great rice. It has a good UI. It can make rice later, on a timer. It keeps rice warm indefinitely, and shows you how many hours old the rice is. It is very easy to clean - the removable top plate is a nice touch. It also makes a great steamer. It's cheaper than the equivalent Zojirushi.Someday I might pay more for inductive or pressure cooking features, but for a basic cooker it's hard to think how this unit could be improved on.

The first time I cooked in it, it heated right up and started cooking. On about the 3rd time, it takes about 20 minutes to heat up and another 20 to cook.  When it cooks, it cooks but I didn't like that it did not come with instructions

body: Cooks rice good. Easy to use and easy to clean. My only complain is, you can't clean actual cooker. I do not think main cooker itself is water proof so I didn't try to wash it off.I used my first one for 3 years or so till I throw it out and buy new one. Yes, 1st one was still working good but it was getting dirty. Considering cost of this cooker, using it for 2-3 years is still good deal. I really wish they made inside of cooker more cleanable but oh well.. for the price, I really can't complain.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2024
author: Meda
title: Update
body: Update: less than a year old and the paint is chopping off near the steam vent. I only ever wipe down this outside lid area with a damp cloth. Nothing abrasive.I'll be honest, I'm not terribly impressed with this. Maybe because I didn't need something fancy? My 10 yr old roma rice cooker was maybe 1/3 the price, speedier, and not so complicated.  This thing is more convoluted than it needs to be. I can't even find the serial number to register it. The two pots of rice ive made so far have spewed water out of the steam cap. The first go I thought too much water. The second time I cut back the water by about half a cup to abour 2.5c and still it spewed out. Its highly aggravating that it slides all over the counter. I ended up stopping in a home improvement store and getting rubber no skid pads and cutting them down. The buttons are hard to read. Yah, not impressed.I'll give it another couple tries then decide if it's worth keeping.

body: I received this rice cooker as a gift from a relative. I made sure to read the instruction booklet and remove and wash all items before using it. It was so easy to use and faster than I expected. The rice was fluffy, delicious, and easy to pick up with chopsticks. Definitely recommend if you??re on a budget but want good rice. I wouldn??t recommend letting the rice go past day 3 though. ????

body: The dumbest purchase I have made in years. I've owned it three months, I've never managed to make rice with it. Every single time, I lose patience thumbing through it's bizarre controls, flipping through the very poorly written manual, and make rice in a pot. Rice is pretty easy, this device is not.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 17, 2023
author: Karen
title: Affordable and Works Great
body: I have been needing a rice cooker for a while now, the cheap one I bought didn't make rice the right way. I didn't gave over $100+ in my budget for the more expensive ones, and was glad to see this at the price. It did take a bit to get the clock set up, but I had hit a different button on accident. My rice comes out perfect! Perfect stickiness, not dry or mushy, cooked to perfection. I had made rice in it for lunch and had left what was left on warm and ate the rest for dinner and the rice was still a great consistency. I love that it's easy to use as well and doesn't get super hot on the exterior.Being half korean rice is a huge staple in my diet, and I have had this for over a month and it still produces great rice. Easy to clean rice pot as well. I don't go with the lines for water to rice ratio, I do the knuckle test where it reaches the top of your third knuckle and I let it soak a few hours before cooking. Great rice every time and I'm super happy with this purchase!

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2023
author: Lala
title: How did I ever manage without this Kitchen Gem?
body: Compact, cute and worry free rice cooker is a must in every kitchen. Perfect rice every time. The shape and color just invites usage and care of the machine. It will make a great gift for young people starting out on their own. No accidental burning or ill prepared rice. A natural disaster displaced our family for a whole year.  I had very little cooking space. A friend mentioned a rice cooker and I couldn??t resist when I saw this futuristic red capsule-like cooker.  The lithium powered clock works even when the machine isn??t plugged in. There is much more it can do, but having perfect rice keeps it busy. I love it!

Haha love the purchase, but ready for an upgrade to have an appliance match our kitchen! But this is a great rice cooker.

author: Jonathan
title: Great product. Terrible customer service
body: Love this rice cooker. Trust the measurements on the bowl. I added way too much water at first. Use steel cut oats and porridge setting on timer to wake up to fully cooked oatmeal! Set the time for when you want it to be READY.  Not for when you want to start cooling.Machine figures it out itself. Just get water ratio correct and let it take it from there.If brown rice is parboiled (most are) just use glutinous setting ( this setting does not make rice too stodgy, despite how it sounds).The inner pot bent. Was not dropped so must have been easy to do. Calle Canadian Customer service. They answered "yeah". Confused I asked "is this (company)" they responded "what's it about". It was so rude it was almost funny. Anyway. They said it was my fault and to call Amazon.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 7, 2023
author: L
title: Rice cooker started to burn rice, and melted on last use.
body: I've been using this rice cooker for less than a year total, approx 1-2 times a week for 2 cups of rice. For the majority of the time, the rice has been great and perfectly textured.This past week the rice started getting hard/burnt on the bottom. I looked at all my settings and I had it as t=0, b=6, and h=0 according to the manuals online. I set b=0 and b=6, which should only impact the soaking time before the cooking.This last time I used it the rice cooker had difficulty opening, and it turns out the rice cooker was completely melted. I had to break some of the plastic just to take the lid out. It looks like some electrical or mechanical failure, as I don't think any rice cooker should be getting hot enough to melt plastic normally. Super disappointing!

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2023
author: Riza

body: This is my favorite brand for Rice cookers and I was glad I was able to save when I got a discount. This is really a great rice cooker! Rice is soft and stays heated for many days without getting burned.

author: Alicia Barrera
title: A game changer
body: So, I bought this rice cooker one night when I was really drunk because I was watching the price and finally saw it at a price I was willing to pay. I am sooo glad I did! Sometimes I only have two hours in between school and work and don??t have much time to wait on rice to cook for an hour. It has a turbo cook option which can take about 36 minutes and even has a nice warming function!It was a really good buy, because you??re not just limited to cooking rice in it. There??s many other options for cooking and I??m really impressed with this rice cooker. You won??t regret purchasing.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2023
author: App
title: Very versatile
body: I did a bit of research before buying this rice cooker and I'm glad I purchased this one. It was highly rated. And I find it is very versatile. I've only had it a short while but I've made regular white rice, oatmeal, polenta, quinoa, and sticky rice (a bit different than regular rice but I'm finding I like it better. It has a different consistency and taste.) Everything I've made turned out great the first time. I did go online and found a number of recipes/instructions on how to make things in a rice cooker and they turned out well.The only thing that I found problematic were the instructions. They are a bit confusing. That's why I went online for all the different recipes. I'm finding that I've only scratched the surface of what this rice cooker can do.I do clean the insides after each use and it's not very difficult. The instructions say not cleaning it out can affect the taste of whatever you make next. Lastly, be sure to wipe out the inner seal on the bottom of the cover because it collects water.I really like this rice cooker because it is well made and I think it will last a long time with heavy usage. It has a lot of bang for the buck. I highly recommend.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2023
author: Artie Slaughter
title: This is not a great choice if your vision sucks and/or you're usually cooking for one
body: tl;dr: I'd spend the extra money on a Zojirushi.This does everything you could want it to do, HOWEVER: the pot's measurement demarcations are merely embossed into the dark-colored nonstick surface, making them difficult to read. Additionally, the markings START at two cups of dry rice, an amount nobody cooking for one could possibly need. If you have a big family and you eat a ton of rice, this is fine, so long as you don't care about squinting to divine the measurements, and the price point of this unit is most bargainous. But compare to the Zojirushis, which have white measurement markings you could read from outer space, and whose measurements start at half a cup of dry rice??to me this is well worth spending more. This Cuckoo is absolutely not a bad unit, it's just these little details that make it terrible for me. To say an additional something nice about it: the red perfectly matches my coffee maker, and together they look super Pinterest-y on my counter. I will miss that.

date: Reviewed in Canada on February 23, 2023
author: Phil from the Peg
title: Meh
body: After searching the internet for some recipes??. I shouldn??t have to do that. I mean, this is the first food appliance I have ever bought that didn??t have simple recipes.The first attempt we had to add more water and cook for an additional 15 minutes. An hour for rice??.. ?!

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2023
author: Annie Oakley
title: Stop searching rice cookers??buy a Cuckoo!
body: Years ago purchased a rice cooker crockpot and it was no better than a saucepan.  I??ve been researching rice cookers for a while, and saw a lot of the more expensive ones for resale on eBay.  The feedback sold me on this one and the price was reasonable.Note, the measuring cup isn??t 8 oz.  Instead of measuring rice to water ratio, learned from a Japanese chef always rinse rice until the water runs clear.  After placing rice in pot cover with water up to first index finger knuckle (above rice).  This method worked very well in the Cuckoo.

But I don??t hate it enough to send back.Pros:* Nice compact machine* Cooks rice just about perfectly (gaba rice included)* Lots of different cooking optionsCons:*Inside measurements are nearly impossible to make out* No outside spoon rest

body: I like to eat rice, I saw someone introduced to buy, I used half a month, I don't like his design, it cooked rice is not as good as they say.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2023
author: The Zel
title: As good as the Zojirishi for a fraction of the price
body: I really wanted to buy the Zojirishi rice cooker but the 200+ price tag was off putting. So we took a chance on this cooker and my wife and I have been so happy. The rice cooks amazing soft fluffy white rice that isn't mushy. I am even eating more rice because it's that darn good. My wife is the big ricerl eater in the house and she loves it too. The rice pot is thick and sturdy not flimsy garbage. And the breakdown for cleaning is super easy. It has the fuzzy logic of the higher price models too. We have ours dialed it perfectly for our taste and everytime the rice comes out amazing. And my wife loves it because she has warm rice ready for her in the morning. If you are in the market for a rice cooker buy this you won't be disappointed. I just wish they had other colors available for this model, that's literally my only gripe about it.

body: WE have gone through many rice cookers in the past.  The inner pot is the issue.  After time the inner pot peels.  I was told when it starts peeling it is toxic and should not be used. body: After a long research I got myself a cuckoo rice cooker in Germany. Now my parents liked my rice and therefore I offered this nice cuckoo to them (nearly the same model).My criteria were:- not too large, I would rather cook twice in one evening rather than having a huge rice cooker- no glas lid, had one and was a mess to clean each time- amount of modes (glutinous, porridge....)- quality of finished rice- not too expensive, we only eat rice from time to time and don't need a cool hi-tech cookerAlthough I don't speak Korean, the buttons are labelled as well in English - no problems to use the menu. date: Reviewed in the United States on December 5, 2022
author: Ms. Qui??ones
title: Great Buy
body: I had three videos but apparently you can??t upload more than one. Lol This a great rice cooker. I??ve made white jasmine rice, mixed grain rice and glutinous white rice with it and they all turned out great!! The black rice was a little under, it cooked better in the instant pot. But other than that all the different rice turned out amazing. It??s super easy to clean, just make sure you dry the inside of the rubber top piece. Like any of these cooking machines, make sure you clean ALL the parts that need cleaning. I REALLY love the ??set-it and finish whatever you have to do?? ability. Turbo mode is awesome!!.PSA: Although it says the different parts are dishwasher safe, in order not to erode the nonstick layer on the inner pot, or compromise the integrity of the rubber seals, I recommend hand washing ALL parts. It cleans so easily, and if you have a scrubby brush for metal straw, it makes it super easy to clean the steam spout and condensation collection tray.Update: I??ve used the rice cooker MANY times (almost daily) and with different rice varieties (Haven??t made Puerto Rican, Caribbean or Basmati rice yet). I have to say, overall I??m SUPER PLEASED with my purchase. It really does cook rice to perfection. For someone who didn??t see the point of rice cookers (I was like??I have hands and a pot) I  TOTALLY get it now. Doesn??t hurt that it??s  aesthetically pleasing, with its sleek design and easily maintained quality. If you??re on the fence????.GET IT!! You won??t regret it. Also I??m almost 100% sure that the ??problem?? on some reviews, with starchy water spewing from the steam spout??..is user error!! Wash your rice!! And CLEAN YOUR MACHINE after EVERY use!!

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2022
author: Jeffrey Finkelstein
title: Great rice every time, but instructions are confusing
body: The instructions are a bit difficult to understand, but once you realize the included measuring cup is 1/2 cup and the measurements inside the unit for water are measured in cups, it becomes easier to understand. It uses a 2:1 water to rice ratio. Simple.To use it add in the rice (1/2 cup per person works with my family), pour in the water, select the rice type using the ??menu?? button (glutonous is short white rice and mixed is long grain rice), then press ??cook??. It takes about 60 mins for 2 cups of rice for 4 people.Fast? No. Simple? Debatable. Perfect rice every time? Yes. It comes out a bit sticky, but plump and tasty. I have not tried the other options as I only plan to use it for rice.It??s easy to clean as well but be sure use the soft side of a sponge or a soft cloth.Note: The coating of the interior of the bowl is very thin. Even a tiny scratch will cause it to peel away after a few uses rendering it unusable. We had to throw it out once that happened. Exercise caution when cleaning and do not put anything metal inside it even when it is in the sink to soak as it can easily damaging it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 7, 2022
author: Dr. Sean Henry
title: Very nice nice entry-level Micon rice cooker, but ...
body: I eat a lot of rice, steel-cut oatmeal, and grits (a.k.a. polenta). I had cooked these successfully up until now in my beloved 8-quart Zavor multi-cooker, but using such a big appliance is overkill to cook just a cup of dry rice or grains for a few servings in a single household. I chose this Cuckoo Micom (micro-computerized) rice cooker for its reputation and brand loyalty, great reviews (on CNN), size and multiple cooking functions. Plus it really looks cute on my counter!This 6-cup unit is perfect for me and doesn't take up too much space on my counter. It works well and I like the way it cooks rice, but the instruction manual with bad English translations isn't always helpful. And exactly how user-friendly is the menu setting named "glutinous" for the USA market?  I had to search the internet for the best ways to cook my favorite rices, grains, and porridges in this device, but there is an active Cuckoo community out there with answers, and things are pretty simple.Like other reviewers have noted, there is nothing on the bottom of this very light unit to keep it from easily sliding across your countertop like a figure-skater. You may have to hold the unit still with your extra hand while you press menu buttons. There ought to be skid-proof rubber "feet" on the base of this unit to prevent that, as is the case with many other small kitchen appliances.I never got this cooker's clock setting feature to work correctly because the "Min/MyMode" button never changed over from "My Mode" to let me set the clock minutes, even after holding down the button for several seconds following the instructions. Fortunately, the minutes on the clock upon unboxing were only 2 minutes off from my local clocktime.If you are older, like me, make sure to wear your reading glasses and turn on the overhead kitchen lights to operate this unit. The small digital screen has no backlighting and there is no light indicating that the rice cooker is even on, but you'll know in a couple of minutes when it starts to make a few cooking noises inside and lets off some steam and rice aroma.This is a very nice "entry-level" Micom rice cooker that looks great on the counter, doesn't take up a lot of space, has a lot of versatile cooking functions, and produces its advertised results. I recommend it for those reasons. However, I am already upgrading to a pricier Zojirushi pressure induction heating rice cooker. Of course I (we) could all save money by cooking rice and grains the old fashioned way by sweating over the stove, but I love the convenience of a "hands-off" rice cooker, plus it gives off less heat in my hot summer kitchen!

It packs a lot into it, from the heavy duty non-stick pan to the Micom (micro computer), fuzzy logic cooking.That said, I had to get my reading glasses to read the display.  The display may be sharp enough but the color and lack of contrast make it difficult to read.  "Glutinous" was the choice shown on the display.  I pressed "Cook" and it showed on the display that it was working.  I couldn't hear anything to indicate that it was working.It took more than 30 minutes for 2 cups of rice but that's not unusual.  They were cooked nicely and there was no scorching or any other problems.Since it has the micro computer controlling the cooking, many options are available to make brown rice and blends just as easy.

title: BEWARE---YOU EAT TEFLON with your RICE---Cheapo Chinese manufacturer.
body: Within mere months, the cook pot's teflon-like coating started to bubble up, corrode, and lift off. Unfortunately (for me) I discovered after eating full pot of cooked rice!!!Reading other feedback, I see this is a common occurrence; just ONE MORE INSTANCE of Chinese manufacturers "ruining" good Korean designed products.Not only is the coating faulty...but this cooker is plastic---most of Korean made Cuckoos are aluminum. Seems the Chinese manufacturer chose to squeeze a few dollars (maybe even cents) more profit by not using Korean quality materials.And FINALLY...I was able to get refund through asurion warranty (thank you, Pastor Fritz)....now I see amazon is NOT OFFERING asurian coverage on this cooker!****RUN QUICKLY AWAY from this cheaply built inferior cooker that can actually poison you!!****Cuckoo makes many models in Korea still---they are much better in buildability, value, longevity....and safety.Since amazon "chooses" to hide country of origin...simply contact Cuckoo USA and they will verify which models are indeed Korean made.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 3, 2020
author: Go Hornets!!!!
title: What a wonderful machine!
body: The good points:  Solid quality material and construction.  Simple and easy to use.  Makes great tasting rice.  Looks good on the counter top.The bad points:  It slides easily on the counter top when pushing a button.  The translations from Korean to English in the users manual is often funny but confusing.The first thing I noticed when I removed the inner cooking pot was its heft.  It has some weight to it.  My older rice cooker had an inner pot made from very thin (and very lightweight) metal.  In fact, it was so thin that it would often burn the rice on the bottom.  I just used the portion that was not burnt.  This has not been an issue with the Cuckoo.  I have made regular long-grain rice, Japanese sticky rice, basmati rice and arborio rice.  Each has turned out just right.  I do wish this cooker had non-skid feet.  When I push one of the control buttons on the front of the machine, it will slide across the counter top.  I have to use two hands -- one to hold the cooker and the other to push the button.  Not a big deal, but non-skid feet would be a welcome addition.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2020
author: Vince
title: Hyundai Elantra of rice cooker.
body: Yeah right it's made in China, sure the pot coating doesn't seem to be rock solid. And this is taking nearly one hour to cook half load of rice. But there's that price. Me as a Korean I strongly approve this cooker as a perfect entry-level cooker. Been using it for nearly a year. And the pot coating had been scratched due to several mistakes(not using sponge side of dish sponge (!?), Putting metal utensils inside the pot... Etc etc...). However coating seemed to be quite sturdy enough. If y'all looking for the actual rock solid inner pot you guys gotta get the stuff w Xwall. But by that price point you probably would've gotten two of this one. Idk. It's the right price with right product. Rice quality is pretty good it gets cooked all nice and sticky(I prefer soggy rice over the typical rice coz It makes the rice dryness right after 12hrs.) Rice does get the yellowness + wateryness after 72hrs.(with botan rice, and this usually means that batch of rice went bad ) unless you're actually going to have the rice 24/7 (yeah really 24/7 have the rice all the time). This isn't the right product for you. But with simplicity and how compact it is. there is nothing better than this cooker. Don't be silly about what you got. You really do get what you paid for.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2019
author: Amazon Customer
title: made in china.
body: As a Korean, I eat rice every single day and I know Cuckoo is the best rice cooker brand out there. I've always been using this brand all my life, same goes for my mother. But I never knew there is a made-in-china version and that's why this was so cheap. I should have checked twice. This one does have a "turbo" cooking feature, which my old cuckoo usually had 10 cups of rice done for 20 minutes. But this one takes forever to "fast cook" 3 cups of rice. so what's the point of having a fast cooking feature when it's only like 4-5 minutes faster than regular time? (45-50 minutes??) It does what it's supposed to do, and keeps the rice fresh for a couple of days (the Korean made ones are usually good up to 90 hours- I tried) overall, it's ok for its price, but I would consider this product as a second machine, (in case I have to cook kalbi chim in my big cuckoo and have to make rice at the same time.. etc)

Maybe this is the differentiating factor.  Well, the rice does taste good.  (but not fabulous, either.)

author: A. Ziraphale
title: One stupid design flaw that keeps this from being a perfect rice cooker
body: As the title of my review suggests, there is one major and, honestly, stupid design flaw that keeps this rice cooker from being my all time fav: there is no place to put the rice paddle. Come on. That's so silly. It's a sleek, red, space-ship like design but you couldn't bother to design a holder or a place for the paddle? Seriously??? Even the cheap $11 aroma rice cooker I used to own had a little handle you could stick the paddle in. Come on cuckoo, this is ridiculous. Another minor design flaw that isn't as bad but is inconvenient is how hard the lid flips up -- the force of the lid flipping up, even with a full pot of rice, is so strong the whole rice cooker jerks backwards a little bit. So you have to keep your hand on the lid and then press the lid open, guiding the lid and not letting it just flip freely.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 30, 2018
author: Bloom W.
title: ... upgrade our cheaper rice cooker and it's been a great change. It cooks the rice evenly and QUICKLY
body: We've had this for a few months now after I decided to upgrade our cheaper rice cooker and it's been a great change. It cooks the rice evenly and QUICKLY. I was surprised how fast it gets the job done. We mostly just use the white or brown rice option but apparently you can also do mixed rice and more. We have used the steam cleaning option that seems to have done a good job. I like the timer and clear screen. The buttons are in both English and Korean. It keeps the rice fresh for us for about 2 days. After that, the leftovers have to be thrown out. I like that it's not loud, doesn't bubble or spill, and we can store it in our cabinet after use. It's actually really easy to clean the inside because the pot is light-weight and soft. We have never had a problem so far with the rice sticking to the pot. The alert to let you know the rice is done is just a little beep. I wish the instructions were more clear about rice:water ratio because the English translation isn't perfect. Also, it's better to do a minimum of 2 servings. We've tried 1 and the rice just doesn't cook well.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 10, 2018
author: adrian
title: Don??t expect fast cooking
body: I bought this particular rice cooker for the look primarily.  It is a beautiful addition to my kitchen.  My current rice cooker, an aroma brand stopped working after 2 years with it.  I figured since this one was over twice as much in price it was going to be amazing.  Now I may be wrong but I thought the purpose of a rice cooker was to save time?  The time rang for cooking just 1 cup of rice is about 35 minutes.  More rice increases the time to almost an hour. My previous  one cooked less than 10 minutes.  This one has a turbo mode but it recommends you presoak your rice before using. And even then it knocks the time down just a few minutes.  And on top of that when I came to check on the cooker the water vent had spewed droplets everywhere!!! So be prepared to lay a piece of paper over it!! I need to clean the outside of it immediately.  I don??t have too much experience with rice cookers so I don??t know if this is the norm.  But if this much vapor spurts with 1 cup of rice I can only imagine with a full pot.  I may go have to find my plain white 30 dollar rice cooker from before.  Not fancy but works way better

author: Nick
title: Cooking pot interior's coating started to peel after 4 uses
body: The cooking pot's interior coating started to peel after 4 uses. We've had three different rice cookers in our household for the last eleven years, and this normally doesn't occur until after 2-3 years of usage. Luckily, this occurred 2 weeks before the "return/refund" deadline. If not, we would've been stuck with a new rice cooker that could potential harm our family if we used it. After owning two rice cookers manufactured in South Korea, I believe this particular Cuckoo rice cooker is a hunk of junk, because it's made in China. Although this is a Korean brand, it's not made there. Make sure to look up the models online to see where they're manufactured. We'll be making sure our next one is made in South Korea. I would've gave it one star, but it does make good rice (before it defects).

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 25, 2025
author: Loring
title: When is a Cup not a Cup?
body: I??m giving this rice cooker 2 stars because while it works, the product title is incredibly misleading. It claims to be a ??10-cup?? rice cooker, which led me to believe it could handle 10 standard U.S. cups (8 oz each) of uncooked rice. In reality, the included measuring cup is only 6 oz, which is not a standard cup measurement. That means this cooker only holds about 7.5 standard cups of uncooked rice, not 10. I found this out when my rice cooker finished cooking and half of the rice (in the top) was dry and undercooked and rice had expanded into the steam ventNowhere in the title or main description is this difference clarified. You have to dig deep into the manual to find that they??re using ??rice cooker cups,?? not standard kitchen measurements. For anyone planning meals or bulk prepping, this discrepancy makes a big difference.Manufacturers need to be more transparent. If you're buying this thinking it can handle 10 U.S. cups of uncooked rice, it cannot. It??s frustrating and feels like a bait-and-switch.

date: Reviewed in Canada on September 29, 2024
author: James Parkes
title: The Rolls Royce of Rice Cookers
body: This rice cooker is an unbelievably capable piece of kitchen gadgetry, the brand recommended to me during a Korean cooking class I attended.Thing is, my buying this rice cooker is like employing a Michelin-starred Chef to make cheeseburgers at a backyard BBQ for a child's birthday party. I will probably never use this rice cooker to its full potential. It does everything, and it does it effortlessly. My first time using it, I wasn't entirely sure I was actually doing it correctly, so I think perhaps the documentation could better explain what you should expect to see when using it as a new owner. But that was me overcomplicating it. Just press the button you want and let it go to work, the results will be perfect.

author: Yen
title: Too many unnecessary features but lacks the more important ones
body: It can cook rice a little better than my instant pot but not the best result. I can cook better quality rice in a casserole on the stove and so much faster. The only problem with stove cooking is you have to check it from time to time and adjust the heat manually. Unfortunately for this rice cooker, there is no option for auto turn off after cooking. Markings on the pot is useless as it's hard to see. But even if it's marked with contrasting color I would not use it as it resulted to very soft rice. I always use equal ratio for rice, 1 cup water to 1 cup rice and the markings tend to have more water. The Set and preset options are very confusing and the manual doesn't really helps. I only use it for rice so it doesn't matter. I was just hoping for a better quality cooked rice and faster cooking time. I don't see any advantage over a $20 rice cooker that cooks the rice faster.

With the Zojirushi cooker we wanted rice almost nightly.  With the Cuckoo - meh - only once a week.  I get it - the Cuckoo is at least $100 less, but if the rice sucks and you have to throw it out - have you saved a dime?

author: H.L
title: Makes delicious rice
body: After doing many research about rice cookers, I??ve decided to give Cuckoo a try. However, I can only this product a 3 star. One reason is because the rice cooker came damaged. The clear container in the back that traps water when opening the lid is slightly cracked. Not sure if it??s just manufacture defects or it??s damaged through shipping. Another reason is the water level is so hard to read. The inner pot is in the color black.The words and numbers on the inner pot is black too, which make it hard to read when trying to measure the correct water level. Also, I feel like the buttons are not very responsive, especially the cancel button. Have to press many times for it to work.Now, let??s talk about the pros of this rice cooker. It has many modes to suit your needs. It also have a delay function of when to start cooking. Unlike other rice cookers, Cuckoo cooks the rice evenly. Also, the look of this rice cooker is very clean and modern.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 2, 2022
author: J Chin
title: dead after 8 months
body: This was our first fancy rice cooker. As a family of 5, we eat a lot of rice - 3 times a day. We had our last one for 10 years when it finally died. I decided to splurge on a fancy one, one that can do more than just cook rice. At first, it was great. The rice came out perfectly. We made white rice, brown rice, quinoa, porridge and it all came out perfectly. But just a week ago, it died, in the middle of cooking rice, it died. No more lights and because it's completely automated, there wasn't a way to trouble shoot. We followed the guide to see if it was user error but honestly, we didn't abuse it in any way. title: Manual sucks, missing key info, hard to figure out
body: The thing cooks efficiently, but the manual is missing key information in several respects and seems to require the user to know things like ratios. The buttons are not intuitive. While the English in the instructions is clear, the instructions manage to be singularly unhelpful. You'd think they would tell you how much rice and water to use in different modes to get to the desired quantity and style. But no. That would have been a step too far. I just wanted a multipurpose rice cooker - I wasn't trying to practice the scientific method. Once I figure out how to use it the hard way I am sure it will be fine. But it seems they were trying to do too much in one machine and missed key data points (give us a table please showing ratios, people) such that I found myself using Google to operate it. I should have got a simpler cheaper model. Waste of money.

body: The lid of this product was broken from the get go. I called customer service and ended up going through the following sequence:-Call number from Amazon chat, no answer-Go through insurance plan we happened to purchase, find out its still under warranty and they gave us the correct number-Call number, it ends up being the West Coast US number and I need to call the East Coast US number-Wait another day for them to call me back and send them tons of details about my order, zero integration with amazon and very manual-No response to confirm they received my info, called back and they said they will ship it out next day from NYIt has been over 3 weeks and I still have not received a replacement lid, nor a shipping number, nor an update. When you call the number, you usually get someone who speaks a foreign language. I ended up fixing the pins myself and reattaching the gasket, but for the price, you should go elsewhere.

author: C. Kim
title: Disappointed... Get what you pay for
body: We've used many Cuckoo rice cookers in the past and were looking forward to this one, esp since the price was so attractive.  Unfortunately, we essentially got what we paid for.  Here are my list of product deficiencies, in no particular order:1) the cooking (inner) pot is very light.  Maybe made out of aluminum.  But more than that the nonstick coating is very skimpy.  Within a week, already the bottom of the pan's coating has scratches.2) the menu buttons design on the top of the lid is poor.  When closing the lid, more often than not, one of the buttons gets pressed and resets the menu settings3) rice & water level markers on the inside of the pot is incredibly illegible, even with flashlights shining on it4) even with the correctly matched water level (say for 4 cups of rice) rice tends to be dry.  we prefer ours fluffy/sticky and have not yet been able to accomplish that despite several attempts to compensate with more water5) on the PLUS side, the handles on the cooking pot is convenient as is the removable inner lid & steam spout for cleaningVery disappointed that the best part of the rice cooker is these 2 features.  Returning this one and after having learned our lesson, we will look for one that costs several times more than this.

author: Paul M. Stovall
title: I smile every time I use this thing
body: Bought this one after returning two other rice cookers from a different company that failed to work out of the box.  This cooker is amazing.  We have only cooked white, brown and oatmeal so far but its really amazing.  Follow the instructions and it lets you know when done.  Easy peezy.  The auto clean function is amazing.  Looking forward to many more different types of dishes with this rice cooker.  For those that say you can just make rice on a stove and pot - yes you can - but you cant get your treadmill workout or other chores done at the same time.  Set up the Steel cut oats - go get on the treadmill and come back to fantastic breakfast -  Use two of their cups of steel cut oats to three of their cups of water - set on porridge - presto - fantastic.  And the verbal walks thrus of whats going on is just fun and informative.  Looks fantastic next to our Wok.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 17, 2024
author: huntinghawk
title: Makes good white rice
body: I'm a third generation American of Japanese Ancestry pushing 60 years of age, and I've spent all my life eating white rice.  My mother taught me how to cook rice on a wood fire, for general knowledge, and I grew up on simple one button rice cookers that would shut off after the boiling stage, while the rice finished cooking by steaming on the residual heat, basically mimicking the open fire cooking method.Growing up in the US, most white rice encountered in restaurants were disappointing, even in so called Japanese restaurants.  Many people don't soak the rice before cooking, and they use the knuckle measuring technique, often resulting in hard or mushy rice.It is common knowledge that rice in Japan is better, primarily because of the quality of the rice.  In Hawaii, we buy relatively inexpensive rice grown in California.  So, it is important to use the best cooking methods to make the best of what we have in terms of rice.The Cuckoo rice cooker is a pressure cooker, so the rice comes out with a nice shiny appearance and the grains hold their texture, i.e. not mushy.  It makes for very good and consistent rice.But it is not perfect rice in my opinion.  The machine calls the white rice "glutinous" rice.  I think this means sticky, which it is.  It is a little too sticky for sushi rice for example.  Also, the rice has a slight dark tint, almost grey.  These are minor in my opinion, as I simply eat white rice like the way most Americans eat bread or pasta, and the Cuckoo machine does a fine job at cooking white rice for Asians like me.

title: Try different cooking modes if your rice is too wet/sticky.
body: I ordered this rice cooker to replace my old one that was having issues. Around here we like jasmine rice and according to google it is not glutinous. So we tried the mixed rice setting and like many of the other reviews it did come out kinda wet and sticky, it also kinda browned the bottom of the rice. So we tried glutinous rice which did not brown the bottom of the rice but it was just as wet and sticky. If you want it to make non-sticky rice you should choose the turbo glutinous rice. It comes out perfect every time for us. I also love the self cleaning function which I use to be sure all the little nooks and crannies get a good cleaning after washing the pot.

body: Overall ???????????????Effectiveness ???????????????Price ???????????????Safe ???????????????Re-purchase? YesAfter using the previous version of this rice cooker for over 10 years, it finally gave out due to a sensor issue. Since I??ve always trusted the quality and reasonable pricing of Korean rice cookers, I started searching for a similar model online. Luckily, I found this newer version of the one I previously owned.First Impression:One thing I realized after ordering was that my old rice cooker was a 5-cup model, but this one is a 10-cup size??so it??s much bigger than I expected! title: Extremely Disappointing Product Quality and Customer Service
body: I have purchased three Cuckoo rice cookers so far, and all of them failed within a year of use. For the first defective unit, I contacted the manufacturer hoping to get it repaired, but they wanted me to ship it to their U.S. service center which would cost over $200 for shipping alone. Adding the repair cost on top of that, it made more sense to just buy a new one rather than repair it. So I had to discard the faulty unit.The second and third units faced the same issue of failing prematurely within a year. This time, I reached out to the Amazon seller for a replacement or refund, but they simply declined citing Amazon's 30-day return policy had lapsed, despite these being expensive $500 products. The seller showed absolutely no interest in making an exception or finding a solution - they just kept copy-pasting generic responses.Not only did the product itself fail to last even a year despite its premium pricing, but the customer service from both the manufacturer and the seller was extremely poor. The manufacturer expected me to pay exorbitant shipping fees, and the seller outright refused any kind of make-good solution like a replacement or refund.It is unacceptable for a $500 appliance to conk out within a year repeatedly. I have been forced to simply discard three faulty units without any resolution from either party. Prospective buyers, please beware and reconsider purchasing this terribly unreliable product with absolutely non-existent after-sales support.

author: ecooleye
title: Screen led is orange not white
body: I purchased the Cuckoo CRP-PK1001S in white. This 10 cup rice cooker does have the voice navigation in Korean, English and Chinese. I selected this model over the CRP-RT0609FW because it was going to be the same price and I liked the look of this one better. My top choice was CRP-LHTR0609FW but I didn't want to spend over $200. I had a Cuckoo 6 cup rice cooker from Korea that lasted nearly 15 years and used it at least 2-3 times per week. (We did use a step-down converter with it to use in the US and we had to replace the converter around the 10 year mark during this time). This one works like our old one and my kid loves the voice navigation function of our old one so we made sure to get one with it. The rice does seem to cook much faster. I would say my old one cooked in 30 minutes and this one does it in about 15-20 minutes. My only gripe is that the LED screen has orange lights instead of the white as depicted in pictures which is why I demoted a star. But if you watch videos or view actual images of this product when powered on, you will see that the display is orange. I don't regret my purchase because I got it for a deal of a price.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2023
author: AmazonCustomers
title: A Perfect Blend of Convenience and Quality
body: I recently purchased the CUCKOO CRP-P0609S 6-cup pressure rice cooker, and I couldn't be happier with my decision. This rice cooker offers a fantastic blend of convenience and quality, which has made it an essential appliance in my kitchen.The 12 menu options are a game-changer, allowing me to cook various types of rice and grains, including white, brown, and mixed rice, as well as porridge and soups. The intelligent cooking algorithm ensures that my rice is always cooked to perfection, with the right amount of moisture and texture.The 6-cup capacity is ideal for my family, as it allows us to cook enough rice for multiple meals. The pressure cooking feature also speeds up the cooking process and helps preserve the nutritional value of the rice.The design is sleek and modern, making it an attractive addition to my countertop. The easy-to-use LED screen and control panel make it simple to select the desired cooking mode and customize settings. The non-stick inner pot is easy to clean, and the safety features, such as the pressure control and automatic steam release, give me peace of mind while using the appliance.In summary, the CUCKOO CRP-P0609S pressure rice cooker has exceeded my expectations and made cooking rice a breeze. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable and high-quality rice cooker that offers both convenience and versatility.
