date: Reviewed in the United States on May 11, 2024
author: Truth Seer
title: A Decent Air Oven with Room for Improvement
body: This Air Oven is a decent addition to any kitchen. It‚Äôs not the best on the market, but it‚Äôs far from the worst. With 18 preset modes, it offers a good range of cooking options. However, like many non-commercial appliances, you‚Äôll need to experiment with temperatures and times to get the best results. The presets are a good starting point, but don‚Äôt expect them to be perfect for every dish.The oven is of a decent size, fitting comfortably in most kitchens without taking up too much space. It‚Äôs a bit on the loud side, but that‚Äôs to be expected with these types of appliances. The fans continue to run even after the device is turned off, which can be a bit annoying but is understandable as it helps to cool down the oven.Despite these minor issues, I still recommend this product. It‚Äôs a solid air oven that does its job well, even if it requires a bit of tweaking to get the best results.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 14, 2024
author: Alex
title: Perfect size air oven for a family
body: The size of this air oven is slightly bigger than I expected but it was great. I like all the presets on this machine as it makes cooking very easy and I didn't have to mess with the temperature much. I have not yet need to clean it so hopefully it won't be too difficult. Overall, this is a nice and easy-to-use air oven. It also feels durable so I'm hoping it will last for some time.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 10, 2024
author: Carol
title: Does it all!
body: This appliance is amazing, does it all! I'm using it mainly as a toaster oven, since I dont want to heat up my oven for just one person. It heats up frozen dinners, toasts bread, bakes pastries for one person, really does it all! Very happy with this item!

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2024
author: Littlelady
title: Sleek Looking Air Fryer!
body: This CUCKOO, Air Fryer Oven is very sleek looking, is nice and large and works really well. The presets are awesome and it heats up quickly. The only thing I don't like about this so far is how hot it gets on the sides and top. I had a similar air fryer that didn't do that so I was surprised by the amount of heat it generates externally, so I decided to pull it out away from my wall and cabinets before use. The oven has the classic pull down door which I love. The rotisserie attachment is simple to use and the drip tray is really easy to clean. This is a really nice airfryer oven over all; one of my favorite features is the dehydrate option which will come in handy when I harvest my garden produce. The Value on this is Decent!

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 15, 2024
author: Saul Luna
title: Love it!
body: This is a full oven and gives the same features as the range does. It is bulky in the box but once out can easily be placed anywhere around the counter. I can now have two ovens for the holidays or just use this one and forget about the cleaning on the range. Would extremely recommend. Would give it more than 5 stars if I could.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 10, 2024
author: mr. opinionated
title: Perfect for 2-3 person household
body: We have been countertop cooking for years.  We rarely ever turn our stove on.We have a larger countertop convection oven however there are times when we are making two items for a meal that require different temperatures.  We would cook one and keep it warm while the other was cooking.  Not the greatest method.  With this to augment our other oven, meal prep will be much easier and faster.We use countertop convection over a standard gas or electric stove as they do not heat up the house like the stoves do.  They also use less electricity than an electric stove.  We find they also cook faster and more evenly than an oven.This oven is all that and a bit more.  It has a nice size.  The controls are clear and bright. It is fair easy to operate.  It comes with a great selection of accessories to include a rotating spit.  We will be trying that feature out over the weekend.I had to try it out as soon as I could.  I cleaned the parts and then grabbed a bag of frozen onion rings.  The bag was just a bit more than the basket could hold, but I threw caution to the wind and piled the few remaining rings on top of the others.  The onion rings were fully cooked in the minimum recommended time.  They were piping hot and were crispy.Other uses will be dehydrating foods and drying nontoxic clay projects.I feel we will be very pleased with this oven.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 7, 2024
author: Kari Biggs
title: Good Air Fryer
body: This air fryer is rather easy to set up and operate as it comes along with a good set of instructions.It's also very easy to clean, which is much appreciated, making cooking worthwhile for someone like me who errs cleaning.It has a dehydrating setting that I can't wait to test out as I've done that before. It cooks food rather well and quickly in some instances, depending on what is cooked and how much of it. It certainly makes meals much easier that using a conventional stove.I recommend this product to anyone searching for a good-quality air fryer with some added benefits !

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2024
author: SiTruHaitian
title: Awesome kitchen appliance
body: The Cuckoo air oven's innovative technology allows for incredibly even cooking, crispy results, and minimal oil usage. The Food comes out perfectly cooked, every time. The oven is also incredibly versatile (Toasting,  reheating, baking etc.). I've made everything from roasted vegetables to crispy fries to perfectly cooked steak. The compact design and easy-to use interface make it a breeze to operate. The price for this appliance is definitely acceptable. Because Of this product I save less time for cleaning it after each use as well. It doesn't take a lot of space on my counter top. This is a fantastic product

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 27, 2024
author: Feliza Hsu
title: Truly a very convenient add on to my kitchen!
body: First of all, it looks small the capacity is larger than it appears.Secondly, it is truly five in one! Just like the display mentioned. It can air fry, air  broil, bake, grill and roast.Last but not least, it is so easy to clean!!! Which is very important for anyone who likes to cook and spends time in the kitchen. The first time  I used the air fry mode and forgot to spray some oil one the basket, some of the item with batter got stock on the basket. So I did what the instruction book suggested, to soak in warm water. So I soak the basket inside the roasting pot in warm water for about 30 minutes. Maybe it does not need that much time. I soak it and went on working on other things so by the time I remember,30 minutes past already. Anyways, all the dried batter came out like butter! I was so happy. The roast pot, which I used for baking a filet, and it was so easy to clean as well.  So far, I only used the baking and air frying function, and so far I am very happy with the result.Note: I used the recipe booklet that comes with the package to adjust the temperature and cooking time.  However, it does take time and maybe more try and error to figure out what would be the best setting.highly recommended it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 6, 2024
author: mostlyharmlessx
title: Great indoor grill, shape/size allows more space to cook
body: This device cooks fairly well as a grill, and is compact for the cooking area provided. We have a Ninja Foodi which overlaps a bit with this cooker, but has less space for cooking due to the round shape.For straight indoor grilling, this is better; more space and easier to work with. I like the grilling accessory alot (pictured), very nice and not too hard to clean. The interior cookpot cleans out pretty easily, there's a nice non-stick surface there. The controls are not hard to learn, just keep the instructions handy the first time or two. There's a recipe book included that gives instructions for cooking using each of it's functions.The grilling works well, meat came out pretty similar to cooking on a gas grill. The size is great, you can cook for 2 at once pretty easily, or 4 if the portions are smaller. The grill pan is lifted off enough to allow extra juices to run off, good design and function all around. I did not try baking yet, but it has several recipes that show you how and looks like it'll work well.Construction overall is pretty solid, and the buttons are easy to use. This unit has both touch-controls and knobs, and both work well. The unit itself, the inner pan, and grill grate all look impressive and well-made. The mesh basket for air frying looks a little flimsy and might be hard to clean, but I can't speak to that yet. However, it'll provide plenty of space for french fries or tater tots.Heating and cooking is pretty quick, much quicker than our big oven. This is a great appliance for one or two people, you can cook quite a few different items and have plenty of food with leftovers. It does what our oven can do but quicker, and cleanup is not bad. I normally grill outside and cook more meat at once, but if you want to grill inside or do smaller portions, this is actually very decent. Like our Ninja Foodi, this isn't a small appliance, so you'll need either a countertop or cabinet to put it in when not in use.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2025
author: Crafty Momma
title: Versatile and Efficient, But Overpriced
body: The CUCKOO One-Touch Personal Blender and Food Mixer (CFM-D60W) is a sleek and efficient kitchen appliance that performs well for daily blending needs, but its price point is higher than comparable options on the market.Performance & Features (‚≠êÔ∏è‚≠êÔ∏è‚≠êÔ∏è‚≠êÔ∏è)This blender offers a powerful motor that easily handles smoothies, protein shakes, and soft ingredients like fruits and yogurt. The two different blade types are a nice touch, allowing for more versatility when blending different textures. The one-touch operation makes it incredibly easy to use, perfect for those who prefer a straightforward, no-fuss experience.Additionally, the two included containers are convenient for on-the-go use, and their compact design makes storage simple. The ETL certification adds an extra layer of safety assurance, which is always a plus.DrawbacksWhile the blender is certainly effective, the price is noticeably high compared to similar personal blenders that offer similar power and features. Some competing models come with additional accessories or larger capacity for a lower cost. Also, it struggles with harder ingredients like ice or frozen fruits unless cut into very small pieces, which limits its use for certain smoothie recipes.Final VerdictIf you're looking for a reliable and stylish personal blender that is easy to use and clean, this is a great option. However, the higher price may not justify the features, especially when more affordable alternatives exist with similar capabilities. If you're set on the CUCKOO brand and don't mind the premium cost, it‚Äôs a solid purchase‚Äîbut if budget is a concern, you might find better value elsewhere.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2025
author: Katherine Y
title: Easy to operate mixer handles small volumes of food readily
body: Sharp stainless-steel blades make grinding and mixing foods easy with this appliance. The mixer set comes with 500- and 1000-ml containers, a 1000-ml container without blades but with a lid, a grinder blade, a mixer blade, and an instruction booklet with simple recipes. The blender is ideal for making smoothies, grinding seeds, and chopping small amounts of vegetables. In seconds, I made a satisfying blueberry smoothie with a few pulses of the appliance.Note that the unit shouldn‚Äôt be operated for more than a minute at a time and should have a three-minute cool-down period before additional use. The appliance is easy to operate and takes up little space but is not intended to handle large quantities of food. According to the instructions, the containers and blades should be cleaned with a brush and natural detergent. The box states that the plastic containers are BPA-free.

title: Makes good drinks mixer
body: Great mixer for drinks works well. Fair pricing. Easy to use and easy to clean up

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2025
author: AnnaBeau
title: Meh
body: For the price, I expected better, honestly.  Buy a Magic Bullet for $20.  The motor of this blender is very weak, and I can really only find functionality for it to whip eggs, or blend a protein powdered shake.  It struggles with frozen fruit and I'm afraid to burn the motor out.  This is not a $100 appliance, by any means.  Also the pieces are hand-wash only, which is a pain.  I do have to say that aesthetically, the unit is pleasant and attractive, but the utility is just not there.  I wanted this for use in the kitchenette at my office, but it's just not up to par with my expectations.  If you have VERY light needs, then this may work for you, but alas, it's not for my needs.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2025
author: Debbie Lee Wesselmann
title: Leaks Too Easily
body: This corded Cuckoo personal blender is more powerful than my cordless personal blenders, but that doesn't make it better.  The worst part is that it leaks too easy, and when it does, the liquid can drain into the motor.  The blade assembly screws into the bottom of the container, but even when I tightened it, frozen ingredients moved it just enough to leak.  To get it safely in place, you have to tighten it strongly, and that means it's then difficult to remove the base.I would have given this personal blender four stars if not for the leaking, but that doesn't mean it is great otherwise.  Even the large container is smaller than my Ninja and Nutribullet.  Again, with frozen ingredients making a thick smoothie, it has issues.  When you remove the blade unit from the bottom, you'll get too much around the blade.  Due to the threads, you cannot drink comfortably out of it.  I had to use a thin silicone spatula to get my smoothie out from around the blades and from the container into a glass.  It did not fully blend my 1/2 frozen banana, but it did the frozen cherries along with the yogurt and almond milk.You have to hold down the pulse button to blend.  That gives you some control, although I found even with non-frozen ingredients, that I had to stop and start a lot to get the ingredients to the bottom.It does have good power for a personal blender, but that's because it plugs in, rather than working cordlessly. I really wanted to like this blender, particularly since I've heard good things about the Cuckoo brand, but I don't.--Debbie Lee Wesselmann

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2025
author: Rick E.
title: Works for Our Use Case well
body: We ordered this blender not to be a juicer but to assist with grinding up spices such as tumeric and ginger before freeze drying it. We wanted a dedicated machine that would not be used for other things and this fit the purpose.It works ok for our use case and is able to get the pieces small enough for effective freeze drying.  Its not a large unit so its easy to put it away when not in use. And with a single button, not much learning curve either. I would prefer it to have a bit more power but its not a grinder really so I cannot expect too much.I think it would be ideal for making smoothies or blending breakfast juice drinks and such.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 10, 2025
author: G. Davies
title: convenient
body: This blender works well for a quick smoothie. You can also use it to blend spices. It comes with a medium and small blender cup as well as a separate medium blender cup with a lid to take your smoothie to go. This would be handy to use if you don't have time to get out a big blender or just need a quick blend to fix and run out the door. I will say, I don't feel like it cut up the fruit as well as my big ninja blender, and I had issues with thicker mixes getting stuck up in the top of the cup and having to take the cup off and get a spoon to stir it to get it to mix well. The motor seemed a little weak but it still worked well for the thinner mixtures .

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2025

title: THIS HAS A POWERFUL BLEND TO IT, BUT IS NOT THE BEST AT CUTTING THROUGH FROZEN INGREDIENTS
body: What I love most about this personal blender is the simplicity of it. It is super easy to use and straightforward. You just press the button when you are ready to blend. The blend has a decent amount of power to it, but it still has some issues blending up ice. I like to use fresh fruit and include ice. The fruit blends perfectly fine, but it has a difficult time cutting through the ice completely. It is simple to clean and store. I am really liking it and have the intentions to keep using it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2025
author: Customer
title: Smoothies and protein shakes all day
body: Great personal smoothie and protein shake blender. I appreciate the fact that it comes with 2 cups. I do wish they were both the large cups but I suppose it's fine to have a combination of 1 large and 1 small rather than 2 small cups. I was wondering about the sufferer blades included. The 4 blade seems to be for fruits and veggies. Or what I'm calling the smoothie blade.  The 2 blade seems to be for seeds or peppercorns. I guess you could also use the 2 blade for maybe making your own peanut butter, maybe? I want thinking about using that one so much but I may have to give it a try. Definitely mags a nice smoothie/protein shake. I experienced no problems with breaking up a half banana, tossing in some frozen and fresh strawberries, milk, and a scoop of vanilla ice cream with a scoop of whey. Turned out super tasty. More importantly, no grittiness from the whey powder. My smoothie was, well, "smooth." Make sure to add enough liquid or the motor could bind.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 10, 2024
author: RS77
title: Perfect glass tea kettle!
body: I searched quite a bit for a temperature controlled electric tea kettle without contact with plastic, and stumbled upon this one. So glad I did! I‚Äôve never heard of the brand before, but went by the reviews. It lives up to the hype. It‚Äôs made of a high quality, durable glass, and the spout is made of stainless steel. It looks so nice on my countertop too. The lid is lined with stainless steel. There are two lids‚Äîone that that has the tea infuser attached, and one that is just the lid itself. I‚Äôve just been using the standard lid without infuser. It‚Äôs very easy to use‚Äîyou can increase or decrease the temperature to specified setting depending on your preference (for example, 176 F for green tea, 194 F for coffee, or 212 F boiling.), and there are several temperature settings. It can also be switched between Fahrenheit and Celsius. Once I turn on, I increase the temperature to my desired setting, then the kettle works automatically to heat the water and beeps when done. It‚Äôs really cool to see the water boiling and it also has color coded lights that change at certain temperatures (from green when cool, to blue, to pink/purple, to red near boiling and boiling point.) The temperature control also shows the temperature at all times while in use, and the kettle has an automatic shut-off feature. It works exactly as described, looks sleek, and is decent quality. The opening is wide, so refilling and washing are easy. The only thing is that the lid needs to be removed by hand to refill or wash the kettle, rather than have a button to push, but it‚Äôs a minor issue for such a great product. I‚Äôd recommend it without hesitation.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 12, 2024
author: Michelangelo Comulada
title: My Favorite Tea Kettle
body: I'm incredibly pleased with my new tea kettle. It's a fantastic replacement for my old one. I appreciate the wide range of temperature options (from 104¬∞F to 212¬∞F) and the rapid boiling time. The 7 LED colors are a fun feature that visually indicate the water temperature, starting blue/green and transitioning to red as the water heats up. I also love the 48-hour warming function, which keeps the water warm for an extended period. Additionally, the removable tea infuser is perfect for brewing large batches of tea for family and friends. The tea kettle is well-constructed and has a sleek design that complements any kitchen aesthetic. I highly recommend this product.

title: Tea basket rusted
body: The kettle is fast at heating water but the buttons are finicky and the tea basket rusted on the first use. I'll try it for a week and decide if it's worth keeping or not.

author: Love

body: I got this electric kettle about 2 weeks ago and started using it right away. I love about it almost everything. Paid almost $80 and threw my old ( not old) away because of the plastic body and the spout were covered in plastic so every time I‚Äôd boil water and take it in my hands I‚Äôd smell that nasty plastic smell. That‚Äôs one of the reasons why I was looking for none plastic one. And I found it. Now I have it and I like it. The only flaw for this kettle I have to mention is in the pictures. Idk what‚Äôs that but unfortunately some white dull spots appeared after just few use. I tried to get rid of it but it‚Äôs not going anywhere. It said stainless steel but now I‚Äôm doubt it. I was expecting it to be more better quality especially for this price but it is what it is. Just thought I should honestly mention it in my review. Maybe it‚Äôs not a big deal? I have no idea what‚Äôs that but something to be aware of when you buy this product. Other than that it‚Äôs a good kettle.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2024
author: Sonora
title: ‚ÄúDisappointing Product Experience, But Excellent Amazon Customer Service‚Äù
body: We were really excited about this product‚Äîit looks great and seemed promising. Unfortunately, during our very first use, the glass part cracked after boiling, which was disappointing. We noticed the issue because it started leaking.Maybe we just had bad luck with this one, as it does look like a well-designed product.On a positive note, Amazon‚Äôs customer service was outstanding. They quickly approved a full refund, which we truly appreciate. Thank you, Amazon, for making the process so smooth and hassle-free!

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 7, 2024
author: E Clam
title: Very fast heating -- Glass Very hot -- Difficult to wash
body: The water and tea kettle was very easy to assemble. However, the container where the tealeaves go is not made of stainless steel, making it very difficult to open, not sustainable, and difficult to clean. The water kettle is made of class, and has a very hot surface, making it difficult to hold onto and easy to break. The kettle heats the water very fast, and has instructions to adjust the temperatures by pushing the button to the desired temperature. However, while it has a memory to do save the temperature desired, it reverts back to 140 degree every time when I want to have the water boiling. Yet, it does boil the water. Therefore, it is confusing as to what setting was set at. I wish it comes with a dial instead. It comes with very detailed instruction how to clean the kettle, which I suspect cleaning will be a step that needs to executed often as tea stain will be a challenge. Otherwise, after several use, it serves my purpose of brewing tea instead leaving it in a cup or leaving it on top of the stove to simmer, either way, does not give rich tea.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2024
author: Fred C. Dobbs
title: A Good Choice for Us. Has the Features We Like and Meets Our Criteria.
body: My wife and I are familiar with the Cuckoo brand, having owned a Cuckoo rice cooker for a number of years. Recently, we've been looking for a new electric tea kettle. When I saw the Cuckoo electric tea kettle advertised on Amazon -- with all of the features we like and knowing the brand -- I figured my finding it was a stroke of good luck.For starters, it's a nice size, shape, and weight for us. The Cuckoo tea kettle is especially easy to operate. The infuser is a feature we may use, although most of our tea-making these days is limited to tea bags for just the two of us, and we have our own favorite teas. The LED lights are a feature that wasn't on our list, but now that we have them on our Cuckoo tea kettle, we like them. We also appreciate having a kettle made mostly of glass, which allows us to enjoy seeing the bubbling water and is a healthier option than having it made some other materials.Overall, the Cuckoo tea kettle is a good choice for us based on its ease of use, performance, and likelihood of reliability and durability based on our experience with the brand. I'm rating it at 4.5 stars and rounding up to 5 stars.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2024
author: Kiwipine
title: Quick and quiet
body: I kinda wish this kettle didn't look like a disco ball when it's on. The colored lights seem to be programmed to change color as the water temperature increases but, for me it's not really necessary. (I have yet to figure out if I can turn them off).The kettle itself looks to be a well made, quality kettle - it boils quickly and quietly. It holds a good volume of water - 1.7 L is perfect for my needs. It's comfortable to hold and pours well. I don't think I will use the tea infuser - It's perfect for a tea drinker, but not mandatory if you're not. Personally, I don't like my coffee tainted by tea. The timer is a nice addition ... but I don't know anyone who would want to keep their water (or tea) hot for 48 hours. More is not necessarily better .... .... and honestly ... do I care what temperature my water is heated to? Can I tell the difference in my coffee if it's 150 or 200 degrees? No, not really - as long as it's hot. I'm sure some people enjoy micro managing their beverages, but I'm happy to simply have enough water hot enough to make a french press full of wonderful , delicious coffee each morning. This kettle can do that ... and more !

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2025
author: Bree
title: Does not keep warm 48 hours. Keeps warm for 2
body: This is a nice kettle. It does what I want, and it does it quickly. The LED lights are a bit bright for my taste, but as it is behind me when I turn it on, it really isn‚Äôt an issue. The minor issue of the kettle itself I find is there is a metal lip inside, where glass meets the metal, causing a bit of a blockage for the water. You really need to tip it over as you pour when you get to the last of the water left if you want every last drop.The rod of the tea infuser I find to be a bit on the wobbly side. The basket and rod are both made of metal which is great. However both the infuser lid and regular lid, while made to look like metal, are plastic. Not thrilled that steam is going upwards onto the plastic, exposing it to heat, and then dripping back down into the water. I was trying to have less plastic exposure in my life.The real issue I have with this thing is the keep warm function. Up top, it emphasizes that it can keep warm up to 48 hours. Not just in the product description, but the pictures too. It can‚Äôt. The manual itself says for TWO hours. I‚Äôve tested it both using the keep warm option where you just set the temp after boiling, as well as the press the power button then set a temp to keep warm option. With both methods, after two hours, it make a few beeps and then shuts off. The keep warm for the advertised 48 hours was what sold me on this kettle, and it stinks that it doesn‚Äôt do what it claims.Overall, I don‚Äôt know what to make of this thing. I thought Cuckoo was supposed to be a good brand. I don‚Äôt love it, but I don‚Äôt think I would recommend it. I don‚Äôt really hate it either. I guess my feelings overall are lukewarm at best, just like my tea.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 11, 2024
author: marchfool
title: Looks great! Works great! Versatile too!!
body: Wow. This Cuckoo Kettle looks great and works great too. Boils water super fast and it‚Äôs super fun watching the light on the bottom change colors change as it heats up. Really like that instead of only one boiling water option, you can control the temperature too. If that‚Äôs not enough, it comes with two lids, one for simple boiling and another with an infuser so you can brew as much tea as you like, directly in the kettle. I like that that the kettle is made of glass so you can see and control precisely how much water you wish to heat up and it‚Äôs fun to watch the water boil too.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2024
author: Dave K
title: Boils Water Fast
body: I was on the lookout for an electric kettle to replace my stovetop model, and this one has exceeded my expectations! It heats water incredibly fast, and I really like the feature of the light that indicates how hot the water is. It‚Äôs easy to fill and pours nicely without any spills. Plus, it comes with two tops‚Äîa standard one and an infuser for tea, though I probably won‚Äôt use the infuser since I don‚Äôt brew large quantities of tea.On the (slightly) negative side:- I wish the markings on the side showed cups instead of liters.- The temperature control increases in increments of 10 degrees Celsius, which can feel odd when set to Fahrenheit.- The cord length is a bit short; I would prefer it to be twice as long.I also conducted some non-scientific temperature tests. After the water reached the set temperature, I tested it with a probe thermometer. At higher settings, it was pretty accurate, but at lower temperatures, it was off by a few degrees. I didn‚Äôt expect perfection, but it‚Äôs something to consider.Overall, it‚Äôs a great kettle that does exactly what I need‚Äîboil water quickly! I highly recommend it for anyone looking for an electric kettle.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 7, 2024
author: Brian
title: nice large capacity tea kettle
body: I actually got this to replace our espresso machine as we were mainly using the espresso machine for the water boiler. This kettle also holds a lot of water, I believe it's almost 2 liters! It comes up to temp quickly and there's an LED ring on the bottom that changes color based on how hot the water is. Neat!I do think I'd prefer having the controls on the base instead of being on top of the handle, for longevity.There's a handy tea infuser that hangs from the top lid. You can also swap it out with a regular lid that doesn't have an infuser hanging from it.Overall, pretty pleased with it!

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2025
author: Amazon Customer
title: Overall, I highly recommend this kettle. It‚Äôs efficient, stylish, and a joy to use.
body: I absolutely love this kettle! The 7 LED colors are such a fun touch‚Äîit‚Äôs not only functional but also adds a bit of excitement to the kitchen. The water heats up quickly, making it super convenient for tea or any other hot drinks. I was able to heat water and also make tea effortlessly, and it was a wonderful experience.The construction feels high-quality, especially with the no-spill pouring spout made of 304 stainless steel. The feature to maintain warmth for up to 48 hours is also impressive, adding a lot of practicality to its cute design.Overall, I highly recommend this kettle. It‚Äôs efficient, stylish, and a joy to use. Perfect for anyone looking to add both function and charm to their daily routine!

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2025
author: Michael Marshall
title: Love being able to select temperature for loose teas
body: I like using loose tea leaves instead of bags. I used to watch the bubble sizes to approximate the temperature a particular tea needed. Now I just select the temperature and let it heat. I‚Äôve also used it to boil water for cooking/baking since it‚Äôs fast. You can‚Äôt select just any temperature; the temperatures are preset and you select the one you want. The choices are 104, 122, 140, 158, 176, 194, 212. There is a string of LEDs around the base of the pot. The color changes as the water heats. In the photos you can see it start out as blue and another photo where it has reached pink. The actual temperature increments as you can see in the video. So you can either watch the color or watch the temperature if you want to keep track of the change. There is also a basket insert shown in a photo. Occasionally, I will have time to sit and drink a pot of tea and I use the basket insert. But usually I only have time for a cup of tea so I use it without the basket to just heat the water and pour it over the leaves. I like this product

body: Best teapot I've ever owned and I've had several.  This is why. date: Reviewed in the United States on July 14, 2025
author: Monks pepper
title: Good water boiler.
body: This is our third electric tea kettle in 9 years after using one in an Irish hotel where we loved it. The first one we bought lasted 7 years; the second lasted 2 years; now we‚Äôre into this Cuckoo for a month. It seems like a quality appliance but it is the costliest. The previous ones were simple pots that only boiled and automatically shut off. The Cuckoo has variable preset temperature settings with auto shut off and 3 beeps to let you know it‚Äôs finished. Others that we considered had individual buttons for each temperature but the Cuckoo has up and down buttons to select the temp ‚Äî some may prefer the individual buttons. ¬†I chose the Cuckoo because its handle shape keeps my hand down and away from the steam ‚Äî our last one allowed your hand to ride up to the top and cook your hand. We‚Äôve never bothered with the tea making features so I have no comment on its effectiveness. It‚Äôs not double-walled like some others but we don‚Äôt have little kids reaching up and touching it. The Cuckoo has a removable top for filling while our previous models were flip-ups. Capacity, boiling speed, and noise are all acceptable.

title: Works great, but not as stable in temperature and keeping things warm as my old cooker
body: Everything is great, and the automated voice assistant and sound effects are cute. There's lots of settings, and it cooks my sticky rice with good pressure while being easy to clean. But it's noticeably not as high quality as my old rice cooker which kept the rice perfectly warm at the same low heat for hours and hours. This rice cooker I think heats up a little too much when keeping it warm after cooking, so the rice on the bottom actually overcooks a little the longer you keep it in there. And I'm talking about the "keep warm" function after it finishes cooking. It's not a big deal, but if you want your rice to stay soft then unplug the cooker and store it in the freezer to microwave it as needed.Don't forget to clean the pressure seal on top and empty the water trap in the back. It's easy to clean!

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2023
author: LIZA
title: I THINK I love this multicooker? But it is a commitment.
body: I've had this for about a year and just started using it a few months ago, after a ton of research. It's a beautiful, sturdy, GIANT machine, and as one person I hate the idea of this sitting permanently on my counter, but I'm fine with my new Cuckoo station. (After all these reviews I bought the rice cooker also, since most likely I'd want rice with whatever I'm cooking in this.)No, it doesn't speak Korean (which annoys me too); it really is enormous; the lid is hydraulic and smashes into my upper kitchen cabinet unless you handle it carefully, both handed.The manual has many issues, since it's inconsistent, vague, and same with online recipes, etc. There is no clear answer on anything really- I've researched this for months now after trying to use it and though it's a nightmare to pore over everyone's input, I think I'm finally figuring this out. I generally know how to cook normally, but this is infuriating since you have no idea when it's at pressure; it's vague whether steam cook is at pressure (it claims not, but the pressure button lights up; the manual says to do something impossible to turn it off or is defective- so here it is contradictory.)Rice: in the Amazon description it specifically states this setting is for LONG GRAIN ONLY; I took a risk and used it for short; it turned out on the very gloppy end. Apparently ppl like to use the steam function, but there are so many variables I have not figured this out yet.  Good thing I also got the actual rice cooker.Multicook- what is this exactly, someone please explain. There is no clear answer anywhere. The top level seems too hot; the lower levels take ages to sear/brown anything.Presets: how to change them is inconsistent, as I'm having a HARD time pressing the buttons, and the menu is difficult to see unless you are looking at it head-on. So this means crouching down and squinting in the 3-second time window to have to figure out what you are pressing.I do love that the handles are not hot and that there is a stainless steel inner pot. However, is defeats the purpose if it comes with a plasticine steamer plate.I do love that it doesn't stink up your kitchen or spew smelly liquids out of the pressure spout. The auto clean is easy buttime-consuming. The buttons are so hard to press that it is a two handed situation, as well as opening the pot, or it will jump otherwise. That or ruin the opening spring. This I find ridiculous for it otherwise being a well designed pot (?)Also the lack of precise documentation is infuriating. It's good for people who just like to press one button and not know why it works.  Or not.That said, I will update if anything else crazy happens...

author: Amazon Customer
title: Disappointed
body: I bought this mainly as a rice cooker. Huge mistake. I had a cuckoo before this one that I loved. I broke the push button opener so I bought several replacement part that was close to the old one but none of them fit. I reluctantly bought this cuckoo to replace the one I broke. I am Korean so I have had several rice cookers and usually bought a Japanese brand. Anyway, I'm very disappointed with this cuckoo. It cooks the rice fine but cannot keep the rice warm properly. The condensation build up is too excessive and the temperature as it keeps it warm is too hot even at the lowest setting. Every time you open the lid, the excessive water that builds up on the lid saturates the rice and makes it mushy. So you end up with mushy rice on top and hard dry rice on the bottom. I will have to buy another one and maybe throw this one away. I threw away the packaging so not sure if I can return it. If you like to keep rice in the cooker for couple of days, do not buy this  cuckoo.

author: Kindle Customer
title: This rice cooker has been on fire
body: When we opened the box, there was a distinct burning smell. When we flipped the rice cooker over, it was plain to see that this thing had recently been on fire. Judging from the melted metal slug at the bottom of the box, it was a pretty hot fire. It's interesting that this clear evidence of fire did not extend to the box itself. If I didn't know any better, I would think that Amazon shipped me a previously owned rice cooker....one that had been through the gauntlet of fire. But, there's nothing in the description that indicates this could be a refurbished product, so I guess this model is prone to very localized fires?Regardless, the fact that this is capable of such extreme melting does give me pause...

author: Aiden
title: Worse rice cooker ever!
body: I'm a korean and I have used rice cookers for my entire life pretty much. And most of them have been Cuckoo but I have never encountered such a bad unit like this one. DO NOT BUY THIS.The bottom of rice if you keep it warm will become crispy that you won't be able to eat. It's not just a bit crispy. Rock hard I'm talking about here. I've tried different warming temp with no success. And the top of cooker retains the evaporated water that when I open it, it drops large amount of water onto the rice. So the top of rice is always soggy. 3 days of warming, you can't even eat it anymore. By the time I decided that the unit was defective, the return window had passed. So I contact Cuckoo west coast branch. They told me to drop it off so I did. It took them a month to send me a new one. They said " we don't sell this model and we couldn't fix it. So we have to reach out the company who sold this and ask them to send you a new one". When i got a new one a few days ago, I was just left with disappointment that it is exactly same as the previous one. Now I don't know what to do with this. STAY AWAY FROM THIS MODEL.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 30, 2021
author: Chad
title: Simply the best!
body: This is a great machine. Much higher quality than that other brand of pressure cooker, which I had and never used. It fixes all the issues I had. No burn notices, no messy steam releases, no reading a recipe that??s ??8 minutes?? but takes 45 total. It??s a breeze to clean with non stick surfaces, and it??s easy to use!  The voice guide will remind you to lock the lid if that??s preventing it from starting, and the steam release is a totally automatic and clean - no towels needed and you don??t have to poke it with the handle of a wooden spoon to keep from getting burned. Yes, the time is total time, but use the presets - they really work. Be confident with it, and you don??t even have to rely on recipes, so it becomes a great tool in the kitchen. I would not attempt to leave rice in those for more than an hour or so - it??s a multi cooker, not a dedicated rice cooker, so do keep that in mind. But the quality of this unit is just the best. A luxurious appliance for your kitchen.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2021
author: Peter K. Evans
title: Advanced features, great looks, but one serious design flaw that ruins it
body: I used the Cuckoo for a couple months before throwing in the towel. The Cuckoo has some very advanced features, not found in other multi-cookers, and looks fantastic. The main problem is the timer. Most recipes call for "pressure cook for x minutes" - but you can't program the Cuckoo to do this. You can only program the timer for the entire time it's cooking, from warm-up to finish. I constantly over-cooked or under-cooked recipes because of this. I switched to the unit recommended by America's Test Kitchen - which isn't as advanced, nor as well built, nor as good looking - and never looked back. The Cuckoo is like a Cadillac with wobbly wheels - great unit but the engineers need to go back to the drawing board and fix this fundamental design flaw.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 11, 2020
author: Aarti
title: Awesome! Love it and you should buy it! Lol
body: Alrighty guys. I am not paid by anyone to write this. I LOVE this electric pressure cooker.It is NOT made in China. It is made in Korea.  I waited and waited and waited and waited a little bit more to see if there was any product coming on the market to replace the most popular item known as instant pot. I did my research for two years. I read reviews looked at pros and cons. I have done my research and I??m happy with my final decision of buying this electric pressure cooker. The inside is stainless steel and not nonstick coating. Outside is nice and pretty looking. InstructIons  are very easy and highly applicable. You don??t need to look for more information online. I just read the manual and the recipe book and I have been using it for two weeks. After two weeks I have realized I have been missing it from my kitchen. My top concern about buying electric pressure cooker was where the manufacturing took place.Now let??s talk about working of the pressure cooker. It has cooked my high heat Indian cooking. It has cooked American food with light saut??. I have posted some pictures of brussels sprouts that I saut??ed for dinner. That was my first cooking in the pressure cooker. I loved it. Family loved it. I have made rice and It cooked very well. I have made curry. I have made lentils. I have made yogurt. It is overall a very very very good product. And I am very happy with my purchase. It talks to you. The lady??s voice will tell you what is required for your recipe to start coming together. I do not mind it. She??s very happy. There is also musical start and end of the recipe. I don??t mind that either. It is overall a very very very good product. And I am very happy with my purchase. Very nicely made product and I can cook it all in one go. I have one manual pressure cooker and one electric pressure cooker so far and I think there??s nothing that can stop me now. Cooking Wise. Don??t think twice before buying this. I think this is the only one in the series that has stainless steel inner part. It was my second  priority to have it the way it is after the location of manufacturing. enjoy guys. Again I am not paid by anyone. This is my honest to goodness feeling about this product.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 3, 2020
author: Toru Hirosawa
title: Reasonable and trusting rice cooker!!!!
body: First of all, I want to point out that I have been eating rice my entire life as a Japanese person and I don??t tolerate ??decent rice?? quality. This rice cooker was very reasonable out of all the other 150 dollar range ones. Even after reading some bad reviews on this product, I trusted my gut after researching about it on different websites. And I??m glad I did!! This one cooks rice in 30 mins which is 20 mins faster than the last rice cooker I had and cooks fluffy, and delicious rice!!!I would like to correct some negative points the people mentioned in reviews coz they weren??t true!??(this came with no instructions)??-it came with super descriptive instructions in English, Chinese and Korean!??(this cooks mushy rice) ??- not true!! It cooked perfectly fluffy rice when I cooked it while following the instructions. I believe this person didn??t measure it right; you know what happens when you put too much water:mushy rice :pAdditionally I would like to point out a few more positive things about this product.?? it talks!!! How great is that?! That itself was very entertaining and made me happy to have this product.?? non sticky pan! Super easy to clean!?? auto clean mode ??- after rinsing the pan, you can simply put the pan back in and push the auto clean button and it sanitizes it for you with steam!! Amazing!I??m more than happy to get this product and I still can??t believe people talked bad about it. I highly recommend it!!!

author: Lynn kang
title: Dry, tasteless
body: Honestly I expected more. My mom??s 15 years old cuckoo never disappointed me and still makes tasty and shiny Rice everytime. Maybe it is okay for some people but Most Korean Like myself can be super picky when it comes to eating rice. I??ve been using a pressure pot for long time which is best way to cook rice I think. I was Looking easier way and I decided to try this.My rice is never shiny, It is dry, old looking and tasteless. Korean people can eat a bowl of rice with little bit of soy sauce with butter or just spoonful of fresh cooked rice. I did a little research and this model is not available in Korea. I think they made it for USA Customer. Cooking set up and setting is quite different from Korea version. I??m switching back to pressure pot and will use cukcoo as a warmer. Now It is expensive warmer.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 7, 2019
author: Andrew
title: Do not purchase as a primary rice cooker
body: I've heard rave reviews, particularly from the Korean community both online and in-person about the Cuckoo line of multi-purpose pressure cookers so we decided to give this one a try. On the first impression, build quality is great with solid plastics and metals with included accessories like a measuring cup and rice paddle. We purchased this with the intention of primarily using it as a rice cooker and after a few rounds of cooking and keeping rice warm, the overall impression goes down. The thing speaks to you throughout the whole process from selection of function, pressurizing of the cooker, and even the release of the pressure with simulated pressure release sounds.If purchasing this for use primarily for cooking rice I would steer clear and go for a Zojirushi. It can cook rice, but not to the standard of an almost $200 rice cooker. Uneven cooking, some parts cooked, overcooked, and even undercooked. The keep-warm function has to be the worst. Steam isn't necessarily being released because of the locking mechanism meaning but even when not locked completely and kept on the keep warm function, actual puddles of steam would collect and continue to be absorbed by the rice. By the end of a 3 hour keep warm, almost all the rice would be too soggy to even enjoy.If you plan on using this rice cooker to cook small batches of rice which would be consumed right away this could be the solution of freeing up counter space for a rice cooker/pressure cooker/slow cooker making it perfect for smaller kitchens. Otherwise, if you're particular with your rice and prefer to keep rice warm for several hours skip this pressure cooker and shop for a dedicated rice cooker.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2019
author: Ryan McLean
title: Timer Flawed - Fundamentally Fails as a Pressure Cooker
body: tl:dr - Cuckoo pressure cooker timer is for total cook time, not time at pressure. This is contrary to how all pressure cooker recipes are written, making this electric pressure cooker essentially pointless for following pressure cooker recipes.I took a chance on this Cuckoo Multi Pressure Cooker over the ubiquitous instant pot. I was excited about the possibility of the automatic steam valve and overall aesthetic. Unfortunately it is getting returned.All pressure cooker recipes are written and based on time-at-pressure.This is done because once a pressure cooker has reached pressure it is at more or less a known temperature (Similar to preheating an oven). The time it takes to get to pressure will vary depending on the size of the pressure cooker, how full it is, and the actual makeup of the stuff (More liquid versus more solid).To account for this - most (probably all - I double checked the manuals for 4 popular brands) other pressure cookers have a timer that doesn't start counting down until the pressure cooker actually reaches pressurization.Design Defect Number 1Unfortunately the timer on this Cuckoo works differently - the timer starts counting down as soon as you start cooking, and doesn't take into account whether the cooker has reached pressurization or not. This presents two big challenges.Following recipes written for other pressure cookers simply will not work, because the pressure cooking times given on those recipes assumes the timer will not start counting down until the pressure cooker reaches the pressurization stage.One example of the result of this - All pressure cooker recipes require some liquid for the pressure cooker to pressurize and work. This can just be plain water or a broth or sauce. In this cuckoo, starting with cold versus warm liquids would affect the outcome of a recipe because the starting temperature of these liquids would affect how long it takes for the unit to come up to pressure. In other pressure cookers, this could still somewhat affect the outcome, but the effect would be much less since the most important thing in pressure cooking is time at pressure.Design Defect Number 2The machine does not give you any indication whether it is at pressure or not - so even if you wanted to take your own run at adjusting recipes written for other pressure cookers, it's impossible (or at least would be very difficult) for you to know how much to adjust the recipe by, because you basically have to just guess whether it is at pressure or not.I don't know how Cuckoo overlooked something so fundamental to pressure cooking. I would not recommend this unit to anyone.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2019
author: TheBlindLife
title: Mostly Accessible for Blind
body: I was waiting for a sale on this unit. Being blind, a hefty investment. I even got Easy Pay with my Chase Amazon card.I wanted a multi - cooker that was accessible to use. This is definitely accessible with the voice guide. Cuckoo failed in one big area with the voice guide.... The unit doesn't speak temperature, or time when being set. Its not impossible to use, but you need to be able to calculate where you are when setting. Luckily, it saves your last setting you set.For blind customers... This is a sturdy device. Definitely built with safety in mind. Most important, no pressure needs to be set. Its automatic. The unit tells you when engaged and when it's safe to open. It also tells you when lock is engaged properly.Their are 8 buttons on the unit. They are slightly raised, but I still used bump dots. The Cook, and Cancel button are the only ones that have an actual tactile bump on them.I'm confident I will get much use with this cooker. If Cuckoo had the unit actually announce temperature, and time level... It would be a perfect addition to any blind cook. I'd give a 90% rating for accessibility.Update:After making a few dishes, I'm updating my rating to 5 from 4. While I was not happy about temp and heat level not having voice... I've managed to do ok. I just have to note my last meal setting, not being able to see the display.I've used the meat, brown, slow cook, and multi - cook settings. All meals came out wonderful.I feel safer. The unit doesn't get as hot on the outside as my Crock Pot. It also sits lower, not as tall. This is actually a safer thing for a blind cook. It also happens to make serving easier.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2018
author: James K
title: TLDR.. For Korean rice, use 6 min
body: The time to replace our old rice cooker was long overdue.. We had a Cuckoo for 7yrs and in the last year, the rice wouldn't stay fresh in the pot and usually had to be thrown away if not eaten by the next day.  I didn't want to pay $400-700 for another rice cooker.. just seems too expensive for just making rice which is the only thing we use it for.  So I ordered this one..The first few times cooking rice, we used the default setting for Rice and it said 8 mins (not including heat up and pressure release).. overall, it takes about 40mins from pressing the cook button to being ready to eat.  While the rice was cooked and ready to eat, it came out a bit too soft and mushy for our taste..  From years of cooking rice, we basically know how much water to put in by eye but then again, this is a larger pot so I thought maybe it was different.  However, lowering the amount didn't help much.  I then decreased the cook time to 6min and the rice came out perfect.  Plus, the cooker kept the rice fresh for several days.  I haven't tried it for anything other than Korean rice but for that, it's fantastic.

title: Too complicated. Manuel is absurdly long and difficult to understand.
body: This thing is pretty much a rocket ship and the 70000000 page long manual it comes with is about as clear as mud. Compared to the Tiger Corporation JBV-A10U-W 5.5-Cup Micom Rice Cooker I just received this thing is just plain difficult to even begin using. I LOVE the concept and if you can figure it out I'm sure you'll love it because it seems so well made. I just really couldn't get my head around it sorry.We went back to just using a stove top pressure cooker and the new rice cooker which can cook enough food for two people and rice at the same time. I think the rice cooker we have is much more limited than the Cuckoo but at least I can use it effectively.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 13, 2018
author: Charlotte R. Alexander
title: Learning curve
body: Have been cooking with the cuckoo for about two weeks.  It??s a beautiful cooker with very nice fit and finish.  I bought this one for the safety feature of the pressure auto releasing so you don??t get scalded.  Also inner pot has handles that don??t get too hot.  So far I??ve made some perfect boiled eggs and some steamed chicken and vegetables. Chicken came out cooked exactly right. The real problem is the manual which is not well translated into english.  Spent over an hour trying to figure out how to put the rubber seals back after cleaning. Photos in manual are very bad.  Recipe book has very weird dishes.  There are no instructions for cooking simple things like boiled eggs or brown rice (which i made a mess of but my fault.).  Testing out the slow cooker feature today.  Smells good!  Hope some someone puts together a cook book. Different system on timing from other brands. This times from the time you start cooking. I understand other brands start timing after pressure builds up.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 22, 2018
author: Bogey17
title: Cuckoo Multi Pressure Cooker Is Not What I Expected!
body: This Cuckoo Multi Pressure cooker really does require a very different learning curve, compared to the Instant Pot 3 & 6 quart Ultra (which I own and very familiar with). The unit itself is quite large for only a 5 quart capacity and takes up a lot of real estate in your kitchen unless you have lots of counter space or space to store it. It does have some positive features (automatic steam release valve, self-cleaning setting, handles on the inner pot) but functions, in my opinion, more as a multi-cooker rather than as a pressure cooker. I found that cooking times are longer compared to my Instant Pot. In addition, my IP Ultra has a 6 quart capacity, has a smaller design, and doesn't take up as much space in my smaller NYC apartment which for me is more practical. Perhaps, this Cuckoo MPC would work for some people, but not for me I'm sorry to say.

author: niky2809

body: First of all, the shipping was fast  and safe as Amazon Prime Member. When I received it, I was a little surprised the box size and weight cause it was heavier and bigger than my expectation.I have already used it a couple of times!!! Each time I am very satisfied with my purchase even though a little pricey just compare to others.My first challenge was chicken and I looked at several recipes on the cookbook to figure it out which feature and time could be used for a delicious Korean braised chicken (Jjimdak). I choose the multi-cook (default heating level 7 and 30 mins for cooking time) to cook 7 drum sticks 10 wings with soy sauce based marinade and then added more sauce, all vegetables with glass noodle (already soaked in the water for 30 mins) using the vegetable for 15 mins. It turned out super well! Chicken was very tender and vegetable was also perfectly cooked. It took almost only 1 hr including preparation also and it was very easier than cooking on the stove. The best convenience is that I don??t need to be a babysitter waiting and checking the food on the stove to make sure the heat and time. If you are looking for the efficient and convenient multi cooker, this is the way to go and everyone should have this Cukcoo Multi cooker. To be honestly, I have learned to enjoy cooking with CUKCOO. Thanks, you have saved my dinner table.I wish an explanation of each different features, level and comparison such as between multi cook and steam cook also should be in more detail on the cookbook. It would be much helpful to use it anytime. Or maybe I didn??t look carefully enough at the each page :(

PFOA FREE!!!!4. Safety: kitchen companion your family needs!  This thing has 14 Safety features include auto steam release. Enjoy your meals without the hassle!5. Preset mode: People who have no time to cook or has to cook all the time, Here is what you need!  I use the preset almost on weekdays. I set it in the morning, and Food is ready when I get home. Like slow cooker!7. Voice Guide: It tells you what??s going on when it releases pressure steam, changes to keep warm mode or errors. I lower the voice guide sound to level one, and I think it??s not annoying as much as default mode.8.  Show how much time the Machine is going to take to complete.9. Recipe Book: I wasn??t very happy with the recipe book it comes with the product. However, I found that they upload the new recipe on their website and FB, IG. Find cuckoo multi cooker on FB page or IG.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2018
author: Radio Ross
title: Excellent Cooker - Not so excellent manual.
body: This is an excellent all-around cooker and it does a great job on *most* things.  If you are a rice aficionado, it may not be as satisfying as a dedicated Cuckoo Rice Cooker - We have one too and, IMHO, it is the best out there.  If you only want one cooker, this is definitely the one to get - far better quality than the cheaper versions.  One caveat: Cuckoo's philosophy for this cooker is, "I Cook, You Rest" which means that their default settings for time and temperature will work in most cases.  You do have the ability to change cooking time for all options but temperature is only adjustable for about half of the options.  While this sounds like a limitation, in practice it is not.  Just be mindful that the temperature setting options are by "level", not degrees Fahrenheit.  That means there's a learning curve but once you get used to this, you'll love this cooker.  One other thing... Do not be tempted to fill the pot above the maximum mark with any liquid - particularly if you are using it in a pressure cook mode.  Doing so guarantees that liquid will spew out of the steam vent and overflow the collection cup - Spoken from experience.I do have one negative:  The user manual is not particularly helpful for cooking practices and the cookbook that's packed in with the cooker is only marginally better.  You WILL have to experiment so don't invite guests for dinner until you've played around a bit.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 19, 2018
author: Spencer Rodriguez
title: Perfect Rice and So Much More. Cuckoo for Cuckoo!
body: Excellent rice cooker (and so much more)! So far I've used this to cook rice (long grain, jasmine, brown) with ease and consistency as well as to make soups and chicken tacos (I am hoping to try baby back ribs soon). The cooker is extremely spacious and can make dinner for multiple families in one go. It was also great for a sushi making party, cooking up perfect rice in a huge quantity at the press of a button. The party was a surefire hit (I recommend buying the little bamboo mats for rolling your sushi).The cooker itself, while easy to use, is pretty sophisticated, so I would definitely recommend reading the manual through to get the most out of your Cuckoo. According to the marketing jargon, it uses an algorithm to adjust cooking according to the quantity of rice and water, and I am inclined to believe given how great the rice has come out each time, despite my less than precise measurements (a scooper is provided, but I prefer to eyeball). On the rice side, this is certainly designed for white rice varieties, and it is cool that it can create different textures (sticky, soft, etc.), but I have also found it to be great for brown rice as well. Plus, it does so much more than cook rice, as noted by the various settings.This machine also talks! I'm not so sure how I feel about the little voice??equal parts useful, terrifying, and just plain bizarre??but there is a mute option that provides immediate relief for those that find it off putting. I am keeping it on for now as the charm remains. It can sometimes even feel cheery. Regardless, it does little to take away from this amazing machine. Go Cuckoo!

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 18, 2018
author: Honest Professional Reviews
title: Excellent Korean-made construction & attention to detail; Easy to use controls. This is the multi-cooker you want.
body: Before I got this Cuckoo multi pressure cooker, I had never used a pressure cooker before. Sure, I HAVE an old fashioned pressure cooker. I am not sure where I got it, but I got it decades ago and it sits quietly on the shelf where I occasionally look at it. But I've always considered it's reason d'etre to be unclear, and the idea of many bars of pressure inside this pot scary.So maybe I wasn't the obvious target for this new do-everything-for-you pressure cooker. Or maybe I was EXACTLY the correct target.Anyway, intrigued by the idea that it does do everything--both pressure cook and release the steam automatically and safely afterwards--I took a flyer.I should add that it doesn't just generically pressure cook. It can also braise/fry in the pot. Which I found fantastically convenient, since it means you can truly make a "one pot" dinner.OK, so for my first attempt at pressure cooking, I went with pulled pork. That's a full day enterprise using other methods. With the pressure cooker, it takes about 1 hour and 15 minutes. I took a 3 pound pork shoulder, added a few tablespoons of oil, browned both sides using the "browning fry"  setting, tossed in some bbq sauce and water, and let the "pressure cook" setting do its magic for 1:15. The machine talks to you (and plays celebratory music--cute), and at 1:15 it let me know everything was done. And it was! I took out the now fully cooked port shoulder, pulled it apart with two forks (could also use a food processor or blender for this), tossed the result back in the pot with part of the sauce that the shoulder had cooked with and a little more bbq sauce, brought it to a simmer using "browning fry" and served. The result: Perfection! (See photo of the original pork shoulder and the final result).It was really really easy and it worked just as it was supposed to. There was never any worry about "is it too soon to release the pressure?"This spoken by someone who has NEVER used a pressure cooker before.Some additional comments:1) It runs SUPER quiet. In fact, the only time I really knew that it was running is either when it said something to me (like "done") or when it was letting off steam. During most of the cooking process, you could forget it's running.2) The documentation could be a bit better about the various cooking modes. Some are obvious, such as "pressure cook", "slow cook", "browning fry" and "clean". (Yeah, it has a self-cleaning mode. Just add 3 cups of water and press start--very nice). But what exactly does "Multi Cook" do? It's never explained anywhere in the manual, and even online I couldn't find a straight answer.3) The parts that come in contact with the food (the bowl, the interior cover) are easily removable for cleaning. The bowl is non-stick on the entire surface (not just the bottom like the cheapo competitors like InstantPot) which is convenient.4) It's a fairly large appliance. Bigger than a bread machine, and it needs a decent amount of clearance above the cooker for the top to open.5) There are many safety interlocks and checks, along with automatic pressure valves that will let off steam if needed to keep the pressure from getting too high.6) Quality is top notch. Everything fits together with tight tolerances and everything works smoothly out of the box. This is made in South Korea, not China, and it's of the expected high quality.7) If you have never used one of these before (raises hand) expect to spend at least half an hour just familiarizing yourself with the cooker and reading through the fairly lengthy instructions.8) Cooking modes include: Pressure cooking, steam cooking, slow cooking, browning/frying.9) Specific food type modes include: soup, meat, vegetables, porridge, rice,10) There's also a "keep warm" mode for delayed serving. And you can set a timer so that cooking starts in the future (up to 12 hour delay).11) As a FYI, here's a list of sample food types for which the manufacturer provides cooking modes/settings. As you can infer, this is a jack of all trades: cauliflower and potato soup; french onion soup; ginger and butternut squash soup; black bean soup; baby back ribs; one pot beans, sausage and greens; teriyaki pork loin; eggplant and roasted red pepper dip; zucchini ratatouille; brown butter fingerling potatoes; horseradish mashed potatoes; breakfast porridge; purple yam barley porridge; jasmine rice; sausage and cheese frittata; mashed potatoes; chile Colorado; creme brulee; New England clam chowder; red lentil chili; steamed artichokes; crab legs; steamed ribs with glutinous rice; turnip cake; Korean steamed ribs; pomegranate sauce; sour cream; yogurt. And obviously the list can go on and on.12) The LCD screen is big and bright. There aren't that many buttons, and selection is mostly done by scrolling through the options on the LCD screen. It's pretty easy-to-use and intuitive--something that does not appear to be the case with many of the competitors.One the whole, this is one fantastic appliance that actually justifies the precious counter space it takes up. Highly recommended!

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2018
author: young
title: Automatic Steam Releaser, Non-stick Coating Stainless Steel Inner Pot, LED screen, Recipebook
body: I do not normally write reviews for any products I buy online, but hope that my review contributes and helps other people decide to purchase Cuckoo's multi-cooker with no doubt. I have been using Cuckoo's rice cookers for about 5 years, and their high quality  rice cookers have never let me down when it comes to making rice. I have compared Cuckoo's multi-cooker with other brands such as Instant pot and Crockpot but the reason that made me chose Cuckoo's product over others was the automatic steam release function for safety and high quality stainless inner pot for maximizing cooking quality. For most pressure cookers, one must manually push the steam cap to release the hot air inside the pot, and this tends to be very dangerous; however, that is not our concern for Cuckoo's Icook Q5 Premium. Especially in a house with kids like mine, this safety feature caught my attention, and I actually think that it is very convenient and helpful. Another thing is that its inner pot is made of non-stick coating stainless steel so that food do not get stuck on its surface or leave stains. Even the inside surface of the outer pot is made of the same material stainless steel as that of the inner pot, and I was actually surprised. My family recently moved into a new house and was looking for new kitchen appliances, and I think this multi pressure cooker would make our dinner table wonderful. It took two days to receive it after placing an order, and it also came with a cookbook which I did not expect. The cookbook looks actually very helpful and would come handy. I am so thrilled to finally get to use Cuckoo's multi pressure cooker.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2018
author: kateoh
title: Rice cooker and pressure cooker combined!
body: I own both the cuckoo pressure rice cooker and the Instant Pot. I used both for different dishes and decided to purchase this one to free up some counter space. The other two were taking up a LOT of room. So far, I've used this multi pressure cooker to make both my go-to rice (as pictured) and a chicken and vegetable dish. Both came out almost the same quality as my previous pressure cookers. I was happy and relieved to find that this muti cooker essentially became the only pressure cooker I need in our home.Pros:- EASY TO USE - I have had trouble using the Instant Pot, never knowing how long to cook and pressurize. But this multicooker takes the guess work out of it with already set functions and time. I also like that there is only one rice function. 30 minutes was the maximum time it allowed and the rice came out perfectly. (I add beans and squash to my rice if you're wondering about the picture)- MULTI- FUCTION - no need for separate rice cooker and pressure cooker! This device does it all, and frees up my counter space in the process.- BEAUTIFUL - clean, classic, white and rose gold - if only all my other kitchen appliances made me feel this good.- GREAT QUALITY - the nonstick surface makes clean up a breeze. Personally, I've never seen nonstick quality this good on other pots and pans.- interactive - lights, sounds, and whistles make the cooking experience feel more modern and luxurious. My son also enjoys helping out pushing the buttons.Cons:- BULKY - it doesn't have to be so bulky, but it is.- EXPENSIVE - but worth it, given that it serves multiple functions.
