body: I cooked steam rice today and I was really surprised how fast it was. The one I used to have which is zojirushi rice cooker had a ??fast?? function and it still took 45mins to cook rice completely. This machine not only allows you custom the menu setting you want to make but also timing.I wasn??t sure about this cooker so I just select ??rice?? and chose 20mins to see what happens.Amazingly it was cooked completely and tasted great! It was really fast and easy.What makes this cooker better than yours is that .. it talks ;)Can??t wait to try other food to make!!!!

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2018
author: Lukiemom
title: Meat comes out tender, juicy, and FAST!
body: We??ve been eyeballing a pressure cooker for a couple years and finally we got it!!  So far we made chili, rice, and beef stew. Meat came out so tender and juicy!! We left the food warm for the next day and it was still good.It was a bit tricky to figure out the cooking modes at first but it got easier once you get the hang of it.I basically set the cooker on ??browning fry?? at highest temp and throw in meat first then add the rest of ingredients. Then switch to the multi cook for 25mins at highest temperature.The pot heats up very fast and I actually liked the fact that we can control the heat level.

date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2018
author: Trang
title: Smart Technology, Versatile and Stylish - A Wonderful Multi-Cooker!
body: I've had the Cuckoo ICOOK Q5 Premium for about 2 weeks now and so far I like it more than my Instant Pot. With smarter technology, this multi-cooker is versatile, easy to use and stylish. The function buttons are simple and intuitive. Use just one "Menu" button to cycle through the different built-in programs: SOUP, MEAT, VEGETABLES, RICE, PORRIDGE, BROWNING FRY, MULTI COOK, STEAM COOK, SLOW COOK, AUTO CLEAN and then use the "Selection" button to adjust the cooking time. Press "Cook" to start...super easy!Two differentiating features intrigued me right away:#1 Auto Clean - releases a strong jet of steam to clean and sanitize the interior of the multi-cooker. I love this function and used it to sanitize my new cooker before cooking food for the first time. Since then I've used it multiple times.#2 Adjustable Keep Warm - lets you set the temperature from 154-176 degrees Fahrenheit. I like having the ability to adjust this and have found the static "Keep Warm" can be too hot for certain foods.Pressure cooking, steaming and cooking rice are the functions I use most and having all of these in one appliance is a huge win. I tested the "Rice" mode first and it cooked jasmine white rice perfectly in about 30 minutes. The unique feature to note about the "Rice" function is it pressure cooks and then steams the rice.Using the "Meat" mode, I pressure cooked 8 sticky rice and banana cakes (thick roll cakes) for 45 minutes and they were cooked perfectly. (If I had boiled these on the stove top, it would have taken about 1 1/2 - 2 hours, a huge time-savings.)I like the steam plate is included and not an accessory for additional purchase. So far I've steamed broccoli using Level 1 for 15 minutes and they came out soft but not mushy. My wish for the "Steam Cook" function would be an option to steam for less than 15 minutes. Some foods are delicate and don't require 15 minutes of steaming. The workaround is to cancel when the needed cook time has elapsed, not a huge inconvenience.I thought the 5-quart pot would be a bit small compared to my 6-quart Instant Pot. So far, it hasn't been an issue. I will have to make a few more family-sized recipes to weigh-in on this further. Perhaps Cuckoo will offer an 8 or even 10-quart multi-cooker size in the near future.I like the look and design of the Cuckoo ICOOK Q5 Premium Multi Cooker. It's an egg-shaped, minimalist design and quite attractive. It's a nice appliance to display on the kitchen counter.The Smart Voice Guide is novel. She announces what the multi cooker is doing like "Steaming is starting" and "Rice is complete". It's really cute and a great way to monitor cooking without having to watch it constantly.In addition to the instruction manual, the multi cooker comes with a 30-recipe cookbook. The recipes are easy and a great way to get acquainted with the cooker and enjoy some yummy dishes. There are also more recipes offered on the Cuckoo website.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2018
author: Amazon Customer
title: Versatile!
body: Ok. It's been a few weeks now and what can I say. It does everything as advertised and really feels like a premium product that will last! I would def recommend.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2018
author: Amazon Customer
title: The one pressure cooker you need to have
body: I have been a cuckoo user since college (as you can kind of see in the photo) and I sincerely trust their quality.Recently I have been looking at pressure cookers and slow cookers yet everything looked too bulky and the price varied so much I just could not decide. When I heard about this new Cuckoo product and saw the design, I had to buy it (mostly to make short braised ribs).The product includes steam tray, rice spoon (?) and measuring cup alongside with recipe book that thoroughly guides you step by step.I am not used to hearing Cuckoo speaking in English, but with the voice guidance, I did not have to read through every letter on my instructional book and successfully made my first Galbi Jjim! Super excited to make other dishes with this gem! :)The product is quite heavy and big!, FYI

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2018
author: Sora
title: simple and easy to use
body: The white sleek design with the rose gold finish looks absolutely beautiful on our countertop. I've been wanting to buy one for the longest time because you can cook a meal without feeling the need to constantly hover over the stove top. The multi-cooker also comes with a small 30-recipe book to help you get started. I recommend reading the instruction manual, because some of the features are hidden and require a bit of studying. It??s simple once you learn it but it??s not exactly a toaster.Once the set up was over, I was ready to cook the first meal. I picked the Korean Steamed Ribs from the recipe book. Normally this would take me about 5-6 hours to cook over a stove and that??s not including prep time, and of course I would be stuck in the kitchen, constantly checking on water/sauce level. But with the multi-cooker, I spent 1 hour soaking the ribs in water and 1 hour marinating, just as the recipe book stated. I did make one mistake because the recipe book clearly states to not add water to the sauce, but I did anyway because I always did. (Don??t add water. My sauce came out watery.) Now, the instructions state that I only needed to steam cook it for 50 minutes, but I found that it wasn??t tender enough. I cooked it for an additional 50 minutes and it was melt-in-your-mouth soft.Total Prep Time: 2 hoursMy recommended cooking time: 1 hr 40 mins on Level 4 Heat

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2018
author: Lee Abbas
title: Great pressure cooker
body: If you want to do more than cook rice, this is a great option for you. My kids were enchanted with the voice instruction and chime. My thirteen year old was able to use it no problem. She made buttery potatoes from the recipe book (picture attached). It made ultra-soft and flavorful potatoes in half the time it usually takes to make roasted/baked/boiled potatoes. I will definitely continue to cook all my potato dishes in it. I am also excited to try to make stews/chillis/soups, as those are usually very long and messy processes on the stove.I did have to experiment with the rice cooking function. I read all the previous reviews here. I tried the suggestion of "Steam Cook, Level 6 for 35 minutes" for Sticky rice. This result was too sticky for my taste. I tried with both equal amounts of rice and water and 1.25 ratio with slightly more water. Both times were same results (second picture in pot). I then used the RICE cook function at level 4 for 20 minutes with equal amounts rice water. This time the rice came out perfect (3rd picture in serving dish) - fluffy soft and just the right amount of stick. The cooker automatically adjusted the time for 36 minutes after it had pressurized - so that was nice that I did not have to guess at the right time.Clean up was amazingly simple. The nonstick surface was great and letting residue just slide off - even for rice! I have to say that this machine is a bit on the bigger size so be prepared to have counter space for it. On the positive side, because it is so beautiful, I don't mind keeping it out.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 28, 2024
author: onlytruemushroom
title: Powerful Suction! Blew My Mind!
body: First off I just want to say that this vacuum is astoundingly quiet. I have never owned a vacuum this quiet. You could have a regular volume conversation or watch TV while this vacuum is running and have no issues. It is lightweight too. Like obviously it‚Äôs not as light as a feather and my arm did get tired holding it after a while, but it wasn‚Äôt excessive and just switching hands back and forth made it not an issue. I consider myself to be pretty weak in the arms and I didn‚Äôt have much trouble. If you are EXTREMELY weak, elderly, disabled, or a literal child, you may have some issues. The average adult should not find this vacuum too heavy, though.Now for the part that blew my mind! After ordering this vacuum but before it arrived, I did a very thorough vacuum of my whole house with my old vacuum. I brushed stuff out from the walls, I got in all the crevices, under everything, made multiple passes. I considered it one last hurrah for my old vacuum, to really get some work in so that the new vacuum wouldn‚Äôt start it‚Äôs first day getting beat up with a deep clean. Well, when I got this new Cuckoo vacuum out, put it together, and started vacuuming, I immediately heard the distinctive clinking of stuff getting sucked up. ‚ÄúOh man‚Äù I thought, ‚ÄúThis vacuum is great. I just spent the weekend vacuuming the crap out of these carpets and this new vacuum is managing to pull more out.‚Äù I just quickly ran through the hallway and two bedrooms to see what it could do. I didn‚Äôt even get in all the corners, just ran it through the heavy traffic open areas. Literally about five minutes of vacuuming and this thing pulled out a wad of hair the size of a baseball and about 3/4 cup of pure dirt. My jaw absolutely dropped. ‚ÄúI just cleaned these carpets a few days ago!‚Äù I thought. ‚ÄúNo, you just cleaned them NOW. They were obviously still filthy before‚Äù I replied to myself.Needless to say I am thoroughly impressed, satisfied, obsessed, and living for this vacuum. It was so simple to put together, to use, and the instructions are straightforward enough that I believe it will be simple to maintain as well. Everything feels extremely high quality, snaps together with confidence, and works amazing. Also the lights on the front are so freaking bright you will be blinding all the dust bunnies under your couch. My ONE COMPLAINT is that you have to plug the vacuum into the dock to charge. You cannot charge just the battery (well, you can technically, by plugging the battery into the base of the dock‚Ä¶)Anyways I highly recommend this vacuum, I firmly believe it is worth the price for the quality and versatility. WAY better than my old vacuum and also way better than the corded push vacuum my mom bought just last year for about $80 less. Once again I am obsessed.

I‚Äôm stunned with how much dust, dirt and debris was pulled out of what I thought were fairly clean rugs.  Totally impressed with this machine! date: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2024

title: Best vacuum ever
body: I‚Äôm hoping this has a long lifetime because I like it better than the high end Dyson I paid 7x as much for.  It picks up more, it‚Äôs quieter and it‚Äôs lighter weight.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2024
author: C. Beth
title: Really nice vacuum
body: I really like this vacuum a lot. On Auto, it changes the suction as needed, or you can use the +/- buttons to manually change it.I love the stand that holds all of the attachments *except* the heavier little spin brush. (I wish it held that too!) I also totally love that I don't have to remove the battery to charge it. I just have to set the vacuum in the stand, and the battery charges. So cool.It has a place to put a spare battery that will also charge automatically, but I don't see anywhere to buy one (not even on their website). I'm hoping those are coming later.It works great and is very flexible. It can also be leaned back REALLY far to go under small spaces.My only big complaint is that it just feels heavy due to how much weight is in the very top, by the handle. My Hoover stick vac feels quite a bit lighter.Overall, though, this is a quality machine, and I love having it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 26, 2024
author: Jen
title: Great Quality! Works Well!
body: In love with this machine! It works so well! I'm impressed with the powerful suction. It easily glides from our carpet to the hardwood floors. I really like that it has an auto mode (or you can manually choose suction); so much easier than having to stop, switch modes from hardwood to carpet, like our other vacuum. I ordered this to have as an option that stays in our newly finished basement and for the stairs, but now I'm contemplating using it for the whole house! It was easy to assemble. It includes a charging rack, with the option of getting an additional battery for back up. I love that the charging rack includes an accessory storage clip; the accessories easily clip in. The only thing I would change is including a spot for the other roller vac to clip to, and maybe even a spot for replacement filters. Highly recommend!

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 16, 2024
author: johngriffin0928
title: Impressive, powerful
body: I've bought several vacuums in recent years that just don't pick up what's on the floor, whether it's carpet or, tile or hardwood. This vacuum far surpasses the others. Its suction is powerful. Its ease of use is commendable. It's battery is long lasting. I don't like vacuuming -- and bad vacuums made that all the more frustrating. Having a vacuum that actually works means my floors are cleaner than they have been, and that does make me happier.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 30, 2024
author: Shelley.1709
title: Powerful and lightweight!
body: This vacuum is the best of both worlds: powerful enough to get a lot of dirt up, and lightweight enough that its easy to operate.  Rounding out the positives of this vacuum are the fact that it's versatile with the included attachments, it's quiet and not loud enough to scare my dogs, and I can quickly and easily go from carpet to hardwood.I admit I had my doubts on how well this would work just based on it's size, weight, and my previous experience with "dust buster" rechargeable hand vacs, but this far exceeded my expectations!  I don't have to drag out a heavy vacuum, only to wrestle with the cord while using and change the settings by stopping and having to bend down to switch the setting on the base.  This one is literally one touch on, off, and to change the settings.The auto sense thing is pretty amazing too!  I have a combination of hard wood, throw rugs, and regular carpeting in my house.  I can easily and quickly go from one surface to another.  It has not scratched my wood floors, which I was nervous about, and it helps me sweep up anything that gets tracked in.The base is easily fitting in a corner of my kitchen and the ability to pop it off the base for sweeping and pop it back on the base for charging makes things so much easier.  No more dragging the big vacuum out of the closet.The attachments fit easily and work well for different messes.  I have two dogs and two grandchildren and this is working well for quick clean ups.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 5, 2025
author: Amelia
title: Emptying is a pain, customer service is less than helpful
body: Changing my review from 2 stars to 1 because a month later, the suction is now awful, the clogging problem has not gotten better, and Cuckoo customer service doesn‚Äôt seem to care that I got a lemon of a product.‚ÄîI‚Äôve had this vacuum for about five months. I was originally really excited to try it, because I‚Äôm a big fan of my Cuckoo rice cooker that I‚Äôve had for years.The suction is great. No issues there. The variety of attachments is nice. However, there‚Äôs one major design flaw.The vacuum holds very little debris‚Äîkind of expected‚Äîbut if you have pets, constant emptying is required. It takes so much effort to really clear out the debris. Simply popping open the can doesn‚Äôt work. Every time I empty it I have to get my hands dirty. Whether it‚Äôs taking it apart or just trying to knock some fur loose. I don‚Äôt fill it past capacity either. This happens regardless of how much is in there. For this reason, I regret my purchase.

body: This is a powerful vacuum, and the stand makes it easy to charge. It was quick and straightforward to set up and learn to operate. The one negative thing I can say about this vacuum is that it is heavy and awkward to use in some situations due to the long size and oddly distributed weight. I like how I can adjust the level of suction to fit the task. A well made machine that really cleans, but is heavy and bulky to use. For the money, this is a good vacuum cleaner.

body: For the price it‚Äôs so worth it. Comes with extra attachments to get in any craves of your home. It also comes with a stand or you can mount it which comes in handy to keep your place organized no need for a bulky vacuum when you can have this beauty in your home ü§©works amazing !

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2024
author: Calvin
title: Strong and portable vacuum
body: I was surprised to learn that CUCKOO had a vacuum option, as I had always known them for their rice cookers only, so I was excited to try this out.It's really lightweight, but very sturdy and surprisingly strong and powerful suction. I have a cheaper cordless vacuum at 1/3 of the price, but this thing blows it out of the water. It's a fantastic alternative to getting out a larger, corded vacuum that's a hassle to bring out and plug in.Coming with plenty of attachments such as a crevice tool and extender, this is really versatile and simple to maneuver around the house. This thing picks up dirt and dust like crazy, the latter of which accumulates pretty quickly if you have a house with a pet like I do.This vacuum is super high quality and I would definitely recommend trying this out.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 29, 2024
author: KBENNETT
title: Great cleaning power
body: This is easy to assemble. It has enough weight to feel like a well made substantial build quality while still being lightweight and easy to maneuver around the house. The included attachments make it easy to clean every surface of your house.  It is not overly loud which my dogs who hate the vacuum seem to appreciate.  The charging/storage stand is a great feature, your vacuum is always ready to go when you need it and the stand is compact enough to store the vacuum in about any space. I have multiple pets and this does an amazing job of cleaning up the pet hair so I can highly recommend this model for pet owners. It works on multiple surfaces and carpet and rug heights with a seamless transition between surfaces. The dust canister is easy to empty and the battery lasts long enough to clean my entire home without needing to recharge. This is a great vacuum!

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 5, 2025
author: Mark K
title: BEST stick vac out there!  - just not as powerful as you really need.
body: This is a great little light weight vacuum cleaner - here is what you need to know.  Dont get this if you have a large house and expect to do the whole thing.  It will run 35 minutes if it isn't sucking much dirt on low... but I always turn it up to high and it will only go about 10 mins on high.  I've ordered a back up battery.  I do love that you can have a charged and charging back up battery ready to go and swap them out.  I have owned two others and this is by far the best - when it comes to taking it apart to empty and clean the filters /  unclogging the floor sweep rotar, etc.  I'd give it 5 stars for all that.  Excellent attachments too, as well as a wonderful stand to hold it (charging stand - for both batteries!  Love that!  I just can't go 5 stars because it doesn't in ANYWAY compare to the power and force of my electric Riccar Vacuum.  I didn't expect it to - but just know - this is for small jobs (small apartment) or just using it 10 mins at a time - a little every day, maybe.  OH - and it is wonderful for cruising around the floor - wiggling under and around the legs of chairs.  I could even go 3 feet under out low bed and all the way under our 5" high off the floor bed.  No big vac will do that.  Again - I would say this is the best out there - for stick vac... but is it all you need?  No - you still need an electric powerful vacuum to really clean good.

date: Reviewed in the United States on May 3, 2025
author: Denny
title: Good vaccum
body: Love this vaccum..Has great suction and easy to clean..Love the fact the filters are washable..Just wish you can order extra just in case

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 7, 2024
author: William's Syndrome Mama
title: CUCKOO Upright Powerclone Cordless Vacuum
body: I've needed a good vacuum for awhile and this one is so very nice! I like that the base where it stands is how it charges, no need to keep track of a specific plug and the base holds it upright so it's not slip sliding all over the room without a home. So very nice. The machine itself is quite light and gets in and out of most nooks and crannies for me without much trouble. I can go under the couch, around the dogs, etc. It DOES fill up pretty quick but I've also got a dog that's part lab and part shepherd so that's on me. As long as it's emptied frequently it works like a dream. If you let it get clogged up it's a bit of a hassle to get it all pulled out but it's possible to do without taking it apart. The dust basin is easy to empty. The suction power is pretty good overall. It's a good vacuum! I'm glad I got it. Would buy this again.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 17, 2024
author: JL
title: Awesome Vacuum
body: Awesome Vacuum! It works really well, the suction is good and cleans the carpet very well. Does it job!

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 14, 2024
author: Wendy Franks
title: Powerful Little Vacuum
body: This vacuum is great for all around your home. It's light, so it's easy to move from room to room. It boasts a 38 minute battery life, which is enough time to get through most of my home, though I wish it was a bit longer so  I could really deep clean without having to charge. It's incredibly quiet, which is great for animals who are scared of loud noises. Overall, a really good vacuum!

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 4, 2024
author: Corey A. Balazowich
title: Pretty impressive!
body: This little sucker is pretty impressive for what it is.Does it compare in power/suction to our corded Miele? No.It is easier to grab and use, lighter and easier to use? Yes.While it's not the complete powerhouse and good enough to yeet all the other vacuums we have into the sun, it does a great job for what it is! it's meant to be a quick way to suck up stuff when you're not committed to a making a whole THING of it and it does great!My only gripe is that the smaller roller head doesn't attach to the stand and it kind of seems like a dumb design to not have a place for that attachment but have one for literally everything else.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 29, 2024
author: LynLivesHerBestLife
title: Great
body: I love this vacuumed it‚Äôs really highly made and great quality if you need a cheap vacuumed cleaner this is what you need I recommend buying.

title: Vaccuum sucks because it does not suck
body: This vacuum does not work on dog hair or sand; it just pushes the debris around and clogs easily.  Very frustrating. If the return window had not closed to quickly I would have returned this.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2025
author: Dmad
title: Vac
body: Great

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 3, 2024
author: PrettyFly
title: High Power Portable Fan!
body: The Fan set includes a carry case, the fan unit, power cord, a remote, and instructions.The fan has a manual toggle switch on the back.  Once turned on, the fan LED display shows, and you can start the fan.The fan has a lot of features for it's portability - including oscillating fan movement, changing speeds, setting timers, and more.  The included remote is very handy, as it is magnetic and circular in shape.  This allows it to be magnetically attached to the front of the fan when not in use.One of my favorite features of the fan is it's versatility.  It can remain folded down for compact use, or the back pole extended to make it a "floor fan".  The fan head can be tilted up and down as well, so you can adjust for the best comfort.I am very happy with the fan speed/power - especially considering it is running on batteries.  The rechargeable feature would be great for camping or outdoor events - but I personally like that it can be moved around the house without the need for power cords.  Hot in the bathroom when getting ready?  Get the fan.  Kitchen hot while cooking?  Easy to grab the fan.When not in use, everything folds up and fits in the included carry case.Not something that I would remove a star for, but a couple of suggestions for the future:- It would be nice if the fan came in other colors.  The fan would look great in black.- It would be nice if you could charge the unit via USB-C instead of the barrel plug connector.  I can see myself loosing the plug, and USB C would be much easier to replace.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 18, 2024
author: Melinda
title: Portable standing fan
body: This fan is about 2.5 to 3 foot tall collapsible and portable. It has 12 different speed settings and a rotate setting. I unplugged it for 4 hours the other day and it didn‚Äôt die!! The battery life is great.

date: Reviewed in Australia on December 17, 2024
author: Jin
title: Excellent little fan
body: Little but powerful. Very happy for this project.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 7, 2024
author: robochick
title: Smaller than expected, the entire base rotates!
body: A few things that stood out to me that I'm not so fond of. When you enable the fan oscillation mode the entire 12 inch diameter base rotates generally when I'm camping in the summer space is of the essents and not being able to put/store things near the fans base is troublesome. Next the fan seems quite small for the overall value of the purchase for the size it is quite but it does not output the force that louder fans put out of the same size. Another issue the translation of the words on the remote are poor, practically the words "Voice", "Swing", and "Light" were a bit confusing. I thought maybe is this fan really voice activated??? I thought the fan might have a night light??? nope and nope. And Finally why can't they of used a USB C charging cord! the charger is a weak 2 amp 24 watt 8 hour charging time adapter absolutely horrid.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 29, 2024
author: Amazon Customer
title: Modern and functional
body: Not necessarily a powerhouse in terms of wind speed, but its compact and portable design sets this fan apart from most. Its cordless operation creates an opportunity for convenience when outlets aren't available. It can run for about 30 hours on a single charge. You still have the option of plugging it into a standard outlet, too.I take it with me outside to my patio where no outlets are accessible. That in itself is worth it. It's great for camping purposes, traveling, or for everyday household use. The swing feature on this fan is pretty cool also. The base rotates and moves the entire fan. It's very unique and modern in that sense.There are 12 levels of wind speed. It lacks a little in this area. Even though there are 12 speeds, I can't feel much of a difference between some of them. Also, I would have assumed obtaining a stronger breeze with applying the top wind selection, but it is very average. I guess I've been a little spoiled with high velocity fans and how powerful they are. Regardless, this fan isn't bad, but its wind speed is equivalent to probably a box fan.You can adjust the height of the fan to accommodate the space you're using it in. It makes a great bedside fan and comes equipped with a remote for full access to all of its functions. I like that it can be completely folded down and stored inside the included storage bag when not in use. It makes it easier for transporting and general storage.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 29, 2024
author: Mr reviewer
title: 10 stars
body: This is a portable folding air wonder has exceeded all my expectations. Its cordless and portable design means you can take it anywhere without any hassle.I love the 12 speeds, the fan tilt adjustment when unfolded, the battery life, the remote control. This fan is great for those summer days when I don't want to rely on air conditioning.The fan is very quiet, which makes it perfect for use while I sleep or work. It's also easy to fold and store, which saves me space.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 5, 2024

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 25, 2024
author: HonestReviews
title: Time Saver!
body: UPDATE BELOW OLD REVIEW (14 OCT 2024)___________________________________________I generally make bread a couple times a week, which, unfortunately, can take hours out of your day each time.  Although it can be time-consuming, the results are worth it.  I don't eat grocery store bread anymore because it tastes - off, in a bad way - compared to a good homemade bread recipe.Using this bread maker removes any remaining major complaints I have about making my own bread.  It literally takes a couple minutes to measure out the flour and put the rest of the ingredients in before you just hit a few buttons and walk away.  The bread comes out tasting great and perfectly baked, and more importantly, it's insanely easy to clean.  Just pull out the mixer blade from the bottom and clean everything out really quick and it's ready to be stored (after it cools down).The only negative I can say about this is that because it's a machine, you can't expect the quality of the bread to be as good as if it's done by hand.  Kneading the dough by hand, such as in the case of sandwich bread, will produce a nice, spongy texture that is uniform and pleasantly chewy.Using this machine will produce bread that still tastes great and has a nice crust, but the texture is more porous, and due to the mixer blade at the bottom during baking, you'll end up with an indentation at the bottom of the loaf.  It's a tradeoff, though.  If you want the machine to do the work, you'll sacrifice a little quality but get back a lot of time.Besides that, the only other complaint that I have is that during the mixing process the machine can start knocking loudly for a few seconds.  It's not a big deal.  Startles the cat sometimes, but he's gotten used to it, so it's fine.The build quality is great, the machine itself doesn't get too hot to touch, and more importantly, it doesn't noticeably heat up the house like the oven does.  I'm in Arizona so that's a big plus in the summer.I hope this thing lasts a long time because I plan on using it for a long time.___________________________________________UPDATE:After learning how to use it more, I've been able to achieve a nice, soft, pretty much non-porous sandwich bread in the machine that I don't feel is a big compromise in anyway.  Updated to five stars, mainly for the convenience.Also, here's my recipe:- Machine setting 1 (Basic, 3:10 hours)- Light Crust setting- 1 1/3 cup cold milk (to help keep rise down a little and get a better shape - warmer for more rise)- 1/4 cup oil (vegetable, canola - your preference)- 450g bread flour on top of oil/milk, put pit in middle for yeast (shallow enough to not get down to liquid)- 1.5 tsp instant yeast in the flour pit- 1.5 to 3 Tbsp sugar in one corner (taste preference, could affect rise)- 1.5 tsp salt in another corner- BakeTry to avoid cutting into the bread for an hour so after it's done so it truly finishes the bake.  I fail at that every time personally, but still.  Top texture and flavor comes after it hits roughly room temperature.Enjoy!

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2025
author: Ann
title: Love love love
body: It's really THAT easy to make bread.Beginner baker here +the machine makes you look like a pro! Nothing to fancy or confusing. BUT still makes alot of awesome stuff. I haven't found 1 recipe I wasn't able to make in this machine!You put all of the ingredients in the machine, no need to mix, stir...nothing. +it mixes on its own.No need to set a timer, even if you have add ins (like raisins, nuts, fruit, etc) that come in after so long of baking. +you literally selet the type of bread and the desired darkness and the machine takes it from there. +the built in drop down box will open at the precise time the add-ins are needed.Don't stress over the mess +because there isn't one. The baking square is non stock and I mean FOR REAL not nonstick like all pots and pans claim they are. This machine is super easy to clean!10 stars

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2024
author: Laurel S.
title: Good features for a mid-priced bread machine
body: Well we needed a new bread machine, since one of our girl's has a gluten allergy our old bread maker went away.  We specifically ordered this one because it has a gluten free setting.The Cuckoo bread maker was simple to use and the directions were clear and easy to follow. I made a gluten free loaf and used the GF setting - fun to have that option programmed in! I also think it‚Äôs nice that you can choose your preferred shade of golden for the crust- lots of ways to customize your breads. There is a nifty little gadget to pull the paddle out of the bottom of your loaf when it gets stuck in there. I never would have thought of that, but it was indeed needed. I took the bread to my Supper Club and got compliments.  I‚Äôll definitely experiment with recipes, but for my first try, I was happy with the way it turned out.*Note-I received this product through the Amazon Vine program  at a significantly reduced cost (I pay taxes on this item, so it is not totally free) in exchange for my honest review and opinion/experience with the item.*After reviewing hundreds of items, this is how I generally rate the products I receive. I strive to be fair and honest, rather than overly enthusiastic just because I received it through Vine. I try to base my review on how I would feel about it if I had paid full price:*5 Stars - I really love it!  It might even be better than described, or a better design or quality than similar items.  It is a good value, I would not hesitate to pay full price for the item for myself or for a gift and highly recommend it.*4 Stars - I like it a lot, it is accurately described, and in comparison to similar items in the same price range seems to be a good value.  It might not be perfect or knock my socks off, but I would consider paying full price for the item for myself or for a gift.*3 Stars - The product is as described, average quality compared to similar items, and priced fairly.  It might not be as well designed as similar items.  It probably has a lot of competition and there might be better choices, I would shop around but might purchase if the price was right.*2 Stars - The product is well below my expectations for the price and quality compared to similar items, has significant issues or damage or poor quality control.  I would not spend my money on it and would recommend you seriously consider the flaws before doing so yourself.*1 Star - This would be a very bad product (perhaps bad customer service on top of it).  This is a product I would throw away and not even donate, and would recommend you do not purchase.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 25, 2024
author: LT
title: Easy to use, the "do it all for you" bread maker, great for dough
body: I enjoy making Sourdough Soft Asian bread with various fillings, like sausage buns. The most challenging part is always the dough-making process, which I find tedious due to the time it consumes, even though I own a reliable Kitchenaid mixer that can handle large batches. However, when I only need to make a small amount, I prefer using an automatic bread maker like Cuckoo. Cuckoo is a known Korean brand name and reputable in kitchen products. I also own another Cuckoo rice cooker and they works great.The menu is user-friendly, offering numerous preset options. My initial attempt at making dough was a triumph! By the time I wake up in the morning, the dough is perfectly risen and ready inside the bread maker. I simply shape it, wrap it around the sausage, let it rise again for 10-15 minutes, bake it for another 12 minutes, and voil√† ‚Äì freshly baked bread for the family in just 30 minutes.I highly recommend it!

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 11, 2024
author: Amazon Customer
title: Nice looking versatile bread maker
body: I have had a Cuisinart bread maker for over 10 years.  Although it still works, it is getting rusty in places inside, so I decided to replace with this stainless steal model.  I like the lower profile of this unit, and the versatility that allows you to use it for other things, such as yogurt.  However the versatility also makes it a little more complicated to operate and select the correct settings.  I think it will come easier the more I use it.  I am short, so when it is sitting on my counter I can not watch the progress unless I use my phone's camera to see what the readings are.  I wish the screen was closer the the front edge, and the buttons for selecting functions was in the back so I could easily see the progress.  I used my regular recipe for bread and the loaf came out perfectly cooked and browned.  I am excited about this new unit.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 19, 2024
author: Dr. T
title: Versatile, works very well, makes good bread, but loaves are inconvenient size for sandwiches
body: I was highly impressed by this bread machine and its variety of settings. The first loaf didn't have enough liquid in the dough (I used my handmade loaf recipe) so it came out very dense, but a few tweaks to the amounts of ingredients, and I am now getting lots of compliments on the bread I'm baking.Any bread machine will take some getting used to, this one seemed to have a much shorter learning curve than I expected. Bread comes out with excellent crumb (that's the part of the loaf that isn't crust) and flavor. The crust seems a bit dark and thick, even on the lightest setting. But I like crusts anyway.Two things that aren't my favorite: first, the "Home made" program allows customization of each step, but it doesn't retain the settings, so it has to be reprogrammed each time. This substantially detracts from its utility because it takes a while to program and the user interface takes some getting used to. Second, while the loaves are beautiful and perfect, they are the wrong size for sandwich slices. They make fabulously huge french toast but not so practical for lunches. This is a "feature" of many bread machines these days, not unique to the Cuckoo.How I'm using this machine now, mostly, is the dough program, and then baking in a Pullman loaf pan. Maybe someday I will get a Bosch mixer for making large batches, but for single loaves this machine is perfect.Four out of five stars because of the loaf geometry and the volatile memory on the custom program, but I would definitely order again.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2024
author: Amazon Shopper
title: Tbh I haven't used it a lot, but so far good results!
body: I have always wanted to get a bread maker, and was intrigued by this one mentioningni could make homemade yogurt! That was a big selling point for me, but I have had a crazier than expected life lately and haven't gotten around to trying that. I have managed to test this out making a couple of batches of bread, and so far I love this machine! It seems to be good quality, is a bit hard to get the hang of without the manual at your side initially, but with the instructions, is fairly easy to use and produced pretty good results for a beginners first attempts. I found it easier to clean than I expected, and appreciated that as well. Overall, especially if I can make decent whole milk homemade yogurt, I found this to be a good value. I have not tried any Gluten free specific recipes but assume it would work as well as any regular gluten recipe in this bread machine. In my limited experience, I would say the manual is your best friend and if you take the time to really read and follow instructions you can expect decent results and save a lot of time. I also appreciated that running this bread maker didn't make my kitchen as hot as baking a loaf of bread in my regular oven. I don't know why, or how, but it was an additional added bonus this summer fore, since I was worried it would be too hot to bake during the heat. I'm unsure if I can get more uniform "store bought" sized and shaped loafs with this machine but that is on my list of things to continue tweaking to try for. So far with my limited used ofmthisnmachineni am pretty impressed, and very pleased with it. I have nothing to comparemit to since it is my first bread maker, but I do like it so far! I also think it is pretty compact and since I have very little counter space, I also like that!

date: Reviewed in Canada on May 19, 2025
author: Spunky
title: Doesn't mix well.
body: I have made 4 loave of bread from the recipe  book provided.  I found anything over 1 1/2 loaf size depending on the recipe would rise too high to meet the top of the bread maker.  The idea of the fruit and nut dispenser is great.  Works well.  Like the preset program numbers.  They are very coinvent.  Most of the breads were very good.  A couple got a little high and pointy in the middle.  When cooking it almost smelled like it was burning despite nothing spilled in the machine.  The dough setting worked well for buns I was making in the oven.  The reason I am giving it a lower rating is because when you follow the directions on how to put stuff in the machine and start the cycle - many things stuck to the side of the pan.  Flour was not mixing in properly and wasn't forming a smooth even ball.  I had to go in with a spatula and push the stuff stuck into the mixture.  Once cooked on 2 of the loaves I found a little flour residue not mixed properly on the bottom of the loaf.  The mixing paddle in most cases stayed in the pan when I tipped the loaf out which was good and not stuck in the bread.  I am not sure how you could fix the mixing issue.  The breads come out more bread shape then other bread makers that have them look like towers.  It is okay for the price but I would want to watch it start up to make sure it mixing everything properly.  It seems to take longer then my older bread maker that took 3 hours for basic bread start to finish.  This one takes between 4 -5 hours.

date: Reviewed in the United States on April 29, 2025
author: joseph
title: Decent Bread Maker, but Not Without Its Flaws
body: I bought the CUCKOO Bread Maker Machine because I wanted an easy and flexible way to make homemade bread without all the manual kneading and guesswork. After using it consistently for about a month, I feel it‚Äôs a decent machine that gets the job done most of the time, but there are enough small frustrations that I can only give it three stars overall.First, the positives: The CUCKOO bread maker has a sleek, modern design that looks good on the kitchen counter. It‚Äôs relatively compact compared to some older bread machines I‚Äôve owned, so it doesn‚Äôt dominate the entire space. The control panel is intuitive, with clearly labeled buttons and a nice digital display that makes selecting programs easy.The 15 menu options are a definite highlight. I appreciated the variety ‚Äî from basic white and whole wheat breads to dough, cake, and even jam. The ability to choose from three different crust colors (light, medium, and dark) is also a nice touch and gives a good amount of customization depending on your personal preferences. I found the medium crust setting generally gave the best balance between texture and flavor.The machine runs fairly quietly and has a decent non-stick bread pan that makes it easy to remove the finished loaf without a fight. Clean-up afterward is simple ‚Äî a quick wipe and rinse, and you‚Äôre done.However, despite these good features, the CUCKOO bread maker has a few significant drawbacks:1. Inconsistent Results:While it produces decent bread most of the time, it‚Äôs not particularly consistent. I followed the same recipe exactly on different days and got noticeably different results ‚Äî one loaf would rise beautifully, and another would come out dense and undercooked. It feels like the internal temperature control isn‚Äôt always spot-on, which can lead to unpredictable outcomes. For beginners or anyone who expects a ‚Äúset it and forget it‚Äù machine, this can be frustrating.2. Weak Kneading Power:The kneading function seems a little underpowered, especially with heavier doughs like whole wheat or doughs that require a lot of elasticity. It struggles with larger batches too. I noticed that sometimes the dough wasn‚Äôt fully incorporated and had to pause the machine, open the lid, and manually adjust or push the dough around to ensure even kneading.3. Limited Customization on Some Programs:Even though there are a lot of presets, you can‚Äôt always fine-tune the settings. For example, you can‚Äôt always adjust kneading or rising times individually, which would have been really helpful for adapting recipes or accounting for different flour types or humidity levels in the kitchen.4. The Manual Could Be Better:The included manual is basic and doesn‚Äôt explain some important things like troubleshooting uneven loaves or adjusting recipes. There‚Äôs no guidance on ingredient order, substitutions, or what to do if you live at a high altitude ‚Äî things that can really affect baking success. I had to rely on trial and error or look up tips online rather than being able to trust the manual alone.Summary:The CUCKOO Bread Maker Machine is a stylish and mostly capable kitchen tool that will definitely work for casual home bakers looking for fresh bread without too much effort. It offers a nice range of settings, looks good, and is easy to clean. However, due to the inconsistencies in performance, weak kneading on heavy doughs, and lack of customization flexibility, it falls short of being a truly outstanding machine.I still use it regularly and enjoy the bread it makes when it turns out well ‚Äî but I wouldn‚Äôt recommend it for serious bakers or anyone looking for absolutely foolproof results. Solid mid-tier bread maker if you catch it on sale, but don‚Äôt expect professional bakery-level results.Three stars.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2025
author: LRM
title: Not great
body: My son in law bought this bread machine as a Christmas present for me.  The very first time I used it, the nut/fruit dispenser didn‚Äôt work.  No biggie, the other machine I had didn‚Äôt have one either.  Today I set everything up to make a loaf, and it didn‚Äôt work.  I did all the troubleshooting from the manufacturer manual and nothing.  Not even 2 months and it just died.  It‚Äôs a great machine, easy to use, mixes well and has versatility.  Price is great, but spend a little more and get yourself a well known brand that will last you awhile.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 20, 2024
author: Mary E. West
title: Makes perfect sized loafs for our small family
body: This is our third bread maker, so we are familiar with the process.  We still have one other bread maker, but we ordered the Cuckoo because it makes the loaf sizes that fit our needs now and has the additional feature of the Automatic Fruit & Nut Dispenser. We initially found the programming of the Cuckoo bread maker a little confusing and we still need to refer to the manual when we are trying different options.  It would have been nice if the manufacturer had included some recipes in the manual. The bead that we have made in the Cuckoo has always come out beautifully, but a little denser than I prefer, but tweaking the recipe may fix that issue.  We recently tried making cinnamon raisin bread using the Automatic Fruit Dispenser and that didn't go as well as we had hoped.  For some reason only about 10 to 20% of the raisins ended up in the bread, the rest was either still in the dispenser or outside of the baking pan.  We did not flour the raisins as suggested in the manual, which might fix that issue.While the bread loafs a larger than most standard size bread, this seems to be common with any bread maker we have had or reviewed.  While we are still learning how to use the Cuckoo Bread Maker, overall we are pleased with it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 19, 2024
author: Ting
title: A good addition to my kitchen
body: I have never used or seen anything like this bread maker. I bought this to try it out just for fun, and it is amazing. It has a lot of different things you can control, including type, crust color, etc. The bread comes out nice and fluffy. I like it so much and would recommend it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 6, 2024
author: Matt
title: Works well for basic bread, but feels a bit flimsy
body: This is a decent bread maker. It is easy to set up and the instructions are pretty clear because it doesn't do much more than make basic bread. It is a bit louder than others I've used.  It kneads the dough well and bakes a nice loaf. It doesn't feel very sturdy. The top is a bit flimsy and you have to be careful when you pull the basket thing out. Overall, it does work, but doesn't have many bells and whistles like some of the better bread makers we've used.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2024
author: LeanneP
title: Gluten free mode really works, makes regular bread well, too.
body: I am very impressed with the gluten-free option on this machine. I used it for our holiday meal making both ‚Äúregular‚Äù and gluten-free breads and it worked flawlessly for both. This was something I was almost expecting to be a gimmick, but thankfully it isn't. The machine works very well and does all of the work for you after adding the ingredients. It is very easy to use and equally easy to clean. I haven't found any issues of concern with this at all and would definitely recommend it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2024
author: John P Nimitz
title: Really awesome bread maker
body: We've used the CUCKOO CBM-AAB101S 2 lb. Multi-Functional Bread Maker a few times since the purchase.  We wanted to give it some time to use and make sure it wasn't a dud!  Great machine, follow the instructions on any pre-mixed breads! Some cannot use a bread maker.  From scratch this is really great maker and creates soft warm delicious bread for us to enjoy!  From sour dough to standard white, so great!  Some reported this arrived damaged, ours did not and we have enjoyed it for the past few months!  I cannot wait to use this when it is winter to fill the house with warm bread scent.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 29, 2024
author: Lisa Z
title: Used it twice so far.
body: I absolutely love this bread maker!  The instructions are clear and easy to understand, and make the bread maker very easy to use. The bread maker has quite a few different settings‚Äî and recipes to match! I‚Äôve made the basic bread and the sweet bread so far, and both turned out great! I followed the recipes exactly and it did not steer me wrong. I am sure that I will experiment with some recipes somedays, but first I have to try all of the recipes that came with the bread maker!

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 20, 2024
author: Brett Combs
title: The Bread Maker That Delivers on Price, Quality and Convenience
body: I recently ordered and received this bread maker, and it's been a fantastic addition to my kitchen.What really stands out to me, beyond the great pricing, is the Crust Color Customization. Whether I'm in the mood for a lighter crust or something with a bit more crunch, I can dial it in perfectly. The option to choose between three different loaf sizes is also a nice touch, especially when I'm baking for just myself or a group.The Automatic Fruit & Nut Dispenser is another feature I love‚Äîit‚Äôs incredibly convenient. No more hovering around the kitchen waiting for the perfect moment to add my mix-ins. It takes care of that for me, ensuring everything is evenly distributed during the kneading process.One of the best parts? The Quick Bake Time. This machine shaves off up to 20 minutes compared to others I‚Äôve used, which means fresh bread faster‚Äîwhat‚Äôs not to love? The bread comes out bakery-quality every time, thanks to the wide-shaped pan that ensures even heat distribution. The texture is spot on‚Äîsoft inside with a perfectly crispy crust.It also comes with all the essentials: a product manual, measuring cup & spoon, nonstick bread pan, paddle hook, and kneading paddle. Everything I need is right in the box, making it super easy to get started.And to top it all off, the unit itself is an attractive stainless steel, which adds a sleek, modern look to my kitchen.Overall, this bread maker is a fantastic find because of its price and its features.  If you are in the market for a great bread machine, give this one a try.

date: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2024
author: Lori Leal
title: So Happy To Have A Bread Maker!
body: I've wanted a good bread maker for so long, and I was super excited to find this one. I love that it's so easy to use and that it has so many cool features. I haven't had a chance to use a lot just yet, but so far, I love it! It's easy to use, it's definitely quality made, it's quiet when running and easy to clean. So far it's capabilities are what I wanted and it's working great. Highly recommend it because for the price it's a steal!

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 19, 2024
author: Dave Stanzito
title: Turns an amateur into a baker (not really but it makes great bread)
body: We had 2 older bread makers that the manuals and other parts were lost when moving in. Those went to the curb today.  We lucked out on this as ours did not arrive broken like others have reported.  It really is as simple as everyone says to make bread with this thing.  Dump in the ingredients (follow the instructions), hit the button, it does the rest.  And who doesn't love fresh baked bread?  This will be getting A LOT of use at our house.Highly recommended item.

date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2024
author: jubaby
title: Fresh homemade bread
body: This bread maker machine is very easy to use. I am using the bread maker machine for the very first time but still it was very easy to use. All the steps and recipes were written in detail and easy to use details. Its easy as a breeze to use.

date: Reviewed in the United States on June 22, 2024
author: Jazz Person
title: This is my first experience with a bread machine‚Äîand I'm hooked
body: I've never made bread before‚Äîwith or without a bread machine‚Äîso I have a lot to learn. But the instructions for this compact little machine are clear and well written; so with a little study, I'm learning how to use it. And it is easy to use once you understand the simple programming.The owner's manual includes basic recipes that are easy to follow, and my first attempt was a small whole wheat loaf. I won't post a picture, because it's hilariously lumpy and misshapen. We all laughed at it‚Äîand then couldn't stop eating because it tasted so good. It was just like you'd want a rustic, whole-grain loaf to be. Crusty but not tough. Dense, but not heavy. It was a great first try and I learned from it (next time I'll add a little more liquid to the dough).This machine is simple, easy to assemble and use, and easy to clean. It's a nice size and doesn't hog too much space on my counter. The owner's manual is informative and well laid out. I'm looking forward to baking more and better loaves of bread with it. Recommended.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 6, 2023
author: Christie M.
title: Not as convenient as I hoped.
body: I use a lot of hot water throughout the day, mainly for tea but I also occasionally drink instant coffee and make broth for recipes. I was looking forward to having hot water at my fingertips throughout the day with this dispenser, but it hasn't been the convenience that I've hoped for. The dispenser holds five liters which is a lot and it takes a very, very long time to come to a boil. Even when I fill it less than half way the boiling time is much longer than any electric kettle I've ever owned. The keep warm feature is nice, but I like my water boiling so I still have to re-boil it when I come back. The machine also doesn't empty all of the water when dispensing, no matter how much I run it I still have to flip the machine over to get all of the water out of the bottom. I'm sure this is useful for some people, but I personally wouldn't buy it again.

However, once I received the return QR code by email, the refund total is discounted and excluded the sales tax Iâ€™ve paid. Amazon, can you tell me why?

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2024
author: Froufrou Paperie
title: Great design, effective heating/dispensing but really large for us
body: I had to replace my other smaller unit when it stopped dispensing just over 1 yr. This unit holds 5L and is quite large for our space. Pros: design, functionality, easy to clean, valueCons: the button sensitivity is a little off-it requires you to touch them with the right amount of pressure, length of time and placement of finger. Once you know, itâ€™s fine but, our guests all needed to be shown.Also, the lid doesnâ€™t quite fit streamlined with the unit which only I notice.Otherwise, this is a really nice unit for people who grew up with the standard airpots in their parentsâ€™ kitchens. (We donâ€™t need the swivel function and actually prefer that it stays put).

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2024
author: Random Reviewer
title: Nice hot water dispenser with a few design flaws.
body: There are several features I like about this hot water dispenser and several features I wish were different.Things I like:1. I found the device easy to use and set up and use directly out of the box and only referred to the manual to review the set-up requirements and how to clean the device.2. Lock/Unlock feature.3. Dispenser feature.4. Keep warm setting.5. Auto turn off after boiling if a keep warm option has not been chosen.6. Touch button panel.Things I wish were different:1. The lid is not removable and the hinge feels flimsy allowing the lid to shift slightly left to right. I worry that it will fail with continued use.2. I don't like the location of the lid release handle. It's directly over the reservoir so if you need to open the lid while the water is hot there is no way to avoid the steam so you need to wear an oven mitt.3. I don't like the location of the plug. This is a pretty large appliance and it's going to take up a substantial amount of counter space. Putting the plug in the back center causes this dispenser to sit several inches forward from the wall or to be placed in a corner to compensate for this.4. Length of time to boil even smaller quantities. I didn't notice much of a difference in time between bringing a full reservoir vs a half reservoir to a boil.5. See through the reservoir panel. I wish it was larger because I find it to be dark and hard to read. I have to put my phone flashlight on to see it.I'm not sure if this hot water dispenser is as good or exceeds other brands but I do think it performs well enough to be considered when shopping for one. There are some flaws but they do not impact this dispenser's performance.

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2023
author: MM
title: Love this even more than I thought I would
body: I drink a LOT of tea, and hot water means I have to boil a lot of it. I have a fabulous kettle that has been doing very well, but however silly, the sound of the water heating and reheating really bothers me while I'm on my zoom calls. So, I was curious how this unit would be, with the ease of having hot water at any moment, without having to wait for the kettle to boil.The unit comes in its own box and is well packaged with foam. It is a bit over a foot high, so it's a decent size, and holds 5 liters of water. It's suggested in the accompanying leaflet (yes I actually read those!) to fill the unit up with water (to the fill line) and let it boil twice, and then discard that water, I assume to boil off any manufacturing residue. I did this, but found I needed to do another big fill / boil off, to get the water to taste clean.But once that is done, it works amazingly well! I was a bit concerned that the water would not be hot enough for my tea, but it's great! And I love the ease of just walking to the dispenser, push the "unlock" button and then the "dispense" one, and get HOT water instantly. (be aware that the water is indeed hot. Initially I had my cup a bit too low, which means that some drops spilled on my hand and that was a bit painful. Simply raising my cup to closer below the spout stopped this from happening)If I have to find one fault, it's that the lid, when opened, can be wiggled a little from side to side on the hinge. It does seem sturdy enough for normal, regular use.I am happy with this dispenser!

date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2023
author: Brittany R.
title: Great Safety Features
body: Overall, I'm really happy with this hot water dispenser. The instructions recommend that you fill it with 5 liters of water, boil it twice, and then dump it before using it the first time. This isn't necessarily a fast boiler. It took about 20 min to boil 5 liters. I like the steam vent on the top that's set near the back of the lid (less risk for a small child reaching up and getting burned by the steam. It's also much quieter than my electric kettle. Once the water is ready, you have to push the unlock button (it lights up when it's unlocked) before you can dispense the water. I really appreciate this safety feature since the water is so hot and could burn an unsuspecting child that is just pushing random buttons. You have to hold the dispense button down the entire time you want it to dispense. Again, a great safety feature. If you are not wanting boiling water, the machine has several temperature options. If you press the keep warm/setting button, it allows you to select what temperature you would like it to warm to and then hold. It can hold a temperature for up to 48 hours.Overall, it's an amazing product and I'd highly recommend it.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 14, 2025

title: Plastic water taste and hot water definitely touching the plastic
body: It looks nice and boils the water pretty quick, this is why two stars.Even after boiling for a couple of time and changing water, it still tastes like plastic. Even though it has â€œmetal spoutâ€ it is there just to barely cover plastic, in reality, the water travels through the plastic pathways, and possibly motor, which defeats the purpose of stainless steel, and â€œno hot water touching plasticâ€. If youâ€™re looking for a healthy way to keep your water warm and not to have plastics in your water - this is sadly not the option.Definitely a return.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 13, 2023
author: Stephanie
title: Need better directions
body: I like this water dispenser, and yet I didnâ€™t start out liking it.First off, it just says open the lid. It didnâ€™t say how to open the lid. I didnâ€™t know if it opened from the back or from the front. After messing with it for about five minutes, and even had my husband take a look, I figured out that you have to press the vent cover and then lift up and the lid will open. I got very frustrated with this. Just post a picture or write how to open it.Next, I was wondering if the lid is detachable for cleaning. I guess itâ€™s not. No instructions about that either.When I boiled the water, I had to keep hitting the unlock button and it was very finicky and didnâ€™t want to disperse the water. I figured it out.Glad it stays locked. Thatâ€™s a plus.It takes about 20 minutes for the water to heat up. Might be quicker to just use my teapot. However, I like the fact that the water after bringing to a boil will remain hot, so no more boiling water on the stove.It holds a good amount of water, so thatâ€™s one more plus for this unit.This is a very large appliance so be sure you have enough room on your counter if you want to use it daily.After all of the hassle, Iâ€™m enjoying my new hot water dispenser.Not sure if I would recommend this particular model. I wish the lid was detachable so it would be easier to clean.

date: Reviewed in the United States on November 27, 2023
author: green_earth
title: Large reservoir, lots of possibilities
body: My summary: this product is beautifully put together and a very attractive addition to any kitchen, produces hot water in abundance, seems to be super easy to clean or keep clean, and as you consider your individual needs this may be a great value for your kitchen or serving area.We have been using this now for several weeks. It gets used for hot drinks, instant breakfast cereals and so forth.Learning to use it as a bin a little bit of a challenge and the process of dispensing and understanding what's happening is a little problematic only in that it's not as straightforward as you might expect. My youngest child is 12 years old, and everybody has gotten used to how it works without complaint - but it did take a fair bit of learning to get everybody up to speed on what to expect. To clarify that point, as it sits it will lock and it's not the type of thing you simply walk up and push the dispense button and expect it to work. You have to push and hold the unlock button first then push dispense and there's usually about a one to two second delay before water comes. This delay ended up in hot water being spilled on our floor, countertop, and table multiple times as people thought something was wrong. Everybody's accustomed to it now and knows what to do - but just a comment to assist those who first begin to use it. Also, if you said it to keep the hot water at a certain temperature it will assume you are in charge of how long it will maintain that temperature. It will stay on for as many days or hours as you leave it on. There is no timer function that I can find. In fact, that's the one thing I wish they would have included is a maximum time for keeping the water warm because it's easy to forget.

author: Tan
title: Great and trustworthy product! I recommend!
body: Why did you pick this product vs others?:Great product! Love it! Great household item to have for the wife! If you drink lots of teas, hot lemon water, instant coffee, or anything else, itâ€™s a great item to have on hand for multiple purposes. Stainless steel insulation instead of plastic and boils/reheats quickly. I also like the automatic lock function for child safety reasons. It also makes sounds to signal the boiling is done and to go press the warm function or that itâ€™s going to turn itself off. One thing to note is after some time of no use while itâ€™s on (~3-5 days), it will automatically turn itself off (auto-shutoff). My assumption is itâ€™s for fire safety reasons if you forget to turn off. It will not unfortunately detect the low water level and turn it off, only after some time as mentioned before.Functionality:Large capacity for holding water, safety, 6 different temperature settings plus boil function, and fits anywhere with a clean and sleek look.Ease of use:Child lock buttons. Read the manual to help learn how to use. Fairly simple. No complaints.Â Size:5L

author: Purchased-reviews
title: Fantastic water boiler from brand,Cuckoo.
body: Reading negative reviews from those who got this product for free is mindboggling. Wish I coupd have gotten this product for free for my review. 1.I liked the stainless pot that could be cleaned. 2. Cuckoo is a trusted and dependable manufacturer of Korea with factory in China as well as in Korea. 3. I like that I could have 5Liters of hot water available. 4. Durability makes this purchase a great investment for years. Great to use at college dorm.

date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2023
author: George butler
title: Works well
body: Takes up a bit of counter top room , so I plan to suspend in a shelf soon , but the instant hot water is what I have been needing for my teas.

date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2024
author: Anonymous
title: Overall decent but the design has some flaws
body: Pros: Easy to set up and use. Dries quicker than using a conventional dryer. Build quality appears to be decent (but we won't know for sure). Barely fits our 10 lb Maltese. Much much quieter.Cons: The front door is cheap and easily scratchable (it will be scratched) and our dog does not like to sit inside the container because the air directly targets the face. This is a massive design flaw and the holes near the facial area need to be sealed off. Price is super steep.

date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2024
author: EDM
title: Amazing pet dry air shower
body: I was nervous that my pet would not get in but the training guide was so helpful and worked. My dog us always clean, my house and furniture is clean the house smells better. My dog loves it. Also much larger than what I expected . Great buy easy to use and clean

date: Reviewed in the United States on September 14, 2024
author: JCN
title: Love!
body: I love this product. I was worried it would be too small for my 12/13-pound dog, but the size is fine. My dog has environmental allergies, so I like the air shower function for removing allergens. I feel much less worried after our walks now.
